# Formats of TradingView Widgets — TradingView

![](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widget-formats/)

[Widget formats](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widget-formats/#widget-formats)

=========================================================================================

TradingView widgets are available in two variations: **Web Component** and **iframe-based** widgets. Both formats display the exact same high-quality market data, charts, and designs. However, they differ in how they are embedded, customized, and loaded inside your website code.

[Comparison](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widget-formats/#comparison)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Feature | Web Component (Recommended) | Iframe |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Best for | Modern sites embedding multiple widgets with shared styling and performance. | Maximum isolation or legacy sites expecting iframe behavior. |
| Implementation | Custom element tag + ES module. | Script that injects an iframe. |
| Page isolation | Runs within host DOM. Scoped and encapsulated via Shadow DOM. | Fully isolated document boundary. |
| Styling | Supports custom CSS tokens, dark/light modes, and selectors for granular control. | Styling is limited to config options; no host-page CSS targeting inside an iframe. |
| Data connection | Can share a single connection across widgets. | Each iframe manages its own connection. |
| Customization | Dynamic updates via attributes; UI updates instantly. | Changes require re-initializing the widget. |
| Integration in SPA/CSR | Friendly to frameworks and hydration; works as native custom elements. | Works anywhere but reloading iframes may be heavy. |
| Bundle and loading | Multiple widgets share code and a single loader, reducing incremental cost. | Each widget loads its own document and assets. |
| Security boundary | Encapsulated but within page process. | Strong isolation via iframe boundary. |

[Why choose Web Component widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widget-formats/#why-choose-web-component-widgets)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Smaller downloads, faster loads.** Multiple widgets use a shared loader, reducing total download size and parsing time.
*   **Rich customization.** You can control sizing layout directly via CSS, change light/dark themes, and use CSS tokens to tweak colors to match your brand perfectly.
*   **Efficiency at scale.** Adding extra widgets has a minimal performance cost. Where applicable, Web Components can share a single data connection, preventing browser resource exhaustion.
*   **Native framework support.** Works natively as a standard HTML element, making integration with any framework or vanilla JS smoother and cleaner.

[Why choose iframe widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widget-formats/#why-choose-iframe-widgets)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **Platform restrictions.** You have a CMS or a legacy environment that blocks custom HTML tags or restricts loading external ES modules.
*   **Security sandboxing.** Your project requires strict content security policies where the widget must be completely isolated from the host page’s code and DOM context.

[How to identify the widget type](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widget-formats/#how-to-identify-the-widget-type)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can confirm a widget’s type in three ways:

*   **The documentation label.** When browsing our [widget collection](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets)
    , every page includes a distinct label near the title:
    
    *   `type: Web Component`
    *   `type: iframe`
*   **The embed snippet.** The code generated for your website is the clearest indicator.
    
    *   Web Component widgets rely on an ES module import and a custom HTML tag starting with `tv-`.
        
            <script type="module" src="https://.../tv-widget.js"></script><tv-mini-chart    symbol="NASDAQ:AAPL"    theme="light"></tv-mini-chart>
        
    *   Iframe widgets rely on a standard script file that injects an `<iframe>`.
        
            <script    src="https://s3.tradingview.com/external-embedding/embed-widget-ticker-tape.js">    {        "symbols": [{"proName": "NASDAQ:AAPL"}],        "theme": "light"    }</script>
        
*   **Runtime inspection.** If you inspect a running widget using your browser’s Developer Tools:
    
    *   Web Components appear as custom elements (e.g., `<tv-mini-ticker-chart>`) and usually attach a Shadow Root.
    *   Iframe widgets render an `<iframe>` element as the outermost container.

[Migration notes](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widget-formats/#migration-notes)

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If you are transitioning from iframes to Web Components, or using both simultaneously, keep the following in mind:

*   You can run both types on the same page. Web Components won’t interfere with iframe widgets and vice versa.
*   Both formats connect to the same TradingView backend and display the same market data. However, new features and performance optimizations may be released for Web Components first as our modern standard.
*   While the underlying widget options are similar, the syntax differs. Always consult the specific widget’s documentation for the correct syntax.

# Financial Widgets Collection — TradingView

![](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/)

TradingView Widgets
===================

Welcome to the TradingView Widgets collection, where financial magic meets the simplicity of copy-paste. These powerful tools will supercharge your website with real-time financial data and stunning charts. Say goodbye to dull numbers and hello to dynamic visualizations that'll make your visitors go "Wow!"

Ready to explore?

Just click on a category below to get started.

#### Charts

Integrate anywhere and everywhere.

[See widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/charts)

#### Watchlists

See everything at a glance.

[See widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/watchlists)

#### Tickers

Built for when you need your chosen symbols to be the big ticket item.

[See widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/tickers)

#### Heatmaps

Light up your site with these colorful macro-view embeds.

[See widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/heatmaps)

#### Screeners

Handy for sorting symbols both by fundamental and technical indicators.

[See widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/screeners)

#### Symbol Details

Get a specific view of the symbol you care about the most.

[See widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/symbol-details)

#### News

Showcase top news, without having to write it yourself.

[See widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/news)

#### Calendars

Display important economic events throughout our world, directly on your site.

[See widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/calendars)

#### Economics

Visualize the global economy with key macro data.

[See widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/economics)

# Your Starting Point For Financial Widgets — TradingView

![](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/getting-started/)

[Getting started](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/getting-started/#getting-started)

============================================================================================

Welcome to our TradingView Widgets documentation.

This documentation will guide you on integrating our financial widgets into your website. You will learn how to customize the widgets, implement and use them effectively for top-notch financial visualization.

[What are widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/getting-started/#what-are-widgets)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**TradingView Widgets** are ready-to-use financial tools you can embed into your website to display market data.

Widgets are perfect for finance blogs, trading platforms, portfolio trackers, or any website that wants to share financial insights with users. With just a few lines of code, you can display interactive charts, live tickers, economic calendars, watchlists, and more.

[How widgets work](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/getting-started/#how-widgets-work)

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Each widget is available as a lightweight HTML snippet or a React component, so you can easily integrate it into any website or app. Widgets come with built-in data provided by TradingView, so there’s no need to connect your own data sources or APIs. All you do is just:

1.  Pick a widget.
2.  Customize it in the constructor.
3.  Copy and paste the auto-generated embed code into your website.

That’s it — the widget appears instantly!

Also, each widget is fully responsive and designed with [accessibility](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/accessibility)
 in mind, making it easy to deliver a seamless experience across devices and assistive technologies.

[Widget formats](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/getting-started/#widget-formats)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

TradingView widgets are available in two variations: **iframe** and **Web Component**. The difference is purely technical. Both formats display the exact same high-quality market data, charts, and designs. However, they differ in how they are embedded, customized, and loaded inside your website code.

*   **Web Component:** This is a newer, more modern technology. You can identify these widgets by the embed code, which uses tags that start with `tv-` (for example, `<tv-mini-ticker-chart>`).
*   **Iframe:** This is the classic embedding method used across the web. The embed code for these will rely on standard `<script>` tags.

When you browse the [widgets collection](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets)
, each page will clearly indicate which format is being used so you know what to expect when you copy the code. If you want to dive deeper into the technical differences, read a [Widget formats guide](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widget-formats)
.

[Where to start](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/getting-started/#where-to-start)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

[Follow step-by-step tutorials](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/tutorials/)

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

New to TradingView Widgets? Start here. Whether you choose the classic iframe or the new Web Component format, we have hands-on guides for you. Visit our tutorials section to learn how to customize and embed widgets to build a complete financial web page.

[Explore available widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Access the directory of all our widgets, customize them, and generate the embed code for your website. Here, you can also find some example configurations of the widgets for efficient usage.

[Explore available markets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/markets)

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------

Browse the full list of all available markets and their data level provided for our widgets. This can range from real-time data, delayed data, to end of day data.

[See widgets in action](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/solutions)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------

Get inspired by live examples of mock websites that show how different widgets work together, creating holistic financial data dashboard sites. You can explore examples like Stocks, Forex, and Crypto to have a better understanding.

[Find quick answers](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/faq/general)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------

Got questions? We’ve gathered answers to the most common ones — from troubleshooting widget issues to understanding customization options.

[More from TradingView](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/getting-started/#more-from-tradingview)

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

We’re not just about widgets. TradingView offers a suite of [other products](https://www.tradingview.com/free-charting-libraries/)
 to fuel your financial ambitions.

*   Explore [Lightweight Charts™](https://www.tradingview.com/lightweight-charts/)
    , our open-source, nimble charting solution designed for developers.
*   If you’re looking for a comprehensive charting solution with all the bells and whistles, our [Charting Libraries](https://www.tradingview.com/advanced-charts/)
     have got you covered.

Just remember, with these products, you’ll need to provide your own data. Widgets, on the other hand, come with built-in data provided by TradingView.

[Next\
\
#### Widgets](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets)

---

# Chart Widgets. Explore, Set Up, Embed — TradingView

![](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/charts/)

Chart Widgets
=============

Advanced Real-Time Chart
------------------------

Here's a free and powerful chart for you to easily embed in any website. Make it yours with a ton of different customization options.

[Get widget](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/charts/advanced-chart/)

Symbol Overview
---------------

Embed the latest quotes, plus a simple chart of a simple, single stock. A great in-depth option for any web or mobile-geared landing pages.

[Get widget](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/charts/symbol-overview/)

Mini Chart new
--------------

This little one lets you see a symbol's price and change percentages, alongside an even more stripped-back chart view.

[Get widget](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/charts/mini-chart/)

Market Summary new
------------------

Why settle for just numbers? This watchlist visualizes multiple symbols at once. Create custom tabs for specific assets or let the widget automatically display the day's hottest and coldest movers.

[Get widget](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/watchlists/market-summary/)

Stock Market
------------

See the top five gaining, losing, and most active stocks for the day. It updates based on current market activity – so you'll always see the most relevant stocks.

[Get widget](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/watchlists/stock-market/)

Market Overview
---------------

Built for when you need to take a macro look at the markets. This widget works particularly well on homepages.

[Get widget](https://www.tradingview.com/widget-docs/widgets/watchlists/market-overview/)

About S&P 500 E-mini Futures
----------------------------﻿

S&P 500 — short for Standard and Poor's 500, is a United States stock market index based on the market capitalizations of the top 500 companies that have common stock listed on the New York Stock Exchange or NASDAQ. With its headquarters in New York City and dating back to 1860, Standard and Poor's has offices in 23 countries and maintains the SP 500 index. Over $1.25 trillion USD is indexed to Standard and Poor's portfolio of indices, which includes the S&P 500. The S&P 500 index seeks to reflect the status of the whole stock market by tracking the return and volatility of the 500 most commonly held large cap company stocks on the NYSE, capturing approximately 80% of total market capitalization. The 500 companies comprising the SP500 span all major industries and are selected based on their market capitalization, liquidity, ability to be traded, as well as other factors. Additionally, the importance of the SP500 is highlighted by its use as an economic indicator of the health of the United States economy.

Frequently Asked Questions
--------------------------﻿

What is the price of S&P 500 E-mini Futures?

The current price of S&P 500 E-mini Futures is 6,907.50 USD — it has risen 0.03% in the past 24 hours. Watch S&P 500 E-mini Futures price in more detail on the [chart](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=CME_MINI:ES1!)
.

What is S&P 500 E-mini Futures volume?

The volume of S&P 500 E-mini Futures is ‪1.69 M‬. Track more important stats on the [S&P 500 E-mini Futures chart](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=CME_MINI:ES1!)
.

What is Open interest? How can I use it to trade S&P 500 E-mini Futures?

[Open interest](https://www.tradingview.com/?solution=43000685269)
 is the number of contracts held by traders in active positions — they're not closed or expired. For S&P 500 E-mini Futures this number is ‪1.91 M‬. You can use it to track a prevailing market trend and adjust your own strategy: declining open interest for S&P 500 E-mini Futures shows that traders are closing their positions, which means a weakening trend.

Is it time to buy or sell S&P 500 E-mini Futures?

Buying or selling futures contracts depends on many factors: season, underlying commodity, your own trading strategy. So mostly it's up to you, but if you look for some certain calculations to take into account, you can study technical analysis for S&P 500 E-mini Futures. Today its technical rating is sell, but remember that market conditions change all the time, so it's always crucial to do your own research. See more of [S&P 500 E-mini Futures technicals](https://www.tradingview.com/symbols/CME_MINI-ES1!/technicals/)
 for a more comprehensive analysis.

### Nominal[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker/#nominal "Direct link to Nominal")

Ƭ **Nominal**<`T`, `Name`\>: `T` & { `[species]`: `Name` }

This is the generic type useful for declaring a nominal type, which does not structurally matches with the base type and the other types declared over the same base type

Usage:

**`Example`**

    type Index = Nominal<number, 'Index'>;// let i: Index = 42; // this fails to compilelet i: Index = 42 as Index; // OK

**`Example`**

    type TagName = Nominal<string, 'TagName'>;

#### Type parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker/#type-parameters-1 "Direct link to Type parameters")

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `T` | `T` |
| `Name` | extends `string` |

* * *

### TradableSolutions[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker/#tradablesolutions "Direct link to TradableSolutions")

Ƭ **TradableSolutions**: [`TradableSolutionsTypes`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradableSolutionsTypes)
\[keyof [`TradableSolutionsTypes`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TradableSolutionsTypes)\
\]

`TradableSolutions` has one of the following keys:

*   `changeAccount` - id of a sub-account suitable for trading the symbol
*   `changeSymbol` - the symbol suitable for trading with current sub-account
*   `openUrl` - the object with URL to be opened and text for solution button

# Louisiana-Pacific Corp CEO Sells Over $2.4 Million in Company Stock — TradingView News

 

[](https://www.tradingview.com/)

Search

EN 

[Get started](https://www.tradingview.com/pricing/?source=header_go_pro_button&feature=start_free_trial)

![TradingView](https://s3.tradingview.com/news/logo/tradingview--theme-light.svg)![TradingView](https://s3.tradingview.com/news/logo/tradingview--theme-dark.svg)

Louisiana-Pacific Corp CEO Sells Over $2.4 Million in Company Stock
===================================================================

Less than 1 min read

[![](https://s3-symbol-logo.tradingview.com/louisiana-pacific.svg)LPX](https://www.tradingview.com/symbols/NYSE-LPX/)

William Bradley Southern, CEO and Director of Louisiana-Pacific Corp, sold 28,332 shares of common stock through multiple transactions on February 2 and 3, 2026, under a Rule 10b5-1 trading plan. The sales were executed at weighted average prices ranging from $85.16 to $91.46, resulting in a total sale amount of $2,477,206. Following these transactions, Southern directly owns 460,883 shares of the company.

|     |     |
| --- | --- |
| Reporter Name | Southern William Bradley |
| Relationship | Chief Executive Officer |
| Type | Sell |
| Amount | $2,477,206 |
| SEC Filing | [Form 4](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1549626/000119312526037007/xslF345X05/ownership.xml) |

SEC Filing: [LOUISIANA-PACIFIC CORP \[ LPX \] - Form 4 - Feb. 04, 2026](https://www.sec.gov/Archives/edgar/data/1549626/000119312526037007/xslF345X05/ownership.xml)

This is an AI-powered summary. It may contain inaccuracies. Consider verifying important information with the source. Please note this summary is solely based on documents filed with the SEC.

# Teledyne Technologies Vice Chairman Jason Vanwees Sells 7,500 Shares — TradingView News

 

[](https://www.tradingview.com/)

Search

EN 

[Get started](https://www.tradingview.com/pricing/?source=header_go_pro_button&feature=start_free_trial)

![TradingView](https://s3.tradingview.com/news/logo/tradingview--theme-light.svg)![TradingView](https://s3.tradingview.com/news/logo/tradingview--theme-dark.svg)

Teledyne Technologies Vice Chairman Jason Vanwees Sells 7,500 Shares
====================================================================

Less than 1 min read

[![](https://s3-symbol-logo.tradingview.com/teledyne-technologies.svg)TDY](https://www.tradingview.com/symbols/NYSE-TDY/)

Jason Vanwees, Vice Chairman of Teledyne Technologies, sold 7,500 shares of common stock on February 3, 2026, at weighted average prices ranging from $630.03 to $631.715, totaling $4,730,783. Post-transaction, Vanwees directly owns 51,024 shares and indirectly owns 2,643 shares through his spouse. The sales were executed in multiple trades, with prices reflecting the weighted average.

| Relationship | Vice Chairman |

# Interface: IDatafeedQuotesApi | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Datafeed)
.IDatafeedQuotesApi

Quotes datafeed API

Methods[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#methods "Direct link to Methods")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### getQuotes[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#getquotes "Direct link to getQuotes")

▸ **getQuotes**(`symbols`, `onDataCallback`, `onErrorCallback`): `void`

This function is called when the library needs quote data. The library assumes that `onDataCallback` is called once when all the requested data is received.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbols` | `string`\[\] | symbol names. |
| `onDataCallback` | [`QuotesCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Datafeed#quotescallback) | callback to return the requested data. |
| `onErrorCallback` | [`QuotesErrorCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Datafeed#quoteserrorcallback) | callback for responding with an error. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### subscribeQuotes[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#subscribequotes "Direct link to subscribeQuotes")

▸ **subscribeQuotes**(`symbols`, `fastSymbols`, `onRealtimeCallback`, `listenerGUID`): `void`

Trading Platform calls this function when it wants to receive real-time quotes for a symbol. The library assumes that you will call `onRealtimeCallback` every time you want to update the quotes.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbols` | `string`\[\] | list of symbols that should be updated rarely (once per minute). These symbols are included in the watchlist but they are not visible at the moment. |
| `fastSymbols` | `string`\[\] | list of symbols that should be updated frequently (at least once every 10 seconds) |
| `onRealtimeCallback` | [`QuotesCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Datafeed#quotescallback) | callback to send realtime quote data updates |
| `listenerGUID` | `string` | unique identifier of the listener |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### unsubscribeQuotes[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#unsubscribequotes "Direct link to unsubscribeQuotes")

▸ **unsubscribeQuotes**(`listenerGUID`): `void`

Trading Platform calls this function when it doesn't want to receive updates for this listener anymore. `listenerGUID` will be the same object that the Library passed to `subscribeQuotes` before.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `listenerGUID` | `string` | unique identifier of the listener |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

*   [Methods](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#methods)
    *   [getQuotes](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#getquotes)
        
    *   [subscribeQuotes](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#subscribequotes)
        
    *   [unsubscribeQuotes](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Datafeed.IDatafeedQuotesApi/#unsubscribequotes)

---

# The Leap ที่จัดขึ้นร่วมกับ TradeStation สิ้นสุดลงแล้ว — TradingView Blog

Main content

[การซื้อขายและโบรคเกอร์](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/th/category/trading/)

The Leap ที่จัดขึ้นร่วมกับ TradeStation สิ้นสุดลงแล้ว
=====================================================

Dec 15, 2025

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/12/Paper-trading-competition-Results-TradeStation.jpg)

ขณะที่ทุกคนกำลังก้าวเข้าสู่ปีใหม่ ขอให้เราใช้เวลาสักครู่เพื่อชื่นชมผู้ชนะและผู้เข้าร่วมแข่งขัน The Leap ครั้งล่าสุด ซึ่งจัดร่วมกับ TradeStation

มีการซื้อขาย 3,621,088 ครั้ง ผู้เข้าร่วมแข่งขัน 82,042 คน เงินรางวัลมูลค่า 50,000 ดอลลาร์สหรัฐ การแข่งขันการเทรดนานสี่สัปดาห์ และเป็นหนึ่งในการแข่งขันการเทรดจำลองระดับโลกที่ปราศจากความเสี่ยง ในช่วงเดือนที่ผ่านมา ผู้เข้าร่วมแข่งขันได้ทดสอบทักษะของตนเองด้วยการเทรดฟิวเจอร์สบนดัชนี S&P 500 ทองคำ รวมทั้ง Bitcoin ด้วยความผันผวนของตลาดอนุพันธ์และความผันผวนของตลาดที่ต้องการความแม่นยำและกลยุทธ์ การแข่งขันจึงผลักดันให้แม้แต่เทรดเดอร์ผู้มีประสบการณ์มากที่สุดต้องฝ่าฟันขีดจำกัดของตนเอง

เราขอขอบคุณทุกท่านที่เข้าร่วม The Leap และทำให้การแข่งขันครั้งนี้เป็นปรากฏการณ์ ตอนนี้มาร่วมแสดงความยินดีกับเทรดเดอร์ที่ก้าวขึ้นมายังจุดสูงสุดและคว้าตำแหน่งบน [กระดานผู้นำ](https://th.tradingview.com/the-leap/november-2025-tradestation/)
:

อันดับที่ 1 รางวัล $8,500 — [apsudnay](https://th.tradingview.com/u/apsudnay/)
  
อันดับที่ 2 รางวัล $7,000 — [AlePiras](https://th.tradingview.com/u/AlePiras/)
  
อันดับที่ 3 รางวัล $6,000 — [Sualokcin](https://th.tradingview.com/u/Sualokcin/)
  
อันดับที่ 4 รางวัล $5,000 — [GHOST\_FX\_GANG](https://th.tradingview.com/u/GHOST_FX_GANG/)
  
อันดับที่ 5 รางวัล $3,500 — [puntopaos](https://th.tradingview.com/u/puntopaos/)

ยังมีเทรดเดอร์อีก 250 รายที่ได้รับรางวัลเงินสดและได้รับการขยายเวลาการสมัครสมาชิก แต่นั่นยังไม่ใช่ทั้งหมด! เรายังมีรางวัลพิเศษอีกสองประเภทด้วย:

การซื้อขายที่ทำกำไรได้มากที่สุด สำหรับผู้ที่ทำกำไรได้มากที่สุดระหว่างการแข่งขัน รางวัลนี้มอบให้กับ:

[ION-TRADER](https://th.tradingview.com/u/ION-TRADER/)
 (การเทรดที่ทำกำไรได้มากที่สุด — $34,473)  
[kairdjuw](https://th.tradingview.com/u/kairdjuw/)
 ($34,098)  
[nadam2001](https://th.tradingview.com/u/nadam2001/)
 ($33,358)  
[Yassine1990](https://th.tradingview.com/u/Yassine1990/)
 ($33,188)  
[jstrading\_8442](https://th.tradingview.com/u/jstrading_8442/)
 ($32,488)

รางวัลผู้ที่ไม่เสียเลย — เน้นย้ำถึงเทรดเดอร์ที่เทรดตลอดการแข่งขันทั้งหมดโดยไม่มีออเดอร์ที่ไม่ทำกำไรแม้แต่ออเดอร์เดียว ผู้ที่ได้รางวัลคือ:

[speculation](https://th.tradingview.com/u/speculation/)
 (ผู้ที่ไม่เสียเลย — $69,433.10)  
[saviour32](https://th.tradingview.com/u/saviour32/)
 ($67,731.80)  
[Hunter1601](https://th.tradingview.com/u/Hunter1601/)
 ($65,391.00)  
[benditopablo](https://th.tradingview.com/u/benditopablo/)
 ($63,531.45)  
[MacDirtee](https://th.tradingview.com/u/MacDirtee/)
 ($58,951.35)

เราได้ก้าวไปอีกขั้นและรวบรวมสถิติสำคัญของการแข่งขันมาให้แล้ว ตรวจสอบสินทรัพย์ที่มีการซื้อขายมากที่สุด profit-to-loss ratio และตัวชี้วัดสำคัญอื่นๆ สแกนตัวเลข มองหารูปแบบ และเตรียมพร้อมสำหรับครั้งถัดไป

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/12/The-leap-results-infographics-Obshhaya-1.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/12/The-leap-results-infographics-Obshhaya-1.png)

เราขอขอบคุณทุกท่านสำหรับเวลา ความพยายาม และความคิดเห็นในครั้งนี้ สำหรับทั้งผู้ชนะการแข่งขันและผู้เข้าร่วมแข่งขัน เราหวังว่า The Leap จะเป็นแหล่งฝึกฝนและแรงบันดาลใจที่ยอดเยี่ยมในการพิชิตตลาด เตรียมตัวให้พร้อมสำหรับ — The Leap ครั้งใหม่กำลังจะมาถึง!

* * *

ผู้ชนะจะได้รับการติดต่อทางอีเมลเพื่อรับรางวัล โปรดตรวจสอบกล่องจดหมายของคุณ!

ค้นหาคำถามที่พบบ่อยเกี่ยวกับ The Leap ใน [แหล่งความรู้ของเรา](https://th.tradingview.com/the-leap/?folder=43000593579)
 และเตรียมพร้อมสำหรับครั้งถัดไป!

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView สร้างสรรมาเพื่อคุณ ดังนั้นคุณไม่ควรพลาดประโยชน์จากฟีเจอร์อันเยี่ยมยอดของเรา

# Siap untuk bergabung dengan The Leap? Ikuti tantangan trading kontrak berjangka kami di bulan Februari! — TradingView Blog

Main content

[TRADING DAN BROKER](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/id/category/trading/)

Siap untuk bergabung dengan The Leap? Ikuti tantangan trading kontrak berjangka kami di bulan Februari!
=======================================================================================================

Jan 27

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/the-leap-eurex.jpg)

Februari adalah saat di mana rencana bertemu dengan eksekusi. Dengan semangat itu, [The Leap](https://id.tradingview.com/the-leap/?source=blog)
 kembali dengan kompetisi baru yang dibangun di sekitar trading kontrak berjangka. Edisi kompetisi trading kertas bebas risiko ini disponsori oleh Eurex dan berlangsung dari tanggal 2 hingga 27 Februari, memberi para trader waktu kurang dari satu bulan untuk melakukan trading, mengadaptasi strategi mereka, dan mendaki papan peringkat.

Seperti biasa, The Leap tetap setia pada format intinya dengan tantangan trading kertas bebas risiko selama satu bulan di mana performa diukur hanya dengan satu hal — keuntungan yang Anda hasilkan pada akhir kompetisi. Kali ini, total hadiah senilai $20.000 siap diperebutkan bersama dengan perpanjangan langganan dan paket data gratis.

Disponsori oleh Eurex, edisi kali ini menempatkan trading kontrak berjangka sebagai fokus utama. Peserta akan melakukan trading dalam kondisi pasar yang realistis, di mana leverage, volatilitas, dan ketepatan waktu akan menjadi kunci keberhasilan. Dengan struktur pasar kelas bursa dan dinamika dunia nyata, kompetisi ini memberi penghargaan kepada para trader yang dapat merencanakan strategi ke depan, mengelola risiko, dan mengeksekusi trade dengan disiplin.

Saat pemenang diumumkan, hadiah yang akan dibagikan adalah sebagai berikut:

*   Juara 1: $3.000
*   Juara 2: $2.500
*   Juara 3: $2.000
*   Juara 4: $1.500
*   Juara 5: $1.000
*   Juara 6–25: $250
*   Juara 26–50: $200
*   Juara 51 hingga 250 — 6 bulan tambahan dari paket langganan Anda saat ini

Selain itu, 250 pemenang teratas akan menerima data Eurex real-time selama 6 bulan.

Edisi The Leap ini diselenggarakan berkat kerja sama dengan Eurex, salah satu bursa derivatif terkemuka di dunia dan bagian dari Deutsche Börse Group. Didirikan pada tahun 1998, Eurex menawarkan berbagai macam produk kontrak berjangka dan option, didukung oleh trading elektronik sepenuhnya, likuiditas yang tinggi, dan kliring terintegrasi.

Siap untuk mengikuti The Leap? Berikut yang perlu Anda ketahui.

*   Masuk ke akun TradingView berbayar Anda
*   [Mendaftar ke kompetisi](https://id.tradingview.com/the-leap/february-2026-eurex/)
    
*   Mulai lakukan trading kertas setelah kompetisi dimulai

Kompetisi dimulai pada 2 Februari, tetapi sebelum Anda mulai, pastikan untuk memeriksa tautan-tautan bermanfaat ini.

*   Pelajari lebih lanjut tentang [The Leap](https://id.tradingview.com/the-leap/?folder=43000593579)
     di Pusat Bantuan kami
*   Baca [Syarat dan Ketentuan](https://id.tradingview.com/the-leap/february-2026-eurex/rules/)
     kami
*   [Pelajari cara melakukan trading kertas](https://id.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000516466/)
     agar Anda siap saat kompetisi dimulai

Terima kasih telah membaca — kami berharap dapat melihat kemampuan trading Anda dalam kompetisi ini.

[Bergabung ke kompetisi](https://id.tradingview.com/the-leap/february-2026-eurex/?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=internal&utm_campaign=The_Leap.)

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView dibangun untuk anda, karenanya manfaatkan fitur-fitur luar biasa kami semaksimal mungkin

[Luncurkan Superchart](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

---

# Explorez le marché des crypto avec les indices CoinDesk — TradingView Blog

Main content

[FLUX DE DONNéES & BOURSES](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/fr/category/data-feeds-exchanges/)

Explorez le marché des crypto avec les indices CoinDesk
=======================================================

Jan 8

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/coindesk-indices.jpg)

Pour avoir une vision claire des marchés, il faut souvent les observer sous plusieurs angles. Les graphiques des cours racontent une histoire, les actifs individuels en racontent une autre, mais les indices révèlent une vue d’ensemble. C’est pourquoi nous continuons à élargir la couverture de nos indices, et aujourd’hui, nous vous présentons notre dernière nouveauté: les indices de CoinDesk, qui vous permettent d’approfondir votre connaissance des marchés des cryptomonnaies.

CoinDesk est l’un des fournisseurs les plus reconnus d’actualités, de recherches et d’informations sur les marchés des cryptomonnaies. Parallèlement à sa présence dans les médias, CoinDesk développe une suite d’indices de qualité professionnelle conçus pour représenter différents segments du marché des cryptomonnaies. Ceux-ci sont largement utilisés par les gestionnaires d’actifs, les analystes et les émetteurs de produits pour évaluer les performances et comprendre la structure du marché.

Grâce à cette intégration, vous pouvez désormais accéder aux indices [CoinDesk 5](https://fr.tradingview.com/symbols/CD5/?exchange=COINDESK)
, [CoinDesk 20](https://fr.tradingview.com/symbols/CD20SPOT/?exchange=COINDESK)
, [CoinDesk 80](https://fr.tradingview.com/symbols/CD80/?exchange=COINDESK)
, [CoinDesk 100](https://fr.tradingview.com/symbols/CD100/?exchange=COINDESK)
, [CoinDesk Memecoin](https://fr.tradingview.com/symbols/CDMEME/?exchange=COINDESK)
, [CDOR Aave USDT](https://fr.tradingview.com/symbols/CDORAUSC/?exchange=COINDESK)
, [CDOR Aave USDC](https://fr.tradingview.com/symbols/CDORAUST/?exchange=COINDESK)
 et [CESR.](https://fr.tradingview.com/symbols/CESR/?exchange=COINDESK)

CoinDesk 20 se distingue comme un indice de référence essentiel, reflétant la performance des principaux actifs numériques et couvrant environ 80% du marché des actifs numériques, avec plus de 15 milliards de dollars de volume de produits liés. À l’autre extrémité du spectre, l’indice CoinDesk Memecoin suit cinquante des memecoins les plus liquides avec une pondération égale et un rééquilibrage régulier.

Pour accéder aux nouveaux indices, ouvrez la [recherche de symboles](https://fr.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000746682/)
 et tapez le préfixe COINDESK:

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/coindesk-indices-screenshot.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/coindesk-indices-screenshot.png)

Nous espérons que ces nouvelles données vous aideront à explorer plus efficacement les marchés mondiaux. N’oubliez pas que TradingView est connecté à des centaines de flux de données, vous donnant ainsi un accès direct à plus de deux millions d’instruments à travers le monde. Nous sommes donc le seul point d’entrée dont vous avez besoin pour tout ce qui concerne les marchés mondiaux.

[Explorer tous les marchés](https://fr.tradingview.com/data-coverage/?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=internal&utm_campaign=xtcom.)

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView est conçu pour vous, assurez-vous donc de profiter au maximum de nos fonctionnalités exceptionnelles

[Lancer Supercharts](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

# Legend | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v28**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

Legend is a list of series and indicators at the top-left corner of any chart. Note that the legend position cannot be changed.

![Legend](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/assets/images/legend-overview-589e075d0acfbee82e1827ff021a6366.gif)

Configure legend[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#configure-legend "Direct link to Configure legend")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

In the _Chart settings → Status line_ dialog, users can configure the legend.

![Chart settings](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/assets/images/legend-chart-settings-ba5635efa6a732d07c7112aac491d3f8.png)

### Symbol logos[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#symbol-logos "Direct link to Symbol logos")

If you want to display logos for symbols within the legend, follow the steps below:

1.  Enable the [`show_symbol_logos`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/Featuresets#show_symbol_logos)
     and [`show_symbol_logo_in_legend`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/Featuresets#show_symbol_logo_in_legend)
     featuresets. The _Logo_ option appears in the settings dialog.
2.  Provide URLs for symbols in the [`logo_urls`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#logo_urls)
     property of the [`LibrarySymbolInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
     object. Pass the object as a parameter to the callback of the [`resolveSymbol`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/datafeed-api/required-methods#resolvesymbol)
     method.

### Open market status[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#open-market-status "Direct link to Open market status")

By default, the library displays the current [market status](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Symbol-Status)
 as an icon within the legend for the main series of the chart. You can hide the market status by adding the [`display_market_status`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/Featuresets#display_market_status)
 featureset to the [`disabled_features`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions#disabled_features)
 array.

### Title[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#title "Direct link to Title")

In the _Title_ drop-down, users can select what title to display in the legend:

*   Description
*   Ticker
*   Ticker and description

You can also set the title using the [Overrides API](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/overrides/)
. For example, the code sample below sets ticker as the legend title.

    chartApi.applyOverrides({ "mainSeriesProperties.statusViewStyle.symbolTextSource": "ticker" })

### Last day change values[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#last-day-change-values "Direct link to Last day change values")

info

Last day change can be displayed in Trading Platform only as it requires quote data.

Last day change is a parameter represented by two values: daily change and percentage daily change. You should specify these values in the [`ch`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DatafeedQuoteValues#ch)
 and [`chp`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DatafeedQuoteValues#chp)
 properties of `DatafeedQuoteValues` and pass them to the library when it calls [`getQuotes`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/datafeed-api/trading-platform-methods#getquotes)
.

Last day change is displayed in _Data Window_ on the right toolbar. You can also add this parameter to the chart legend using the [overrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/overrides/)
:

    var widget = window.tvWidget = new TradingView.widget({    // ...    overrides: {        "paneProperties.legendProperties.showLastDayChange": true,    }});

Users can toggle the _Last day change values_ option in the _Chart settings_ dialog to change the last day change visibility in the legend. This option is hidden by default. To display it, you should enable one of the following featuresets:

*   [`legend_last_day_change`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/Featuresets#legend_last_day_change)
     — adds the extra _Last day change values_ option to the _Chart settings_ dialog.
*   [`show_last_price_and_change_only_in_series_legend`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/Featuresets#show_last_price_and_change_only_in_series_legend)
     — adds the last day change to the legend and hides the OHLC values. Also, the extra _Last day change values_ option appears in the _Chart settings_ dialog.

### Resolution[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#resolution "Direct link to Resolution")

To disable resolution changing in the Legend, use the [`legend_inplace_edit`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/Featuresets#legend_inplace_edit)
 featureset. If you want to completely remove the _Resolution_ button from the _Legend_, use the [`hide_resolution_in_legend`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/Featuresets#hide_resolution_in_legend)
 featureset.

![Remove resolution from Legend](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/assets/images/no-resolution-legend-d2ff263da43b9adaccb9f03d4c14f1a3.png)

Visibility customization[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#visibility-customization "Direct link to Visibility customization")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can use [featuresets](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/Featuresets)
 or [overrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/overrides/)
 to customize the legend's visibility such as hiding or displaying particular legend elements. The sections below describe customization via featuresets. For more information about override properties that affect the legend, refer to [Legend](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/overrides/chart-overrides#legend)
.

### Hide legend[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#hide-legend "Direct link to Hide legend")

You can hide the legend widget from the chart. To do this, include the `legend_widget` [featureset](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/Featuresets)
 in the [`disabled_features`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions#disabled_features)
 array.

![Disable legend](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/assets/images/legend-hide-widget-3dd01451f395dfc5103ec54c4af6d01e.png)

### Hide legend buttons[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#hide-legend-buttons "Direct link to Hide legend buttons")

You can hide all legend buttons by including the `edit_buttons_in_legend` in [`disabled_features`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions#disabled_features)
. Besides, disabling `show_hide_button_in_legend`, `format_button_in_legend`, or `delete_button_in_legend` allows customizing particular legend buttons. For example, the _Settings_ button is hidden in the image below.

![Hide settings button](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/assets/images/legend-hide-settings-button-ea0b509607eabb58e03b821aa1f09a22.png)

### Hide context menu[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#hide-context-menu "Direct link to Hide context menu")

When right-clicking the legend, you can access the context menu. To disable this feature, include `legend_context_menu` in [`disabled_features`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions#disabled_features)
.

![Hide context menu](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/assets/images/legend-context-menu-c540f1b35be76b429f05e5fcf6314aa4.png)

Price formatting[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#price-formatting "Direct link to Price formatting")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Prices are formatted according to the `pricescale`, `minmov`, `minmove2`, `fractional`, and `variable_tick_size` properties specified in the [`LibrarySymbolInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
 object. For more information, refer to the [Price format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format)
 section.

You can also apply custom formatting using the [`numeric_formatting`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions#numeric_formatting)
 property of [Widget Constructor](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/core_concepts/Widget-Constructor)
.

    new TradingView.widget({    container: "chartContainer",    locale: "en",    library_path: "charting_library/",    datafeed: new Datafeeds.UDFCompatibleDatafeed("https://demo-feed-data.tradingview.com"),    symbol: "AAPL",    interval: "1D",    numeric_formatting: { decimal_sign: "," },})

In the code sample above, the comma separates the decimal/fractional part of the price.

![Numeric formatting](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/assets/images/legend-numeric-formatting-89279aeecf4432504fdc3f71267a581f.png)

NaN values in legend[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#nan-values-in-legend "Direct link to NaN values in legend")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

If `NaN` values appear in the legend instead of the expected data, ensure that you have implemented the [`getQuotes`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/datafeed-api/trading-platform-methods#getquotes)
 and [`subscribeQuotes`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/datafeed-api/trading-platform-methods#subscribequotes)
 methods of the [Datafeed API](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/datafeed-api/)
. This issue appears in [mobile applications](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/mobile_specifics/)
 when running outside of tracking mode. Outside this mode, the legend shows only three values: the closing price, price change, and price change percentage. The library retrieves these values from the [`getQuotes`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/datafeed-api/trading-platform-methods#getquotes)
 request, otherwise, it displays `NaN`.

Note that open, high, and low prices and indicator values are displayed in the tracking mode only. To activate this mode, users should long tap on the chart. A single tap on the chart exits this mode.

Display delayed data information[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#display-delayed-data-information "Direct link to Display delayed data information")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Follow the steps below to enable the _Data is delayed_ section in the legend:

1.  Add the [`display_data_mode`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/customization/Featuresets#display_data_mode)
     featureset to the [`enabled_features`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions#enabled_features)
     array of the [Widget Constructor](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/core_concepts/Widget-Constructor)
     object.
2.  Provide the following properties in the [`LibrarySymbolInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
     object. Pass this object as a parameter to the callback of the [`resolveSymbol`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/datafeed-api/required-methods#resolvesymbol)
     method.
    *   [`data_status`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#data_status)
         – specify `"delayed_streaming"`.
    *   [`delay`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#delay)
         – specify delay in seconds.

The image below shows the _Data is delayed_ section and the _D_ icon in the legend.

![Data is delayed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/assets/images/data-is-delayed-741f2420c57908102a90defe6f7294c6.png)

*   [Configure legend](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#configure-legend)
    *   [Symbol logos](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#symbol-logos)
        
    *   [Open market status](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#open-market-status)
        
    *   [Title](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#title)

    *   [Resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#resolution)

    *   [Hide legend](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend/#hide-legend)

# Die Krypto-Fundamentaldaten sind nun als Indikatoren verfügbar — TradingView Blog

Main content

[MARKTANALYSE](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/de/category/market-analysis/)

Die Krypto-Fundamentaldaten sind nun als Indikatoren verfügbar
==============================================================

Jan 6

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/crypto-fundamentals-as-indicators-preview.jpg)

Wir haben mit dem neuen Jahr verschiedene neue Indikatoren in unser Serviceangebot aufgenommen, mit denen Sie sich nun die fundamentalen Metriken der Kryptowährungen direkt auf Ihrem Chart ansehen können. Diese Indikatoren umfassen mehr als 30 Metriken und sind in 4 Kategorien unterteilt: Finanzdaten, Netzwerk, Inhaber und Social.

Mit diesen Tools können Sie sich nun nicht nur die Preisbewegungen der Kryptos ansehen, sondern auch ihre On-Chain-Daten, die Inhaberstruktur und die sozialen Entwicklungen. Sie können mehr über die einzelnen Indikatoren in unserer [Wissensbasis](https://de.tradingview.com/support/folders/43000587405/)
 erfahren. Sehen wir uns an dieser Stelle jedoch die allgemeinen Kategorien einmal genauer an.

Finanzdaten
-----------

Diese Kategorie verbindet die Wirtschaftsdaten mit der Netzwerkaktivität und den Marktbewertungen.

Zum Beispiel: **Die realisierte Marktkapitalisierung** berechnet den Gesamtwert von allen Coins basierend auf ihrer letzten Preisbewegung in der Blockchain. Auf diese Weise erhalten Sie ein genaues Bild davon, was die Teilnehmer tatsächlich bezahlt haben (im Vergleich zu einer klassischen Marktkapitalisierung). Dies ist nützlich, um den Fair-Value zu beurteilen, sowie um Akkumulierungs- und Distributionsphasen zu erkennen. Sie können dies auch verwenden, um langfristige Zonen zu erkennen und das Interesse von großen Teilnehmern zu erfassen.

Das **RVT-Ratio** (90 Tage) vergleicht die Marktkapitalisierung mit dem On-Chain-Transaktionsvolumen, um auf diese Weise zu bewerten, ob die aktuellen Preise der echten Netzwerkaktivität entsprechen. Die weiteren Metriken, wie die Qualitätsverhältnisse für das Angebot und das 1-jährige aktive Angebot (%) können Ihre Analysen optimieren, indem Sie Ihnen Einsichten in den Anteil der langfristigen Inhaber und das Verhalten von „schlafenden“ Wallets liefern.

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/crypto-fundamentals-as-indicators-1.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/crypto-fundamentals-as-indicators-1.png)

Netzwerk
--------

Die Netzwerkdaten umfassen die tatsächliche Nutzeraktivität und die Intensität der Netzwerknutzung. Steigende **aktive Adressen**, **Transaktionszahlen** und **Volumen** (in Token und USD) können oft die Stärke eines Preistrends bestätigen, während Divergenzen zwischen den Preisen und der On-Chain-Aktivität uns ein spekulatives Wachstum suggerieren können.

Die weiteren Metriken, wie die **Anzahl der großen Transaktionen** und **großes Transaktionsvolumen** verfolgen die Aktivitäten von großen Akteuren und institutionelle Kapitalzuflüsse mit. **Total Value Locked (TVL)** hilft uns dabei, die Skala und Nachfrage für das Krypto-Ökosystem einzuschätzen (insbesondere von DeFi-Protokollen).

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/crypto-fundamentals-as-indicators-2.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/crypto-fundamentals-as-indicators-2.png)

Inhaber
-------

Mit diesen Indikatoren können wir uns ansehen, wie die Token unter den Adressen verteilt sind, basierend auf einem eingestellten Schwellenwert („X“). Dieser Indikator zeigt uns das kombinierte Verhalten von Adressen über einem bestimmten Guthabenstand an und ermöglicht eine Analyse von unterschiedlichen Inhabergruppen – von Kleinanlegern bis zu den Walen:

*   Ein niedriger X-Wert deckt ein weites Spektrum von Adressen ab, inklusive kleine Inhaber und aktive Wallets. Verwenden Sie dies, wenn Sie das allgemeine Engagement einschätzen möchten.
*   Ein mittlerer X-Wert ist für mittlere bis große Inhaber nützlich. Hierbei werden die kleineren Guthaben herausgefiltert, um die Dynamiken klarer darstellen zu können.
*   Hohe X-Werte isolieren das Walverhalten. Dies ist nützlich, wenn wir uns die Kapitalverschiebungen und potenzielle Marktveränderungen ansehen möchten.

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/crypto-fundamentals-as-indicators-3.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/crypto-fundamentals-as-indicators-3.png)

Diese Indikatoren verwenden einen Standardwert für „X“. Sie können diesen Wert jedoch gemäß Ihren Bedürfnissen einstellen.

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/crypto-fundamentals-as-indicators-5.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/crypto-fundamentals-as-indicators-5.png)

Social
------

Die Social-Indikatoren zeigen die Stimmung unter den Nutzern einer bestimmten Krypto an. **Sentiment (%)** zeigt die allgemeine Stimmung an: Ein Wert in der Nähe von 100 steht hierbei für eine positive Einstellung, während Werte in der Nähe von 0 für eine negative Stimmung stehen.

Metriken wie **aktive Beiträge**, **erstellte Beiträge**, **aktive Beitragende** und **erstellte Beitragende** messen das Interesse in der Community für ein Asset und das allgemeine Engagement, und die **soziale Dominanz (%)** vergleicht die Erwähnungen eines Assets mit anderen Krypto-Projekten. Diese Einsichten sind insbesondere nützlich, um die Frühphasen eines steigenden Interesses zu erkennen und die Stabilität zu beurteilen.

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/crypto-fundamentals-as-indicators-4.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2026/01/crypto-fundamentals-as-indicators-4.png)

Mit diesen neuen Indikatoren können Sie nun eine noch tiefere Ansicht der Märkte erhalten und besser informierte Entscheidungen treffen.

Wir sind der Hoffnung, dass Sie mit dieser Ergänzung für Ihre Charts, die Kryptomärkte noch besser analysieren werden. Teilen Sie uns bitte mit, was Sie von diesen Indikatoren halten. Mit Ihrem Feedback können wir unsere Plattform noch besser machen.

Team TradingView

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView wurde für Sie entwickelt. Stellen Sie sicher, dass Sie unsere fantastischen Features optimal nutzen

[Supercharts öffnen](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

# Sonuçlar belli oldu — işte The Leap kazananları — TradingView Blog

Main content

[İşLEM VE ARACı KURUM](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/tr/category/trading/)

Sonuçlar belli oldu — işte The Leap kazananları
===============================================

Dec 5, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-preview.jpg)

Deneme işlemleri yarışmamız [The Leap](https://tr.tradingview.com/the-leap/)
, yeni bir dönemini daha tamamladı. Bugün, Kasım ayı kazananlarımızın başarısını kutluyor ve dünya çapındaki yatırımcılara ilham vermeye devam eden değerli görüşlerinden bazılarını paylaşıyoruz.

Ayrıntılara girmeden önce, bu 30 günlük işlem yarışmasında bize katılan tüm yetenekli ve tutkulu yatırımcılara bir kez daha teşekkür etmek istiyoruz. Yarışma süresince yaklaşık 60 bin yatırımcı, küçük bir ada satın almaya yetecek — 40 milyon doların üzerinde para kazandı. Sizin kararlılığınız, enerjiniz ve büyüme arayışınız mükemmel bir rekabet ortamı oluşturdu. Bu sadece kazanmakla ilgili değil; öğrenmek, uyum sağlamak ve yatırımcı olarak daha iyi hale gelmekle de ilgilidir.

Şimdi, deneme İşlemleri hesaplarında en çok kâr elde eden ve işlem dahileri olarak konumlarını güvence altına almayı başaran seçkin katılımcıların isimlerini açıklayalım.

Birinci, $3.000 ödül — [hoy8886](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/hoy8886/)

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-1.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-1.png)

İkinci, $1.500 ödül — [oyoyosan](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/oyoyosan/)

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-2.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-2.png)

Üçüncü, $1.200 ödül — [Harsh\_Raj02](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/Harsh_Raj02/)

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-3.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-3.png)

Dördüncü, $1.000 ödül — [daedunamul](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/daedunamul/)

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-4.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-4.png)

Beşinci, $800 ödül — [lkn81213](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/lkn81213/)

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-5.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-5.png)

Aynı zamanda, geri kalan para ödüllerini ve abonelik uzatmalarını kazanan 245 ek kazanana da özel bir alkış — bu kadar başarılı olmak için harika bir iş çıkardınız.

Aşağıda, yarışmanın genel istatistikleri de yer almaktadır: Ne kadar sayıda kârlı işlem olduğunu, katılımcıların hangi işlemleri yapmayı tercih ettiğini ve daha fazlasını kontrol edin — kim bilir, belki bir sonraki yarışmada size yardımcı olacak bazı trendleri fark edersiniz.

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-all.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/12/the-leap-4-winners-all.png)

Bu olağanüstü yatırımcıları kutlamak için bize eşlik edin! Elde ettikleri sonuçlar özveri, pratik ve bilgiye olan tutkunun neleri başarabileceğine tanıklık ediyor.

Bu sefer başaramadınız mı? Endişelenmeyin, yakında yeteneklerinizi denemek için yeni bir fırsat olacak. Bir sonraki yarışmamıza hazır olun ve aşağıdaki destek bağlantılarını inceleyerek başlayın:

*   Rehberimizi [buradan izleyin.](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EVnNI-9Gahc)
    
*   [Yardım Merkezimizi](https://tr.tradingview.com/the-leap/?folder=43000593579)
     ziyaret edin.
*   Bir sonraki yarışma için hazırlanın!

TradingView Ekibi

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView sizin için tasarlandı, bu nedenle muhteşem özelliklerimizden en iyi şekilde yararlandığınızdan emin olun

[Süpergrafikleri Aç](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)
