# Core trading concepts | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v28**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

Trading Platform provides the ability to trade right from the chart. Users can manage orders, track positions, monitor their potential profits and losses, and more. In this article, you will learn about the trading components, how they integrate into the chart widget, and how they work together.

Components[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#components "Direct link to Components")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Trading in Trading Platform is based on two key components: the [Broker API](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#broker-api)
 and the [Trading Host](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#trading-host)
. The diagram below illustrates how these components should be integrated with the library and your backend server.

[![Diagram illustrating trading components](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/trading-components-diagram-light.svg)![Diagram illustrating trading components](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/trading-components-diagram-dark.svg)](https://www.tradingview.com/img/trading-components-diagram-light.svg)

info

The _Backend trading server_ and _Broker API_ are the parts that you should implement.

### Broker API[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#broker-api "Direct link to Broker API")

The Broker API is a component that enables trading. Its purpose is to connect TradingView charts with your trading logic. In JavaScript terms, the Broker API is an object that implements a particular interface.

You can implement the Broker API through the [`IBrokerWithoutRealtime`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerWithoutRealtime)
 interface.

### Trading Host[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#trading-host "Direct link to Trading Host")

The Trading Host is an API for interaction between the Broker API and the trading-related library code. Its purpose is to receive information from your backend server where the trading logic is implemented and provide updates to the library.

The Trading Host is described by the [`IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost)
 interface. You should call the Trading Host methods from your [Broker API](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#broker-api)
 implementation.

How to enable trading[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#how-to-enable-trading "Direct link to How to enable trading")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Implement quote methods[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#implement-quote-methods "Direct link to Implement quote methods")

info

To use Trading Platform, in addition to the [required](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/datafeed-api/required-methods)
 Datafeed API methods, you should implement the quote-related methods.

*   [`getQuotes`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/datafeed-api/trading-platform-methods#getquotes)
    
*   [`subscribeQuotes`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/datafeed-api/trading-platform-methods#subscribequotes)
    
*   [`unsubscribeQuotes`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/connecting_data/datafeed-api/trading-platform-methods#unsubscribequotes)
    

The library calls these methods to request real-time quotes, which are data sets that describe the current prices. Quotes are used in most Trading Platform features including the [Order Ticket](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/order-ticket)
, [Legend](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/ui_elements/Legend)
, and widgets, such as [Watchlist](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/Watch-List)
, [Details](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/#details)
, [News](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/news)
, and [Depth of Market](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/depth-of-market)
.

### Connect Broker API[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#connect-broker-api "Direct link to Connect Broker API")

To enable trading, you should pass the function that returns a new object of the Broker API implementation to the library. To do this, use the [`broker_factory`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TradingTerminalWidgetOptions#broker_factory)
 property of the [Widget Constructor](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/core_concepts/Widget-Constructor#trading-platform-parameters)
. Note that this function should accept the Trading Host instance as a parameter.

In the code sample below, the function assigned to `broker_factory` accepts `tradingHost` parameter, which is an instance of [`IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost)
. This function returns an instance of `BrokerSample` that implements [`IBrokerWithoutRealtime`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerWithoutRealtime)
.

    const datafeed = new Datafeeds.UDFCompatibleDatafeed("https://demo-feed-data.tradingview.com");new TradingView.widget({    container: "chartContainer",    locale: "en",    library_path: "charting_library/",    datafeed: datafeed,    symbol: "AAPL",    interval: "1D",    broker_factory: function(tradingHost: IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost) { return new Brokers.BrokerSample(tradingHost, datafeed); },})

How library gets user's data[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#how-library-gets-users-data "Direct link to How library gets user's data")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

When the chart is initially loaded, the library requests data from your Broker API implementation through the following methods:

*   [`orders`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerWithoutRealtime#orders)
    
*   [`positions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerWithoutRealtime#positions)
    
*   [`executions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerWithoutRealtime#executions)
    
*   [`individualPositions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerWithoutRealtime#individualpositions)
     (optional, required if the [`supportPositionNetting`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpositionnetting)
     flag is set to `true`).

Using these methods, the library retrieves data about the orders, positions, and executions the user already had before the chart creation. Then, the library gets updates for these orders and positions through the Trading Host methods. Refer to the [next section](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#how-components-work-together)
 for a step-by-step example.

How components work together[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#how-components-work-together "Direct link to How components work together")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

To understand how the library, [Broker API](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#broker-api)
, [Trading Host](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#trading-host)
, and your implemented trading logic should work together, consider the following step-by-step example. Suppose a user wants to buy 10 AAPL shares. This user action initiates three consecutive steps:

[![Diagram illustrating the action steps](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/create-order-steps-diagram-light.svg)![Diagram illustrating the action steps](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/create-order-steps-diagram-dark.svg)](https://www.tradingview.com/img/create-order-steps-diagram-light.svg)

In the subsequent sections, you will delve into each of these steps, finding detailed explanations and sequence diagrams:

1.  [Order creation](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#1-order-creation)
    
2.  [Execution update](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#2-execution-update)
    
3.  [Equity update](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#3-equity-update)
    

You can also refer to the diagram that illustrates the entire process, starting from creating the order in the UI until the position is established.

Expand to view the diagram of the entire process.

[![Diagram illustrating the entire process of creating order](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/create-order-complete-diagram-light.svg)![Diagram illustrating the entire process of creating order](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/create-order-complete-diagram-dark.svg)](https://www.tradingview.com/img/create-order-complete-diagram-light.svg)

### 1\. Order creation[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#1-order-creation "Direct link to 1. Order creation")

The diagram below illustrates the process of creating an order.

[![Order creation diagram](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/order-creation-diagram-light.svg)![Order creation diagram](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/order-creation-diagram-dark.svg)](https://www.tradingview.com/img/order-creation-diagram-light.svg)

1.  The user specifies 10 units of AAPL shares in the [Order Ticket](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/order-ticket)
     and clicks the _Buy_ button.
2.  The library interprets this action as a trigger to notify your Broker API implementation that the user wants to create the order.
3.  The library calls the [`placeOrder`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerWithoutRealtime#placeorder)
     method, passing along the order data. The code sample below shows an example of the data object:
    
        {    "symbol": "NasdaqNM:AAPL",    "type": 2,  // OrderType.Market    "side": 1,  // Side.Buy    "qty": 10,    "currentQuotes": {        "ask": 173.68,        "bid": 173.68    },    "customFields": {}}
    
    Note that your Broker API implementation should interpret this call as a notification of the user's intent to create the order.
4.  Your Broker API implementation responds with [`PlaceOrderResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PlaceOrderResult)
    .
5.  The library waits for your backend server to create the order within 10 seconds and provide the updated information. Note that the library will return a [timeout issue](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue)
     if it fails to receive a timely order update.
6.  Your Broker API implementation requests your backend server to create the order.
7.  Your backend server creates the order and prepares the updated information.
8.  Your backend server provides a response to your Broker API implementation with updated information.
9.  Your Broker API implementation calls the [`orderUpdate`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#orderupdate)
     method. As a parameter, it sends the [`PlacedOrder`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PlacedOrder)
     object to the library. Note that the `qty`, `id`, `symbol`, and `side` properties must be identical to the data provided in step 3 within the `placeOrder` method.  
    The code sample below shows an example of the `PlacedOrder` object:
    
        {    "id": "1",    "qty": 10,    "side": 1,   // Side.Buy    "status": 6, // OrderStatus.Working    "symbol": "NasdaqNM:AAPL",    "type": 2    // OrderType.Market}
    
    info
    
    Here, the object has a `status` property that indicates the order's current [status](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/enums/Charting_Library.OrderStatus)
    . Initially, when the order is created but has not been executed, it is assigned the _working_ status. Once the order is executed, its status should be updated to _filled_. This will be done [further](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#2-execution-update)
    .
    
10.  The Trading Host receives updates and informs the library and the UI that the order has been created.
11.  The user sees the new working order in the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/account-manager/)
    .

After this, the backend server should [execute the working order](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#2-execution-update)
 and add a new position.

### 2\. Execution update[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#2-execution-update "Direct link to 2. Execution update")

At this point, the user can see the new working order in the Account Manager. Following this, your backend server is responsible for executing the order and creating a position. The diagram below illustrates this process.

[![Execution update diagram](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/execution-update-diagram-light.svg)![Execution update diagram](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/execution-update-diagram-dark.svg)](https://www.tradingview.com/img/execution-update-diagram-light.svg)

1.  Your backend server executes the order and prepares the updated information. Note that the order execution and update might be processed on external sources, such as exchanges. However, your server is expected to manage this information and provide it in the format required by the library.
2.  Your backend server provides a response to your Broker API implementation with updated information.
3.  Your Broker API implementation calls the [`executionUpdate`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#executionupdate)
     method. As a parameter, it sends the [`Execution`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Execution)
     object. The code sample below shows an example of this object:
    
        {    "price": 173.68,    "qty": 10,    "side": 1,  // Side.Buy    "symbol": "NasdaqNM:AAPL",    "time": 1697032262341}
    
4.  Your Broker API implementation calls the [`orderUpdate`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#orderupdate)
     method to update the order status to _filled_. As a parameter, your Broker API implementation sends the [`PlacedOrder`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PlacedOrder)
     object. The code sample below shows an example of this object:
    
        {    "id": "1",    "qty": 10,    "side": 1,    // Side.Buy    "status": 2,  // OrderStatus.Filled    "symbol": "NasdaqNM:AAPL",    "type": 2,    // OrderType.Market}
    
5.  Your Broker API implementation calls the [`positionUpdate`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate)
     method to notify the Trading Host that the position is added. As a parameter, it sends the [`Position`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position)
     object. The code sample below shows an example of this object:
    
        {    "id": "NasdaqNM:AAPL",    "qty": 10,    "side": 1,  // Side.Buy    "symbol": "NasdaqNM:AAPL",    "avgPrice": 173.68}
    
6.  The Trading Host receives updates and informs the library and the UI that the order has been executed and the position has been added.
7.  The user sees a new position of 10 AAPL shares in the Account Manager.

After this, your backend server should [update user's equity](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#3-equity-update)
.

### 3\. Equity update[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#3-equity-update "Direct link to 3. Equity update")

At this stage, the user sees that the order has been executed and the position has been created. Next, your backend server should update the user's equity and the Profit & Loss (P&L) values for all active positions whenever there is a price change.

info

To keep the UI data up-to-date, you should constantly provide updates of users' entity and P&L values whenever changes occur.

The diagram below illustrates the process of updating the equity and P&L values.

[![Equity update diagram](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/equity-update-diagram-light.svg)![Equity update diagram](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/img/equity-update-diagram-dark.svg)](https://www.tradingview.com/img/equity-update-diagram-light.svg)

1.  Your backend server calculates the user's equity and P&L and prepares the updated information. Note that the calculations might be processed on external sources, such as exchanges. However, your server is expected to manage this information and provide it in the format required by the library.
2.  Your backend server provides a response to your Broker API implementation with updated information.
3.  Your Broker API implementation calls the [`equityUpdate`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#equityupdate)
     method to notify the Trading Host about equity updates. As a parameter, it sends the accurate equity amount, for example, `[10000000]`.
4.  Your Broker API implementation calls the [`plUpdate`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#plupdate)
     method to notify the Trading Host about P&L updates.
5.  The Trading Host notifies the library and the UI about updates.
6.  The user sees updated equity and P&L values in the Account Manager.

At this stage, the user owns the position of 10 AAPL shares.

Implementation example[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#implementation-example "Direct link to Implementation example")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

You can reference the example of the Broker API implementation on GitHub. However, the repository is private and requires you to [get access](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/getting_started/quick-start#getting-access)
 first.

The [Broker API example](https://github.com/tradingview/trading_platform/tree/master/broker-sample "The repository is private.")
 🔐 includes TypeScript source code, which can serve as a template for your implementation. Note that this example does not establish a connection with an actual broker but simulates order and position management as a mock setup. This implementation is also used on the [Trading Platform demo page](https://trading-terminal.tradingview-widget.com/)
 where you can experiment with various trading features.

*   [Components](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#components)
    *   [Broker API](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#broker-api)
        
    *   [Trading Host](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#trading-host)
        
*   [How to enable trading](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#how-to-enable-trading)
    *   [Implement quote methods](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#implement-quote-methods)
        
    *   [Connect Broker API](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#connect-broker-api)
        
*   [How library gets user's data](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#how-library-gets-users-data)
    
*   [How components work together](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#how-components-work-together)
    *   [1\. Order creation](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#1-order-creation)
        
    *   [2\. Execution update](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#2-execution-update)
        
    *   [3\. Equity update](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#3-equity-update)

# TradeStation sponsorluğundaki The Leap sona erdi: Sonuçlar açıklandı — TradingView Blog

Main content

[İşLEM VE ARACı KURUM](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/tr/category/trading/)

TradeStation sponsorluğundaki The Leap sona erdi: Sonuçlar açıklandı
====================================================================

Jul 4, 2025

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-preview.jpg)

Yaz zirveye ulaşırken, grafiklerdeki harareti bir kenara bırakıp en son The Leap yarışmasındaki yatırımcıların olağanüstü performansını kutlama zamanı. TradeStation sponsorluğunda bir ay süren deneme işlemleri yarışmamız sona erdi ve sonuçlar açıklandı.

Bu yarışma, 50.000$’lık ödül havuzundan pay almak için 54 binden fazla yatırımcıyı küresel, risksiz bir savaşta bir araya getirdi. Dört hafta boyunca katılımcılar, S&P 500, altın ve hatta Bitcoin üzerinden vadeli işlemler yaparak becerilerini test ettiler. Vadeli işlemlerin değişken doğası ile hem hassasiyet hem de strateji gerektiren piyasa dalgalanmaları, en deneyimli yatırımcıları bile sınırlarını zorlamaya itti.

The Leap’e katıldığınız ve bu yarışmayı olağanüstü kıldığınız için her birinize teşekkür ederiz. Şimdi, zirveye yükselen ve [skor tablosunda](https://tr.tradingview.com/the-leap/)
 yerini alan yatırımcıları kutlayalım:

1\. sıra, 8.500$ ödül — [BitK77](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/BitK77/)

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-1.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-1.png)

2\. sıra, 7.000$ ödül — [JYnVN](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/JYnVN/)

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-2.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-2.png)

3\. sıra, 6.000$ ödül — [magot98](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/magot98/)

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-3.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-3.png)

4\. sıra, 5.000$ ödül — [ElMariachi333](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/ElMariachi333/)

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-4.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-4.png)

5\. sıra, 3.500$ ödül — [pocorabanne](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/pocorabanne/)

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-5.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-5.png)

Toplamda 250 yatırımcı nakit ödüller ve abonelik uzatmaları kazandı, ama hepsi bu kadar değil! Bu kez yarışma, iki yeni kategorinin tanıtımına sahne oldu:

*   En Kârlı İşlem: Yarışma sırasında en yüksek kârlı işlemleri yapanlar için. Bu ödülün sahipleri:
    *   [Tugzz](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/Tugzz/)
         (en büyük başarılı işlem — 29.528,3$)
    *   [danielezannin](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/danielezannin/)
         (27.733,3$)
    *   [Devesh\_Gugale](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/Devesh_Gugale/)
         (26.323,3$)
    *   [lukaszGLM](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/lukaszGLM/)
         (24.988,3$)
    *   [chung3207](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/chung3207/)
         (24.978,3$)
*   Kusursuz Seri: Tüm yarışmayı tek bir kârsız emir olmadan tamamlayan yatırımcıları vurgular. Ödülleri kazananlar:
    *   [diegogpueyo](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/diegogpueyo/)
         (elde edilen kâr — 54.754,8$)
    *   [Clive\_Thompson](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/Clive_Thompson/)
         (47.856,75$)
    *   [AnalizyD](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/AnalizyD/)
         (47.178,25$)
    *   [chancefischerr](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/chancefischerr/)
         (39.997,4$)
    *   [tamirishere](https://tr.tradingview.com/u/tamirishere/)
         (37.091,7$)

Bununla da kalmayalım, sizin için yarışmanın genel istatistiklerini hazırladık. En çok işlem gören varlıklara, kâr/zarar oranlarına ve diğer bilgilere göz atın. Bir sonraki yarışmamıza hazırlanmak için rakamları keşfedin, trendleri belirleyin ve sonuçları analiz edin.

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-overall-stats.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/07/tradingview-leap-by-tradestation-results-overall-stats.png)

İster zirveye çıkmış olun, ister yol boyunca değerli bir deneyim kazanmış olun, katılan herkesi tebrik ederiz. Bir sonraki yarışma turumuz için bizi izlemeye devam edin ve bu işlem becerilerinizi geliştirmeye devam edin. Aşağıdaki destek bağlantılarını inceleyerek başlayın.

*   [Leap rehberimizi](https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EVnNI-9Gahc)
     izleyin
*   Bilgi bankamızda [sıkça sorulan Leap sorularını](https://tr.tradingview.com/the-leap/?folder=43000593579)
     bulun
*   Bir sonraki yarışma için hazırlanın!

TradingView Ekibi

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView sizin için tasarlandı, bu nedenle muhteşem özelliklerimizden en iyi şekilde yararlandığınızdan emin olun

[Süpergrafikleri Aç](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

# Nossos Pine Script️® Wizards para 2024 — TradingView Blog

Main content

[PINE SCRIPT®](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/pb/category/pine/)

Nossos Pine Script️® Wizards para 2024
======================================

Jan 15, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/01/pine-script-wizards-2024-preview.jpg)

Mais uma vez, chegamos à época do ano em que destacamos os mais novos membros de nosso Hall da Fama do Pine Script️®. Esses autores excepcionais não são apenas programadores; eles são os pilares de nossa comunidade, contribuindo com sua engenhosidade, habilidades e conhecimento para aperfeiçoar a experiência do TradingView para todos.

Como seus predecessores, esses dois magos doaram um tesouro de scripts de código aberto à comunidade do TradingView, fornecendo ferramentas de negociação transparentes e versáteis e inspirando inúmeros programadores do Pine. Não deixe de explorar suas criações. Seus trabalhos publicados são tão bonitos quanto úteis.

Sem mais delongas, vamos conhecer nossos [Pine Wizards de 2024](https://br.tradingview.com/pine-wizards/)
. Em nome de todos os TradingViewers, obrigado!

#### KioseffTrading

Os scripts de [KioseffTrading](https://br.tradingview.com/u/KioseffTrading/#published-scripts)
 redefinem a estética dos gráficos. Reconhecido por suas visualizações de perfil de volume e delta de volume, esse programador prolífico e magistral mescla de forma eficiente a arte com a análise para produzir scripts úteis com visuais impressionantes.

#### LuxAlgo

A equipe [LuxAlgo](https://br.tradingview.com/u/LuxAlgo/#published-scripts)
 é uma potência no cenário cada vez mais abrangente de scripts comunitários do TradingView, fornecendo consistentemente scripts de código aberto de qualidade e de grande utilidade que abrangem um espectro impressionante de conceitos diversos e originais. As contribuições da LuxAlgo se tornaram as favoritas dos traders e programadores que buscam scripts versáteis e de alto nível.

Look first Then leap
--------------------

O TradingView é feito para você, então garanta que você está aproveitando ao máximo nossos recursos incríveis

[Abrir Supergráficos](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

# Enumeration: PriceScaleMode | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.PriceScaleMode/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.PriceScaleMode

Enumeration Members[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.PriceScaleMode/#enumeration-members "Direct link to Enumeration Members")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### Normal[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.PriceScaleMode/#normal "Direct link to Normal")

• **Normal** = `0`

Normal mode of the price scale

* * *

### Log[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.PriceScaleMode/#log "Direct link to Log")

• **Log** = `1`

Logarithmic mode of the price scale

* * *

### Percentage[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.PriceScaleMode/#percentage "Direct link to Percentage")

• **Percentage** = `2`

Percentage mode of the price scale

* * *

### IndexedTo100[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.PriceScaleMode/#indexedto100 "Direct link to IndexedTo100")

• **IndexedTo100** = `3`

Indexed to 100 mode of the price scale

*   [Enumeration Members](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.PriceScaleMode/#enumeration-members)
    *   [Normal](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.PriceScaleMode/#normal)

About E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures
-----------------------------------﻿

E-mini Dow Jones ($5) futures are derivative contracts with the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) Index as an underlying asset. These mini futures are electronically traded and give investors exposure to the US stock market through 30 US blue-chip companies, but they represent only a portion ($5) of standard Dow Jones futures, which makes them more accessible. Since these are still futures, traders use them to hedge their stock portfolios against risks that are inevitable in markets.

Frequently Asked Questions
--------------------------﻿

What is the price of E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures?

The current price of E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures is 49,690 USD — it has risen 0.28% in the past 24 hours. Watch E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures price in more detail on the [chart](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=CBOT_MINI:YM1!)
.

What is E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures volume?

The volume of E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures is ‪118.36 K‬. Track more important stats on the [E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures chart](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=CBOT_MINI:YM1!)
.

What is Open interest? How can I use it to trade E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures?

[Open interest](https://www.tradingview.com/?solution=43000685269)
 is the number of contracts held by traders in active positions — they're not closed or expired. For E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures this number is ‪70.47 K‬. You can use it to track a prevailing market trend and adjust your own strategy: declining open interest for E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures shows that traders are closing their positions, which means a weakening trend.

Is it time to buy or sell E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures?

Buying or selling futures contracts depends on many factors: season, underlying commodity, your own trading strategy. So mostly it's up to you, but if you look for some certain calculations to take into account, you can study technical analysis for E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures. Today its technical rating is buy, but remember that market conditions change all the time, so it's always crucial to do your own research. See more of [E-mini Dow Jones ($5) Futures technicals](https://www.tradingview.com/symbols/CBOT_MINI-YM1!/technicals/)
 for a more comprehensive analysis.

# Interface: AccountManagerColumnBase<TFormatterName> | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Broker](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Broker)
.AccountManagerColumnBase

Column description for an account manager table

Type parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `TFormatterName` | extends [`StandardFormatterName`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/enums/Broker.StandardFormatterName)<br> \| [`FormatterName`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Broker#formattername) |

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### alignment[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#alignment "Direct link to alignment")

• `Optional` **alignment**: [`CellAlignment`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Broker#cellalignment)

Horizontal alignment of the cell value. The default value is `left`.

| alignment | description |
| --- | --- |
| left | It aligns the cell value to the left |
| right | It aligns the cell value to the right |

* * *

### dataFields[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#datafields "Direct link to dataFields")

• **dataFields**: `TFormatterName` extends [`StandardFormatterName`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/enums/Broker.StandardFormatterName)
 ? [`StandardFormattersDependenciesMapping`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.StandardFormattersDependenciesMapping)
\[`TFormatterName`<`TFormatterName`\>\] : `string`\[\]

`dataFields` is an array with data object fields that is used to get the data to display in a column.

The displayed value in the column will only change if one of the corresponding data object values change.

If the `formatter` is not set, the displayed values will be space-separated in the column.

If a `formatter` is specified, it will only get the specified values.

Specify an empty array as the `dataFields` and the formatter will receive the entire data object.

**Example** Example

*   If you have column with `dataFields` set as `['avgPrice', 'qty']`, then displayed value will update only if `avgPrice` or `qty` values of the data object have been changed.
*   If you have column with `dataFields` set as `[]`, then displayed value will update if some data object values have been changed.

* * *

### formatter[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#formatter "Direct link to formatter")

• `Optional` **formatter**: `TFormatterName`

Name of the formatter to be used for data formatting. It can be one of two types - `StandardFormatterName` or `FormatterName`. If `formatter` is not set, then the value is displayed as is. Formatter can be a default or a custom one.

Default formatter names are listed in `StandardFormatterName` enumerator. If you want to use a custom formatter, you must typecast its name to `FormatterName` to confirm your confidence that you are using the correct name.

Here is the list of default formatters:

| name | description |
| --- | --- |
| `StandardFormatterName.Date` | Displays the date or time. |
| `StandardFormatterName.DateOrDateTime` | Displays the date or date and time. This formatter accepts an `{dateOrDateTime: number, hasTime: boolean}` object. If `hasTime` is set to `true` then the date and time are displayed. Otherwise only the date is displayed. |
| `StandardFormatterName.Fixed` | Displays a number with 2 decimal places. |
| `StandardFormatterName.FixedInCurrency` | Displays a number with 2 decimal places and adds currency. |
| `StandardFormatterName.FormatPrice` | Displays symbol's price. |
| `StandardFormatterName.FormatQuantity` | Displays an integer or floating point quantity, separates thousands groups with a space. |
| `StandardFormatterName.FormatPriceForexSup` | The same as `formatPrice`, but it makes the last character of the price superscripted. It works only if instrument type is set to `forex`. |
| `StandardFormatterName.LocalDate` | Displays the local date or time. |
| `StandardFormatterName.LocalDateOrDateTime` | The same as `StandardFormatterName.DateOrDateTime`, but it displays time in the local timezone. |
| `StandardFormatterName.Pips` | Displays a number with 1 decimal place. |
| `StandardFormatterName.Profit` | Displays profit in account currency. It also adds the `+` sign, separates thousands and changes the cell text color to red or green. |
| `StandardFormatterName.ProfitInInstrumentCurrency` | Displays profit in instrument currency. It also adds the `+` sign, separates thousands and changes the cell text color to red or green. |
| `StandardFormatterName.Side` | It is used to display the side: Sell or Buy. |
| `StandardFormatterName.PositionSide` | It is used to display the position side: Short or Long. |
| `StandardFormatterName.Status` | It is used to format the `status`. |
| `StandardFormatterName.Symbol` | It is used for a symbol field. It displays `brokerSymbol`, but when you click on a symbol the chart changes according to the `symbol` field. |
| `StandardFormatterName.Text` | Displays a text value. |
| `StandardFormatterName.Type` | It is used to display the type of order: Limit/Stop/StopLimit/Market. |
| `StandardFormatterName.VariablePrecision` | Displays a number with variable precision. |

* * *

### help[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#help "Direct link to help")

• `Optional` **help**: `string`

Tooltip string for the column.

* * *

### hideByDefault[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#hidebydefault "Direct link to hideByDefault")

• `Optional` **hideByDefault**: `boolean`

When set to `true` will hide the column by default

* * *

### highlightDiff[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#highlightdiff "Direct link to highlightDiff")

• `Optional` **highlightDiff**: `boolean`

`highlightDiff` can be set with `StandardFormatterName.FormatPrice` and `StandardFormatterName.FormatPriceForexSup` formatters to highlight the changes of the field. If set to `true` then custom formatters will also get previous values.

* * *

### id[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#id "Direct link to id")

• **id**: `string`

Column id. Unique identifier of column.

* * *

### isCapitalize[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#iscapitalize "Direct link to isCapitalize")

• `Optional` **isCapitalize**: `boolean`

If set to `true`, the first character of every word in the sentence in the column will be capitalized. The default value is `true`.

* * *

### label[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#label "Direct link to label")

• **label**: `string`

Column title. It will be displayed in the table's header row.

* * *

### notHideable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#nothideable "Direct link to notHideable")

• `Optional` **notHideable**: `boolean`

When set to `true` will prevent the column from hiding.

* * *

### notSortable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#notsortable "Direct link to notSortable")

• `Optional` **notSortable**: `boolean`

When set to `true` will prevent column sorting.

* * *

### showZeroValues[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#showzerovalues "Direct link to showZeroValues")

• `Optional` **showZeroValues**: `boolean`

When set to `true` any zero values will be hidden. Default is `true`

* * *

### sortProp[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#sortprop "Direct link to sortProp")

• `Optional` **sortProp**: `string`

Data object key that is used for data sorting

If `sortProp` is not provided, then the first element of the `dataFields` array will be used. If the `dataFields` array is empty, then column sorting will be unavailable.

* * *

### tooltipProperty[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#tooltipproperty "Direct link to tooltipProperty")

• `Optional` **tooltipProperty**: `string`

Key of the row object that is used to get the tooltip to display when hovering over a cell. The tooltip property refers to an object whose keys are property names and values are the corresponding tooltips.

*   [Type parameters](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#type-parameters)
    
*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#properties)
    *   [alignment](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#alignment)
        
    *   [dataFields](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerColumnBase/#datafields)

# Interface: AccessList | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccessList/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v27**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.AccessList

Defines a whitelist / blacklist of studies or drawing tools.

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccessList/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### tools[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccessList/#tools "Direct link to tools")

• **tools**: [`AccessListItem`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccessListItem)
\[\]

Array of items which should be considered part of the access list

* * *

### type[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccessList/#type "Direct link to type")

• **type**: `"black"` | `"white"`

List type. Supported values are: `black` (all listed items should be disabled), `white` (only the listed items should be enabled).

# Trade crypto with BTCC directly on TradingView — TradingView Blog

Main content

[TRADING AND BROKERAGE](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/en/category/trading/)

Trade crypto with BTCC directly on TradingView
==============================================

Dec 9, 2025

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/12/crypto-btcc-on-tradingview_blogcover.jpg)

We’re expanding our roster of integrated partners with BTCC — a cryptocurrency exchange established in 2011 and licensed in multiple jurisdictions internationally. Traders can now connect to BTCC from the trading panel and trade crypto without leaving their TradingView charts.

Headquartered in Lithuania, BTCC serves 10M+ users in 100+ countries. As one of the longest-running exchanges in the digital-asset space, the firm emphasizes reliability, regulatory compliance, and deep market liquidity, giving traders the confidence to execute substantial positions with minimal price impact.

The exchange provides streamlined onboarding, competitive fees, and a full suite of welcome perks such as sign-up bonuses and referral rewards. Whether you are refining strategies or entering the market for the first time, BTCC’s low-latency infrastructure and dedicated customer support make it easy to get started and stay informed.

To begin trading with BTCC, open the [trading panel](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/)
, select the exchange’s icon, and log in with your account. For additional details, visit the BTCC profile on TradingView.

[BTCC broker profile](https://www.tradingview.com/broker/BTCC/?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=internal&utm_campaign=BTCC.)

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/12/crypto-btcc-on-tradingview_blogscreenshot.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/12/crypto-btcc-on-tradingview_blogscreenshot.png)

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView is built for you, so make sure you're getting the most of our awesome features

[Launch Supercharts](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

# Cakupan derivatif yang diperluas dengan option Eurex di TradingView — TradingView Blog

Main content

[FEED DATA DAN BURSA](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/id/category/data-feeds-exchanges/)

Cakupan derivatif yang diperluas dengan option Eurex di TradingView
===================================================================

Dec 24, 2025

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/12/eurex-options-on-tradingview-preview.jpg)

Kami terus memperluas cakupan bursa, instrumen, dan kelas aset yang tersedia di TradingView. Untuk memberikan transparansi dan kedalaman yang lebih pada analisis Anda, kami memperkenalkan tambahan baru pada penawaran data derivatif kami: data option dari Eurex, kini hadir pada chart Anda.

Eurex adalah salah satu bursa derivatif terkemuka di dunia dan merupakan bagian penting dari Deutsche Börse Group. Sejak diluncurkan pada tahun 1998, Eurex telah memantapkan dirinya sebagai platform utama untuk trading, kliring, dan manajemen risiko. Sebagai pelopor trading elektronik sepenuhnya, Eurex menawarkan akses ke beragam produk kontrak berjangka dan option, melayani pelaku pasar di seluruh Eropa dan global.

Dengan integrasi ini, Anda sekarang dapat mengakses data option untuk beberapa benchmark terpenting di Eropa. Ini termasuk kontrak pada Indeks EURO STOXX 50 (EUREX:OESX), Indeks Bank EURO STOXX (EUREX:OESB), dan option DAX (EUREX:ODAX), yang umumnya digunakan untuk menganalisis ekspektasi pasar ekuitas dan risiko sektor tertentu.

Kami juga menambahkan option pada kontrak berjangka VSTOXX (EUREX:OVS2), yang menawarkan wawasan tentang ekspektasi volatilitas pasar, serta option akhir hari EURO STOXX 50 (EUREX:OEXP) dan option STOXX Europe 600 (EUREX:OXXP), yang membantu para trader untuk menilai sentimen pasar Eropa secara lebih luas. Bersama-sama, instrumen-instrumen ini memberikan wawasan berharga untuk analisis volatilitas, [strategi hedging](https://id.tradingview.com/options/strategies/handbook/CME_MINI-ES1!/)
, dan perencanaan trading.

Untuk mulai menjelajahi data baru ini, buka [pencarian simbol](https://id.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000746682/)
 dan ketik salah satu ticker dalam tanda kurung di atas:

[![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/12/eurex-options-on-tradingview-1.png)](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2025/12/eurex-options-on-tradingview-1.png)

Kami berharap penambahan data terbaru ini membantu Anda dalam menjelajahi pasar global dengan lebih efektif. Ingat, TradingView terhubung ke ratusan feed data, memberikan akses langsung ke lebih dari dua juta instrumen di seluruh dunia — menjadikan kami satu-satunya platform yang Anda butuhkan untuk melacak segala hal di pasar dunia.

[Jelajahi seluruh pasar](https://id.tradingview.com/data-coverage/?utm_source=blog&utm_medium=internal&utm_campaign=xtcom.)

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView dibangun untuk anda, karenanya manfaatkan fitur-fitur luar biasa kami semaksimal mungkin

[Luncurkan Superchart](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

# Interface: IBrokerTerminal | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.IBrokerTerminal

The Broker API is a key component that enables trading. Its main purpose is to connect TradingView charts with your trading logic. Refer to the [Core trading concepts](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/)
 article for more information.

Hierarchy[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#hierarchy "Direct link to Hierarchy")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`IBrokerCommon`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon)
    
*   [`IBrokerAccountInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerAccountInfo)
    
    ↳ **`IBrokerTerminal`**
    

Methods[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#methods "Direct link to Methods")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### accountManagerInfo[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#accountmanagerinfo "Direct link to accountManagerInfo")

▸ **accountManagerInfo**(): [`AccountManagerInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccountManagerInfo)

The library calls `accountManagerInfo` to get information required for building the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/account-manager/)
.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns "Direct link to Returns")

[`AccountManagerInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccountManagerInfo)

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon)
.[accountManagerInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon#accountmanagerinfo)

* * *

### accountsMetainfo[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#accountsmetainfo "Direct link to accountsMetainfo")

▸ **accountsMetainfo**(): `Promise`<[`AccountMetainfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccountMetainfo)
\[\]>

The library calls `accountsMetainfo` to get a list of accounts for a particular user. The method should return an array that contains an ID and name for each account.

Note that if `accountsMetainfo` returns an array containing more than one element, you should implement the [setCurrentAccount](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerAccountInfo#setcurrentaccount)
 method. Refer to [Multiple accounts](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/account-manager/multiple-accounts)
 for more information.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`AccountMetainfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.AccountMetainfo)
\[\]>

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerAccountInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerAccountInfo)
.[accountsMetainfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerAccountInfo#accountsmetainfo)

* * *

### cancelOrder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#cancelorder "Direct link to cancelOrder")

▸ **cancelOrder**(`orderId`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `cancelOrder` to request canceling an order. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new order state. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.orderUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#orderupdate)
 method right afterwards.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `orderId` | `string` | The ID of the order to cancel. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### cancelOrders[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#cancelorders "Direct link to cancelOrders")

▸ **cancelOrders**(`symbol`, `side`, `ordersIds`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `cancelOrders` to request canceling multiple orders for a symbol. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new order states. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.orderUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#orderupdate)
 method right afterwards.

`cancelOrders` is only called when users click the _CXL all_ button in the [Depth of Market](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/depth-of-market)
 widget.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | The symbol identifier. |
| `side` | [`Side`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/enums/Charting_Library.Side) | An order side. |
| `ordersIds` | `string`\[\] | IDs of the orders to cancel. The orders are selected based on the specified `symbol` and `side`. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### chartContextMenuActions[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#chartcontextmenuactions "Direct link to chartContextMenuActions")

▸ **chartContextMenuActions**(`context`, `options?`): `Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#actionmetainfo)
\[\]>

The library calls `chartContextMenuActions` when users open the [context menu](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/ui_elements/context-menu)
 on the chart. This method also renders the _Trade_ button in the context menu.

This method should return an array of [ActionMetaInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#actionmetainfo)
 elements, each of them representing one context menu item.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `context` | [`TradeContext`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TradeContext) | A context object passed by the library. |
| `options?` | [`DefaultContextMenuActionsParams`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DefaultContextMenuActionsParams) | Default options for the context menu action parameters. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-4 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#actionmetainfo)
\[\]>

A promise that resolves to an array of [ActionMetaInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#actionmetainfo)
, which may be empty. In that case, the _Trade_ button will be removed from the context menu.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon)
.[chartContextMenuActions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon#chartcontextmenuactions)

* * *

### closeIndividualPosition[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#closeindividualposition "Direct link to closeIndividualPosition")

▸ **closeIndividualPosition**(`individualPositionId`, `amount?`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `closeIndividualPosition` if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportCloseIndividualPosition](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportcloseindividualposition)
 or [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPartialCloseIndividualPosition](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpartialcloseindividualposition)
 configuration flag is `true`. `closeIndividualPosition` allows [closing an individual position](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#close-positions)
 by ID.

Note that the library expects you to call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate)
 method right afterwards. Otherwise, the library will return a [timeout issue](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue)
.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-3 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `individualPositionId` | `string` | The individual position ID. |
| `amount?` | `number` | The amount is specified if `supportPartialCloseIndividualPosition` is `true` and the user wants to close only [part of the individual position](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#partial-closing)<br>. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-5 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### closePosition[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#closeposition "Direct link to closePosition")

▸ **closePosition**(`positionId`, `amount?`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `closePosition` to request [closing a position](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#close-positions)
 by ID. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new position state. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate)
 method right afterwards. Otherwise, the library will return a [timeout issue](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue)
.

`closePosition` is only called if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportClosePosition](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportcloseposition)
 or [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPartialClosePosition](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpartialcloseposition)
 configuration flag is `true`.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-4 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `positionId` | `string` | The position ID. |
| `amount?` | `number` | The amount is specified if [`supportPartialClosePosition`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpartialcloseposition)<br> is `true` and the user wants to close only [part of the position](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#partial-closing)<br>. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-6 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### connectionStatus[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#connectionstatus "Direct link to connectionStatus")

▸ **connectionStatus**(): [`ConnectionStatus`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/enums/Charting_Library.ConnectionStatus)

Defines a connection status for the Broker API. If any other value than `1` ("Connected") is returned, the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/account-manager/)
 will display an endless spinner instead of users' trading data. If you want to handle other scenarios, for example when the status is disconnected, you need to manage this scenario within your implementation and use the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.connectionStatusUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#connectionstatusupdate)
 method.

If the method is not implemented or returns a value other than `1`, the following error will appear in the console: _Method connectionStatus is not properly implemented_.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-7 "Direct link to Returns")

[`ConnectionStatus`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/enums/Charting_Library.ConnectionStatus)

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#inherited-from-3 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon)
.[connectionStatus](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon#connectionstatus)

* * *

### editIndividualPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#editindividualpositionbrackets "Direct link to editIndividualPositionBrackets")

▸ **editIndividualPositionBrackets**(`individualPositionId`, `brackets`): `Promise`<`void`\>

This method is called if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportIndividualPositionBrackets](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportindividualpositionbrackets)
 configuration flag is on. It displays a dialog that enables take-profit and stop-loss editing.

Note that the library expects you to call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate)
 method right afterwards.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-5 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `individualPositionId` | `string` | ID of existing individual position to be modified |
| `brackets` | [`Brackets`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Brackets) | new Brackets to be set for the individual position |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-9 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### editPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#editpositionbrackets "Direct link to editPositionBrackets")

▸ **editPositionBrackets**(`positionId`, `brackets`, `customFields?`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `editPositionBrackets` if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPositionBrackets](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpositionbrackets)
 configuration flag is `true`. It shows a dialog that enables take-profit and stop-loss editing.

Note that the library expects you to call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate)
 method right afterwards.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-6 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `positionId` | `string` | The ID of the existing position to be modified. |
| `brackets` | [`Brackets`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Brackets) | New brackets to be set for the position. |
| `customFields?` | [`CustomInputFieldsValues`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.CustomInputFieldsValues) | Custom fields to display in the dialog. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-10 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

 to `true`.

 flag is set to `true`.

### isTradable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#istradable "Direct link to isTradable")

▸ **isTradable**(`symbol`): `Promise`<`boolean` | [`IsTradableResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IsTradableResult)
\>

The library calls `isTradable` to check if a symbol can be traded. If the method returns `false`, users will see the _Non-tradable symbol_ message in the UI when creating orders. You can also display a custom message with the reason why the symbol cannot be traded and the possible solution to resolve the issue. To do this, return an `IsTradableResult` object.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-12 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | The symbol identifier. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-17 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`boolean` | [`IsTradableResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IsTradableResult)
\>

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#inherited-from-11 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon)
.[isTradable](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon#istradable)

* * *

\>

This method is called to receive leverageInfo from the broker.

| Name | Type | Description |

\>
