### modifyOrder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#modifyorder "Direct link to modifyOrder")

▸ **modifyOrder**(`order`, `confirmId?`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `modifyOrder` to request modifying an existing order. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new order state. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.orderUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#orderupdate)
 method right afterwards. Otherwise, the library will return a [timeout issue](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue)
.

Note that [market orders](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/orders#order-types)
 cannot be modified.

To enable an order preview before modification, set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportModifyOrderPreview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmodifyorderpreview)
 configuration flag to `true`. Refer to [Enable order preview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-order-preview)
 for more information.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-14 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `order` | [`Order`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#order) | Order information. |
| `confirmId?` | `string` | The ID of the confirmed order. This parameter is passed if [`supportModifyOrderPreview`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmodifyorderpreview)<br> is `true`. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-19 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### orders[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#orders "Direct link to orders")

▸ **orders**(): `Promise`<[`Order`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#order)
\[\]>

The library calls `orders` to request data on the user's active orders. This data is displayed on the _Orders and Positions_ pages of the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/account-manager/#orders-and-positions)
.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-20 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`Order`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#order)
\[\]>

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#inherited-from-12 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon)
.[orders](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon#orders)

* * *

### ordersHistory[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#ordershistory "Direct link to ordersHistory")

▸ **ordersHistory**(): `Promise`<[`Order`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#order)
\[\]>

The library calls `ordersHistory` to request orders history. It is expected that returned orders will have a final status (`rejected`, `filled`, `cancelled`).

This method is only required when you set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportOrdersHistory](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportordershistory)
 flag to `true`. This flag adds the _History_ page, where order history is displayed, to the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/account-manager/)
. Refer to the [History](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/account-manager/#history)
 section for more information.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-21 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`Order`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#order)
\[\]>

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#inherited-from-13 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon)
.[ordersHistory](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon#ordershistory)

* * *

### placeOrder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#placeorder "Direct link to placeOrder")

▸ **placeOrder**(`order`, `confirmId?`): `Promise`<[`PlaceOrderResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PlaceOrderResult)
\>

The library calls `placeOrder` to request placing an order pre-filled with partial or complete information. You should handle this request on your backend side. For more information, refer to [Order creation](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#1-order-creation)
.

To display an order preview before placing it, set [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPlaceOrderPreview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportplaceorderpreview)
 to `true`. Refer to [Enable order preview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-order-preview)
 for more information.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-15 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `order` | [`PreOrder`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PreOrder) | Order information. |
| `confirmId?` | `string` | The ID of the confirmed order. This parameter is passed if [`supportPlaceOrderPreview`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportplaceorderpreview)<br> is `true`. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-22 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`PlaceOrderResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PlaceOrderResult)
\>

An object with the order ID.

* * *

### positions[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#positions "Direct link to positions")

▸ **positions**(): `Promise`<[`Position`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position)
\[\]>

Called by Trading Platform to request [positions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions)
. Required if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPositions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpositions)
 flag is set to `true`.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-23 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`Position`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Position)
\[\]>

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#inherited-from-14 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon)
.[positions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon#positions)

* * *

### previewLeverage[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#previewleverage "Direct link to previewLeverage")

▸ **previewLeverage**(`leverageSetParams`): `Promise`<[`LeveragePreviewResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LeveragePreviewResult)
\>

This method is called to receive [LeveragePreviewResult](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LeveragePreviewResult)
 object which holds messages about the leverage value set by the user.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-16 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `leverageSetParams` | [`LeverageSetParams`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LeverageSetParams) | `leverageSetParams` is an object similar to leverageInfoParams, but contains an additional `leverage: number` field, which holds the leverage value set by the user. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-24 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`LeveragePreviewResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LeveragePreviewResult)
\>

* * *

### previewOrder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#previeworder "Direct link to previewOrder")

▸ **previewOrder**(`order`): `Promise`<[`OrderPreviewResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OrderPreviewResult)
\>

The library calls `previewOrder` to show an order preview when a user clicks _Buy order_ or _Modify order_ in the Order Ticket. To [enable order preview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-order-preview)
, set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPlaceOrderPreview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportplaceorderpreview)
 or [BrokerConfigFlags.supportModifyOrderPreview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmodifyorderpreview)
 configuration flag to `true`.

This method returns estimated commission, fees, margin, and other information for the order without it actually being placed.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-17 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `order` | [`PreOrder`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PreOrder) | Order information. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-25 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`OrderPreviewResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.OrderPreviewResult)
\>

* * *

### quantityFormatter[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#quantityformatter "Direct link to quantityFormatter")

▸ **quantityFormatter**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.INumberFormatter)
\>

Provide a custom quantity formatter for the specified symbol.

| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | symbol identifier |

`Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.INumberFormatter)
\>

.[quantityFormatter](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon#quantityformatter)

* * *

### reversePosition[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#reverseposition "Direct link to reversePosition")

▸ **reversePosition**(`positionId`): `Promise`<`void`\>

This method is called if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportNativeReversePosition](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportnativereverseposition)
 configuration flag is on. It allows reversing the position by ID.

Note that the library expects you to call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate)
 method right afterwards. Otherwise, the library will return a [timeout issue](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue)
.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-19 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `positionId` | `string` | position |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-27 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### setCurrentAccount[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#setcurrentaccount "Direct link to setCurrentAccount")

▸ **setCurrentAccount**(`id`): `void`

The library calls `setCurrentAccount` when users switch accounts using the drop-down menu in the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/account-manager/)
. This method provides your backend server with the ID of the selected account.

Note that `setCurrentAccount` is required if [accountsMetainfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerAccountInfo#accountsmetainfo)
 returns an array containing more than one element. Refer to [Multiple accounts](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/account-manager/multiple-accounts)
 for more information.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-20 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | [`AccountId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#accountid) | The ID of the selected account. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-28 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#inherited-from-16 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerAccountInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerAccountInfo)
.[setCurrentAccount](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerAccountInfo#setcurrentaccount)

* * *

### setLeverage[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#setleverage "Direct link to setLeverage")

▸ **setLeverage**(`leverageSetParams`): `Promise`<[`LeverageSetResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LeverageSetResult)
\>

This method is called to send user's leverage value to the broker. The value should be verified and corrected on the broker's side if required, and sent back in the response.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-21 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `leverageSetParams` | [`LeverageSetParams`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LeverageSetParams) | `leverageSetParams` is an object similar to leverageInfoParams, but contains an additional `leverage: number` field, which holds the leverage value set by the user. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-29 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`LeverageSetResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LeverageSetResult)
\>

* * *

### spreadFormatter[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#spreadformatter "Direct link to spreadFormatter")

▸ **spreadFormatter**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.INumberFormatter)
\>

Provide a custom spread formatter for the specified symbol.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-22 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | symbol identifier |

\>

* * *

### subscribeEquity[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#subscribeequity "Direct link to subscribeEquity")

▸ **subscribeEquity**(): `void`

The method should be implemented if you use the standard Order Ticket and support stop loss. Equity is used to calculate Risk in Percent.

Once this method is called the broker should provide equity (Balance + P/L) updates via [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.equityUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#equityupdate)
 method.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-31 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### subscribeMarginAvailable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#subscribemarginavailable "Direct link to subscribeMarginAvailable")

▸ **subscribeMarginAvailable**(`symbol`): `void`

The method should be implemented if you use the standard Order Ticket and want to show the margin meter.

Once this method is called the broker should provide margin available updates via [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.marginAvailableUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#marginavailableupdate)
 method.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-23 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | symbol identifier |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-32 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### subscribePipValue[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#subscribepipvalue "Direct link to subscribePipValue")

▸ **subscribePipValue**(`symbol`): `void`

Use this method to enable dynamic updates of the pip value defined in [InstrumentInfo.pipValue](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.InstrumentInfo#pipvalue)
. The pip value is displayed in the _Order info_ section of the [Order Ticket](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/order-ticket)
 and is used to calculate the trade value and risks. Once `subscribePipValue` is called, your Broker API implementation should provide pip value updates through the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.pipValueUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#pipvalueupdate)
 method.

If `subscribePipValue` is not implemented, the static value from [InstrumentInfo.pipValue](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.InstrumentInfo#pipvalue)
 is displayed in the UI.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#parameters-24 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | symbol identifier |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-33 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### symbolInfo[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#symbolinfo "Direct link to symbolInfo")

▸ **symbolInfo**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`InstrumentInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.InstrumentInfo)
\>

The library calls `symbolInfo` to request symbol information for the [Order Ticket](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/order-ticket)
 and [Depth of Market widget](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/depth-of-market)
.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | The symbol identifier. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#returns-34 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`InstrumentInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.InstrumentInfo)
\>

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#inherited-from-18 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon)
.[symbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerCommon#symbolinfo)

* * *

### unsubscribeEquity[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IBrokerTerminal/#unsubscribeequity "Direct link to unsubscribeEquity")

▸ **unsubscribeEquity**(): `void`

The method should be implemented if you use the standard Order Ticket and support stop loss.

Once this method is called the broker should stop providing equity updates.

# Interface: TimescaleMark | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.TimescaleMark

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### color[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#color "Direct link to color")

• **color**: `string`

Color for the timescale mark

* * *

### id[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#id "Direct link to id")

• **id**: `string` | `number`

ID of the timescale mark

* * *

### imageUrl[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#imageurl "Direct link to imageUrl")

• `Optional` **imageUrl**: `string`

Optional URL for an image to be displayed within the timescale mark.

The image should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively.

Examples:

*   `https://s3-symbol-logo.tradingview.com/crypto/XTVCBTC.svg`
*   `/images/myImage.png`
*   `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
*   `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`

* * *

### label[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#label "Direct link to label")

• **label**: `string`

Label for the timescale mark

* * *

### labelFontColor[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#labelfontcolor "Direct link to labelFontColor")

• `Optional` **labelFontColor**: `string`

Color for the timescale mark text label. If undefined then the value provided for `color` will be used.

* * *

### shape[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#shape "Direct link to shape")

• `Optional` **shape**: [`TimeScaleMarkShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#timescalemarkshape)

Shape of the timescale mark

* * *

### showLabelWhenImageLoaded[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#showlabelwhenimageloaded "Direct link to showLabelWhenImageLoaded")

• `Optional` **showLabelWhenImageLoaded**: `boolean`

Continue to show text label even when an image has been loaded for the timescale mark.

Defaults to `false` if undefined.

* * *

### time[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#time "Direct link to time")

• **time**: `number`

Time for the mark. Unix timestamp in seconds.

* * *

### tooltip[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#tooltip "Direct link to tooltip")

• **tooltip**: `string`\[\]

Tooltip content

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#properties)
    *   [color](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#color)
        
    *   [id](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#id)

# Interface: IBrokerWithoutRealtime | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v28**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Broker](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker)
.IBrokerWithoutRealtime

The Broker API is a key component that enables trading. Its main purpose is to connect TradingView charts with your trading logic. Refer to the [Core trading concepts](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/)
 article for more information.

Hierarchy[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#hierarchy "Direct link to Hierarchy")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`IBrokerCommon`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon)
    
*   [`IBrokerAccountInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo)
    
    ↳ **`IBrokerWithoutRealtime`**
    
    ↳↳ [`IBrokerTerminal`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerTerminal)
    

Methods[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#methods "Direct link to Methods")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### accountManagerInfo[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#accountmanagerinfo "Direct link to accountManagerInfo")

▸ **accountManagerInfo**(): [`AccountManagerInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo)

The library calls `accountManagerInfo` to get information required for building the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/)
.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns "Direct link to Returns")

[`AccountManagerInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountManagerInfo)

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon)
.[accountManagerInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#accountmanagerinfo)

* * *

### accountsMetainfo[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#accountsmetainfo "Direct link to accountsMetainfo")

▸ **accountsMetainfo**(): `Promise`<[`AccountMetainfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountMetainfo)
\[\]>

The library calls `accountsMetainfo` to get a list of accounts for a particular user. The method should return an array that contains an ID and name for each account.

Note that if `accountsMetainfo` returns an array containing more than one element, you should implement the [setCurrentAccount](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo#setcurrentaccount)
 method. Refer to [Multiple accounts](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/multiple-accounts)
 for more information.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`AccountMetainfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.AccountMetainfo)
\[\]>

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerAccountInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo)
.[accountsMetainfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo#accountsmetainfo)

* * *

### cancelOrder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#cancelorder "Direct link to cancelOrder")

▸ **cancelOrder**(`orderId`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `cancelOrder` to request canceling an order. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new order state. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.orderUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#orderupdate)
 method right afterwards.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `orderId` | `string` | The ID of the order to cancel. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### cancelOrders[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#cancelorders "Direct link to cancelOrders")

▸ **cancelOrders**(`symbol`, `side`, `ordersIds`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `cancelOrders` to request canceling multiple orders for a symbol. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new order states. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.orderUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#orderupdate)
 method right afterwards.

`cancelOrders` is only called when users click the _CXL all_ button in the [Depth of Market](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/depth-of-market)
 widget.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | The symbol identifier. |
| `side` | [`Side`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/enums/Broker.Side) | An order side. |
| `ordersIds` | `string`\[\] | IDs of the orders to cancel. The orders are selected based on the specified `symbol` and `side`. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### chartContextMenuActions[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#chartcontextmenuactions "Direct link to chartContextMenuActions")

▸ **chartContextMenuActions**(`context`, `options?`): `Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo)
\[\]>

The library calls `chartContextMenuActions` when users open the [context menu](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/context-menu)
 on the chart. This method also renders the _Trade_ button in the context menu.

This method should return an array of [ActionMetaInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo)
 elements, each of them representing one context menu item.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `context` | [`TradeContext`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.TradeContext) | A context object passed by the library. |
| `options?` | [`DefaultContextMenuActionsParams`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.DefaultContextMenuActionsParams) | Default options for the context menu action parameters. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-4 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`ActionMetaInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo)
\[\]>

A promise that resolves to an array of [ActionMetaInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#actionmetainfo)
, which may be empty. In that case, the _Trade_ button will be removed from the context menu.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon)
.[chartContextMenuActions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#chartcontextmenuactions)

* * *

### closeIndividualPosition[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#closeindividualposition "Direct link to closeIndividualPosition")

▸ **closeIndividualPosition**(`individualPositionId`, `amount?`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `closeIndividualPosition` if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportCloseIndividualPosition](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportcloseindividualposition)
 or [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPartialCloseIndividualPosition](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpartialcloseindividualposition)
 configuration flag is `true`. `closeIndividualPosition` allows [closing an individual position](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#close-positions)
 by ID.

Note that the library expects you to call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate)
 method right afterwards. Otherwise, the library will return a [timeout issue](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue)
.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-3 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `individualPositionId` | `string` | The individual position ID. |
| `amount?` | `number` | The amount is specified if `supportPartialCloseIndividualPosition` is `true` and the user wants to close only [part of the individual position](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#partial-closing)<br>. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-5 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### closePosition[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#closeposition "Direct link to closePosition")

▸ **closePosition**(`positionId`, `amount?`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `closePosition` to request [closing a position](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#close-positions)
 by ID. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new position state. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate)
 method right afterwards. Otherwise, the library will return a [timeout issue](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue)
.

`closePosition` is only called if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportClosePosition](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportcloseposition)
 or [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPartialClosePosition](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpartialcloseposition)
 configuration flag is `true`.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-4 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `positionId` | `string` | The position ID. |
| `amount?` | `number` | The amount is specified if [`supportPartialClosePosition`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpartialcloseposition)<br> is `true` and the user wants to close only [part of the position](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions#partial-closing)<br>. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-6 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### connectionStatus[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#connectionstatus "Direct link to connectionStatus")

▸ **connectionStatus**(): [`ConnectionStatus`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/enums/Broker.ConnectionStatus)

Defines a connection status for the Broker API. If any other value than `1` ("Connected") is returned, the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/)
 will display an endless spinner instead of users' trading data. If you want to handle other scenarios, for example when the status is disconnected, you need to manage this scenario within your implementation and use the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.connectionStatusUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#connectionstatusupdate)
 method.

If the method is not implemented or returns a value other than `1`, the following error will appear in the console: _Method connectionStatus is not properly implemented_.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-7 "Direct link to Returns")

[`ConnectionStatus`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/enums/Broker.ConnectionStatus)

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#inherited-from-3 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon)
.[connectionStatus](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#connectionstatus)

* * *

### currentAccount[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#currentaccount "Direct link to currentAccount")

▸ **currentAccount**(): [`AccountId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#accountid)

The library calls `currentAccount` to get the current account's ID.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-8 "Direct link to Returns")

[`AccountId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#accountid)

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#inherited-from-4 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerAccountInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo)
.[currentAccount](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo#currentaccount)

* * *

### editIndividualPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#editindividualpositionbrackets "Direct link to editIndividualPositionBrackets")

▸ **editIndividualPositionBrackets**(`individualPositionId`, `brackets`): `Promise`<`void`\>

This method is called if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportIndividualPositionBrackets](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportindividualpositionbrackets)
 configuration flag is on. It displays a dialog that enables take-profit and stop-loss editing.

Note that the library expects you to call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate)
 method right afterwards.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-5 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `individualPositionId` | `string` | ID of existing individual position to be modified |
| `brackets` | [`Brackets`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.Brackets) | new Brackets to be set for the individual position |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-9 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### editPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#editpositionbrackets "Direct link to editPositionBrackets")

▸ **editPositionBrackets**(`positionId`, `brackets`, `customFields?`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `editPositionBrackets` if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPositionBrackets](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpositionbrackets)
 configuration flag is `true`. It shows a dialog that enables take-profit and stop-loss editing.

Note that the library expects you to call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate)
 method right afterwards.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-6 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `positionId` | `string` | The ID of the existing position to be modified. |
| `brackets` | [`Brackets`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.Brackets) | New brackets to be set for the position. |
| `customFields?` | [`CustomInputFieldsValues`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.CustomInputFieldsValues) | Custom fields to display in the dialog. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-10 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### executions[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#executions "Direct link to executions")

▸ **executions**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`Execution`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.Execution)
\[\]>

The library calls `executions` to request executions for the specified symbol. If you want executions to be displayed on the chart, set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportExecutions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportexecutions)
 to `true`.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | The symbol identifier. |

`Promise`<[`Execution`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.Execution)
\[\]>

 flag is set to `true`.

### isTradable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#istradable "Direct link to isTradable")

▸ **isTradable**(`symbol`): `Promise`<`boolean` | [`IsTradableResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IsTradableResult)
\>

The library calls `isTradable` to check if a symbol can be traded. If the method returns `false`, users will see the _Non-tradable symbol_ message in the UI when creating orders. You can also display a custom message with the reason why the symbol cannot be traded and the possible solution to resolve the issue. To do this, return an `IsTradableResult` object.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-11 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | The symbol identifier. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-17 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`boolean` | [`IsTradableResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IsTradableResult)
\>

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#inherited-from-11 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon)
.[isTradable](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#istradable)

* * *

\>

This method is called to receive leverageInfo from the broker.

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |

\>

* * *

### modifyOrder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#modifyorder "Direct link to modifyOrder")

▸ **modifyOrder**(`order`, `confirmId?`): `Promise`<`void`\>

The library calls `modifyOrder` to request modifying an existing order. You should handle this request on your backend side and provide the library with a new order state. To do this, call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.orderUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#orderupdate)
 method right afterwards. Otherwise, the library will return a [timeout issue](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue)
.

To enable an order preview before modification, set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportModifyOrderPreview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmodifyorderpreview)
 configuration flag to `true`. Refer to [Enable order preview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-order-preview)
 for more information.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-13 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `order` | [`Order`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#order) | Order information. |
| `confirmId?` | `string` | The ID of the confirmed order. This parameter is passed if [`supportModifyOrderPreview`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmodifyorderpreview)<br> is `true`. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-19 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### orders[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#orders "Direct link to orders")

▸ **orders**(): `Promise`<[`Order`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#order)
\[\]>

The library calls `orders` to request data on the user's active orders. This data is displayed on the _Orders and Positions_ pages of the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/#orders-and-positions)
.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-20 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`Order`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#order)
\[\]>

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#inherited-from-12 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon)
.[orders](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#orders)

* * *

### ordersHistory[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#ordershistory "Direct link to ordersHistory")

▸ **ordersHistory**(): `Promise`<[`Order`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#order)
\[\]>

The library calls `ordersHistory` to request orders history. It is expected that returned orders will have a final status (`rejected`, `filled`, `cancelled`).

This method is only required when you set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportOrdersHistory](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportordershistory)
 flag to `true`. This flag adds the _History_ page, where order history is displayed, to the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/)
. Refer to the [History](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/#history)
 section for more information.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-21 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`Order`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#order)
\[\]>

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#inherited-from-13 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon)
.[ordersHistory](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#ordershistory)

* * *

### placeOrder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#placeorder "Direct link to placeOrder")

▸ **placeOrder**(`order`, `confirmId?`): `Promise`<[`PlaceOrderResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.PlaceOrderResult)
\>

The library calls `placeOrder` to request placing an order pre-filled with partial or complete information. You should handle this request on your backend side. For more information, refer to [Order creation](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/#1-order-creation)
.

To display an order preview before placing it, set [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPlaceOrderPreview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportplaceorderpreview)
 to `true`. Refer to [Enable order preview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-order-preview)
 for more information.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-14 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `order` | [`PreOrder`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.PreOrder) | Order information. |
| `confirmId?` | `string` | The ID of the confirmed order. This parameter is passed if [`supportPlaceOrderPreview`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportplaceorderpreview)<br> is `true`. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-22 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`PlaceOrderResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.PlaceOrderResult)
\>

An object with the order ID.

* * *

### positions[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#positions "Direct link to positions")

▸ **positions**(): `Promise`<[`Position`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.Position)
\[\]>

Called by Trading Platform to request [positions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/trading-concepts/positions)
. Required if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPositions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportpositions)
 flag is set to `true`.

`Promise`<[`Position`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.Position)
\[\]>

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon)
.[positions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#positions)

* * *

### previewLeverage[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#previewleverage "Direct link to previewLeverage")

▸ **previewLeverage**(`leverageSetParams`): `Promise`<[`LeveragePreviewResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.LeveragePreviewResult)
\>

This method is called to receive [LeveragePreviewResult](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.LeveragePreviewResult)
 object which holds messages about the leverage value set by the user.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-15 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `leverageSetParams` | [`LeverageSetParams`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.LeverageSetParams) | `leverageSetParams` is an object similar to leverageInfoParams, but contains an additional `leverage: number` field, which holds the leverage value set by the user. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-24 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`LeveragePreviewResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.LeveragePreviewResult)
\>

* * *

### previewOrder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#previeworder "Direct link to previewOrder")

▸ **previewOrder**(`order`): `Promise`<[`OrderPreviewResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderPreviewResult)
\>

The library calls `previewOrder` to show an order preview when a user clicks _Buy order_ or _Modify order_ in the Order Ticket. To [enable order preview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/order-ticket#enable-order-preview)
, set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportPlaceOrderPreview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportplaceorderpreview)
 or [BrokerConfigFlags.supportModifyOrderPreview](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportmodifyorderpreview)
 configuration flag to `true`.

This method returns estimated commission, fees, margin, and other information for the order without it actually being placed.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-16 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `order` | [`PreOrder`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.PreOrder) | Order information. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-25 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`OrderPreviewResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.OrderPreviewResult)
\>

* * *

### quantityFormatter[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#quantityformatter "Direct link to quantityFormatter")

▸ **quantityFormatter**(`symbol`): `Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.INumberFormatter)
\>

Provide a custom quantity formatter for the specified symbol.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-17 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | symbol identifier |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-26 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`INumberFormatter`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.INumberFormatter)
\>

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#inherited-from-15 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerCommon](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon)
.[quantityFormatter](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerCommon#quantityformatter)

* * *

### reversePosition[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#reverseposition "Direct link to reversePosition")

▸ **reversePosition**(`positionId`): `Promise`<`void`\>

This method is called if the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportNativeReversePosition](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.BrokerConfigFlags#supportnativereverseposition)
 configuration flag is on. It allows reversing the position by ID.

Note that the library expects you to call the [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.positionUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#positionupdate)
 method right afterwards. Otherwise, the library will return a [timeout issue](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/common-issues#timeout-issue)
.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-18 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `positionId` | `string` | position |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-27 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<`void`\>

* * *

### setCurrentAccount[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#setcurrentaccount "Direct link to setCurrentAccount")

▸ **setCurrentAccount**(`id`): `void`

The library calls `setCurrentAccount` when users switch accounts using the drop-down menu in the [Account Manager](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/)
. This method provides your backend server with the ID of the selected account.

Note that `setCurrentAccount` is required if [accountsMetainfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo#accountsmetainfo)
 returns an array containing more than one element. Refer to [Multiple accounts](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/account-manager/multiple-accounts)
 for more information.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-19 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `id` | [`AccountId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Broker#accountid) | The ID of the selected account. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-28 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#inherited-from-16 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[IBrokerAccountInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo)
.[setCurrentAccount](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerAccountInfo#setcurrentaccount)

* * *

### setLeverage[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#setleverage "Direct link to setLeverage")

▸ **setLeverage**(`leverageSetParams`): `Promise`<[`LeverageSetResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.LeverageSetResult)
\>

This method is called to send user's leverage value to the broker. The value should be verified and corrected on the broker's side if required, and sent back in the response.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-20 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `leverageSetParams` | [`LeverageSetParams`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.LeverageSetParams) | `leverageSetParams` is an object similar to leverageInfoParams, but contains an additional `leverage: number` field, which holds the leverage value set by the user. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-29 "Direct link to Returns")

`Promise`<[`LeverageSetResult`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.LeverageSetResult)
\>

* * *

▸ **subscribeDOM**(`symbol`): `void`

Library is requesting that realtime DOM (Depth of Market) updates should be supplied for this symbol

| `symbol` | `string` | symbol identifier |

* * *

### subscribeEquity[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#subscribeequity "Direct link to subscribeEquity")

▸ **subscribeEquity**(): `void`

The method should be implemented if you use the standard Order Ticket and support stop loss. Equity is used to calculate Risk in Percent.

Once this method is called the broker should provide equity (Balance + P/L) updates via [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.equityUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#equityupdate)
 method.

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-32 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### subscribeMarginAvailable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#subscribemarginavailable "Direct link to subscribeMarginAvailable")

▸ **subscribeMarginAvailable**(`symbol`): `void`

The method should be implemented if you use the standard Order Ticket and want to show the margin meter.

Once this method is called the broker should provide margin available updates via [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.marginAvailableUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#marginavailableupdate)
 method.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-23 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | symbol identifier |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-33 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### subscribePipValue[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#subscribepipvalue "Direct link to subscribePipValue")

▸ **subscribePipValue**(`symbol`): `void`

The method should be implemented if you use a standard Order Ticket. `pipValues` is displayed in the Order info and it is used to calculate the Trade Value and risks. If this method is not implemented then `pipValue` from the `symbolInfo` is used in the order panel/dialog.

Once this method is called the broker should provide `pipValue` updates via [IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost.pipValueUpdate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerConnectionAdapterHost#pipvalueupdate)
 method.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#parameters-24 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | symbol identifier |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Broker.IBrokerWithoutRealtime/#returns-34 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

\>

 and [Depth of Market widget](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/depth-of-market)
.

| `symbol` | `string` | The symbol identifier. |

\>
