# Interface: IDatafeedChartApi | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.IDatafeedChartApi

Methods[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#methods "Direct link to Methods")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### getBars[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#getbars "Direct link to getBars")

▸ **getBars**(`symbolInfo`, `resolution`, `periodParams`, `onResult`, `onError`): `void`

This function is called when the chart needs a history fragment defined by dates range.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbolInfo` | [`LibrarySymbolInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo) | A SymbolInfo object |
| `resolution` | [`ResolutionString`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring) | Resolution of the symbol |
| `periodParams` | [`PeriodParams`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PeriodParams) | An object used to pass specific requirements for getting bars |
| `onResult` | [`HistoryCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#historycallback) | Callback function for historical data |
| `onError` | [`DatafeedErrorCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#datafeederrorcallback) | Callback function whose only argument is a text error message. If using special characters, please consider `encodeURIComponent`. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### getMarks[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#getmarks "Direct link to getMarks")

▸ **getMarks**(`symbolInfo`, `from`, `to`, `onDataCallback`, `resolution`): `void`

The library calls this function to get marks for visible bars range. The library assumes that you will call `onDataCallback` only once per `getMarks` call.

A few marks per bar are allowed (for now, the maximum is 10). The time of each mark must match the time of a bar. For example, if the bar times are `2023-01-01`, `2023-01-08`, and `2023-01-15`, then a mark cannot have the time `2023-01-05`.

**Remark:** This function will be called only if you confirmed that your back-end is supporting marks ([DatafeedConfiguration.supports\_marks](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DatafeedConfiguration#supports_marks)
).

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbolInfo` | [`LibrarySymbolInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo) | A SymbolInfo object |
| `from` | `number` | Unix timestamp (leftmost visible bar) |
| `to` | `number` | Unix timestamp (rightmost visible bar) |
| `onDataCallback` | [`GetMarksCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#getmarkscallback)<br><[`Mark`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.Mark)<br>\> | Callback function containing an array of marks |
| `resolution` | [`ResolutionString`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring) | Resolution of the symbol |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### getServerTime[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#getservertime "Direct link to getServerTime")

▸ **getServerTime**(`callback`): `void`

This function is called if the `supports_time` configuration flag is `true` when the chart needs to know the server time. The library expects a callback to be called once. The time is provided without milliseconds. Example: `1445324591`.

`getServerTime` is used to display countdown on the price scale. Note that the countdown can be displayed only for [intraday](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-minutes-intraday)
 resolutions.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `callback` | [`ServerTimeCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#servertimecallback) |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### getTimescaleMarks[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#gettimescalemarks "Direct link to getTimescaleMarks")

▸ **getTimescaleMarks**(`symbolInfo`, `from`, `to`, `onDataCallback`, `resolution`): `void`

The library calls this function to get timescale marks for visible bars range. The library assumes that you will call `onDataCallback` only once per `getTimescaleMarks` call.

**Remark:** This function will be called only if you confirmed that your back-end is supporting marks ([DatafeedConfiguration.supports\_timescale\_marks](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DatafeedConfiguration#supports_timescale_marks)
).

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters-3 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbolInfo` | [`LibrarySymbolInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo) | A SymbolInfo object |
| `from` | `number` | Unix timestamp (leftmost visible bar) |
| `to` | `number` | Unix timestamp (rightmost visible bar) |
| `onDataCallback` | [`GetMarksCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#getmarkscallback)<br><[`TimescaleMark`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark)<br>\> | Callback function containing an array of marks |
| `resolution` | [`ResolutionString`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring) | Resolution of the symbol |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns-3 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### getVolumeProfileResolutionForPeriod[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#getvolumeprofileresolutionforperiod "Direct link to getVolumeProfileResolutionForPeriod")

▸ **getVolumeProfileResolutionForPeriod**(`currentResolution`, `from`, `to`, `symbolInfo`): [`ResolutionString`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring)

The library calls this function to get the resolution that will be used to calculate the Volume Profile Visible Range indicator.

Usually you might want to implement this method to calculate the indicator more accurately. The implementation really depends on how much data you can transfer to the library and the depth of data in your data feed. **Remark:** If this function is not provided the library uses currentResolution.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters-4 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `currentResolution` | [`ResolutionString`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring) | Resolution of the symbol |
| `from` | `number` | Unix timestamp (leftmost visible bar) |
| `to` | `number` | Unix timestamp (rightmost visible bar) |
| `symbolInfo` | [`LibrarySymbolInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo) | A Symbol object |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns-4 "Direct link to Returns")

[`ResolutionString`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring)

A resolution

* * *

### resolveSymbol[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#resolvesymbol "Direct link to resolveSymbol")

▸ **resolveSymbol**(`symbolName`, `onResolve`, `onError`, `extension?`): `void`

The library will call this function when it needs to get SymbolInfo by symbol name.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters-5 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbolName` | `string` | Symbol name or `ticker` |
| `onResolve` | [`ResolveCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolvecallback) | Callback function returning a SymbolInfo ([LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)<br>) |
| `onError` | [`DatafeedErrorCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#datafeederrorcallback) | Callback function whose only argument is a text error message |
| `extension?` | [`SymbolResolveExtension`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension) | An optional object with additional parameters |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns-5 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### searchSymbols[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#searchsymbols "Direct link to searchSymbols")

▸ **searchSymbols**(`userInput`, `exchange`, `symbolType`, `onResult`, `searchSource?`): `void`

Provides a list of symbols that match the user's search query.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters-6 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `userInput` | `string` | Text entered by user in the symbol search field |
| `exchange` | `string` | The requested exchange. Empty value means no filter was specified |
| `symbolType` | `string` | Type of symbol. Empty value means no filter was specified |
| `onResult` | [`SearchSymbolsCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#searchsymbolscallback) | Callback function that returns an array of results ([SearchSymbolResultItem](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SearchSymbolResultItem)<br>) or empty array if no symbols found |
| `searchSource?` | [`SearchInitiationPoint`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.SearchInitiationPoint) | The source of the search ([SearchInitiationPoint](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.SearchInitiationPoint)<br>). |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns-6 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### searchSymbolsPaginated[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#searchsymbolspaginated "Direct link to searchSymbolsPaginated")

▸ **searchSymbolsPaginated**(`options`, `onResult`): `void`

Provides a list of symbols that match the user's search query.

Optional. If defined, the library uses this method instead of the non-paginated `searchSymbols`.

Use the `start` property from `options` to determine which "page" of results to return. For example, if the first call returns 50 results with 10 remaining, the second call will have `start` set to `50`. When no more results are available on the server, set the `symbolsRemaining` parameter of the callback to `0`.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters-7 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `options` | [`SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions) | Object containing the user input, exchange, symbol type, and search source ([SearchInitiationPoint](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.SearchInitiationPoint)<br>). |
| `onResult` | [`SearchSymbolsPaginatedCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#searchsymbolspaginatedcallback) | Callback function that returns an array of results ([SearchSymbolResultItem](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SearchSymbolResultItem)<br>) or an empty array if no symbols are found. |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns-7 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### subscribeBars[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#subscribebars "Direct link to subscribeBars")

▸ **subscribeBars**(`symbolInfo`, `resolution`, `onTick`, `listenerGuid`, `onResetCacheNeededCallback`): `void`

The library calls this function when it wants to receive real-time updates for a symbol. The library assumes that you will call the callback provided by the `onTick` parameter every time you want to update the most recent bar or to add a new one.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters-8 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbolInfo` | [`LibrarySymbolInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo) | A SymbolInfo object |
| `resolution` | [`ResolutionString`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring) | Resolution of the symbol |
| `onTick` | [`SubscribeBarsCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#subscribebarscallback) | Callback function returning a Bar object |
| `listenerGuid` | `string` |     |
| `onResetCacheNeededCallback` | () => `void` | Function to be executed when bar data has changed |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns-8 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### subscribeDepth[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#subscribedepth "Direct link to subscribeDepth")

▸ **subscribeDepth**(`symbol`, `callback`): `string`

The library calls this function when it wants to receive real-time symbol data in the [Depth of Market](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/depth-of-market)
 (DOM) widget. Note that you should set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportLevel2Data](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportlevel2data)
 configuration flag to `true`.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters-9 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `symbol` | `string` | A SymbolInfo object |
| `callback` | [`DOMCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#domcallback) | A function returning an object to update DOM data |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns-9 "Direct link to Returns")

`string`

A unique identifier that will be used to unsubscribe from the data

* * *

### unsubscribeBars[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#unsubscribebars "Direct link to unsubscribeBars")

▸ **unsubscribeBars**(`listenerGuid`): `void`

The library calls this function when it doesn't want to receive updates anymore.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters-10 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `listenerGuid` | `string` | id to unsubscribe from |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns-10 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### unsubscribeDepth[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#unsubscribedepth "Direct link to unsubscribeDepth")

▸ **unsubscribeDepth**(`subscriberUID`): `void`

The library calls this function to stop receiving real-time updates for the [Depth of Market](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/trading_terminal/depth-of-market)
 listener. Note that you should set the [BrokerConfigFlags.supportLevel2Data](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.BrokerConfigFlags#supportlevel2data)
 configuration flag to `true`.

#### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#parameters-11 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `subscriberUID` | `string` | A string returned by `subscribeDepth` |

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#returns-11 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

*   [Methods](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#methods)
    *   [getBars](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi/#getbars)

• `Optional` **callWithLast**: `boolean`

**.

.WidgetBarParams

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### datawindow[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#datawindow "Direct link to datawindow")

• `Optional` **datawindow**: `boolean`

Enables data window widget in the widget panel on the right.

**`Default`**

    false

* * *

### details[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#details "Direct link to details")

• `Optional` **details**: `boolean`

Enables details widget in the widget panel on the right.

**`Default`**

    false

* * *

### news[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#news "Direct link to news")

• `Optional` **news**: `boolean`

Enables news widget in the widget panel on the right.

**`Default`**

    false

* * *

### watchlist[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#watchlist "Direct link to watchlist")

• `Optional` **watchlist**: `boolean`

Enables watchlist widget in the widget panel on the right.

**`Default`**

    false

* * *

### watchlist\_settings[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#watchlist_settings "Direct link to watchlist_settings")

• `Optional` **watchlist\_settings**: `Object`

Watchlist settings

#### Type declaration[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#type-declaration "Direct link to Type declaration")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `default_symbols` | `string`\[\] | Sets the list of default symbols for watchlist. Any item in the list which is prefixed with `###` will be considered a section divider in the watchlist. **`Default`** \[\] **Example:** `default_symbols: ['###TOP SECTION', 'AAPL', 'IBM', '###SECOND SECTION', 'MSFT']` |
| `readonly?` | `boolean` | Enables read-only mode for the watchlist **`Default`** `ts false` |

 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.StudyFilledAreaGradientColorStyle

Study filled area gradient styles.

Hierarchy[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFilledAreaGradientColorStyle/#hierarchy "Direct link to Hierarchy")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`StudyFilledAreaStyleBase`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFilledAreaStyleBase)
    
    ↳ **`StudyFilledAreaGradientColorStyle`**
    

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFilledAreaGradientColorStyle/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### bottomColor[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFilledAreaGradientColorStyle/#bottomcolor "Direct link to bottomColor")

• `Optional` **bottomColor**: `string`

Gradient bottom color.

* * *

### bottomValue[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFilledAreaGradientColorStyle/#bottomvalue "Direct link to bottomValue")

• `Optional` **bottomValue**: `number`

Value at which the bottom of the gradient is drawn.

**`Example`**

    75 // Drawn at price = 75

* * *

### fillType[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFilledAreaGradientColorStyle/#filltype "Direct link to fillType")

• **fillType**: `"gradient"`

Gradient fill type

* * *

### topColor[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFilledAreaGradientColorStyle/#topcolor "Direct link to topColor")

• `Optional` **topColor**: `string`

Gradient top color.

* * *

### topValue[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFilledAreaGradientColorStyle/#topvalue "Direct link to topValue")

• `Optional` **topValue**: `number`

Value at which the top of the gradient is drawn.

**`Example`**

    75 // Drawn at price = 75

* * *

### transparency[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFilledAreaGradientColorStyle/#transparency "Direct link to transparency")

• **transparency**: `number`

Filled area's transparency.

**`Min`**

0

**`Max`**

100

 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

# Interface: WatchlistSettings | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v27**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.WatchlistSettings

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### default\_symbols[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#default_symbols "Direct link to default_symbols")

• **default\_symbols**: `string`\[\]

Sets the list of default symbols for watchlist. Any item in the list which is prefixed with `###` will be considered a section divider in the watchlist.

**`Default`**

\[\]

**Example:**

    default_symbols: ['###TOP SECTION', 'AAPL', 'IBM', '###SECOND SECTION', 'MSFT']

* * *

### readonly[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#readonly "Direct link to readonly")

• `Optional` **readonly**: `boolean`

Enables read-only mode for the watchlist.

**`Default`**

    false

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#properties)
    *   [default\_symbols](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#default_symbols)
        
    *   [readonly](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v27/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#readonly)

---

Subscribe for only one update, and remove subscription afterwards. (the callback will be executed only once)

 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.VolumeOscillatorIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Volume Oscillator' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

# Interface: WatermarkLine | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatermarkLine/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v28**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.WatermarkLine

Defines the text and font properties for a line of the watermark.

The default values for sizing and placement are as follows:

*   1st line: fontSize: 96, lineHeight: 117, vertOffset: 0
*   2nd line: fontSize: 48, lineHeight: 58, vertOffset: 5

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatermarkLine/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### fontSize[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatermarkLine/#fontsize "Direct link to fontSize")

• **fontSize**: `number`

Font size to be used (defined in pixels).

* * *

### lineHeight[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatermarkLine/#lineheight "Direct link to lineHeight")

• **lineHeight**: `number`

Line height (defined in pixels).

* * *

### text[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatermarkLine/#text "Direct link to text")

• **text**: `string`

Text to be displayed.

* * *

### vertOffset[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatermarkLine/#vertoffset "Direct link to vertOffset")

• **vertOffset**: `number`

Vertical offset distance (defined in pixels).

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatermarkLine/#properties)
    *   [fontSize](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatermarkLine/#fontsize)
        
    *   [lineHeight](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatermarkLine/#lineheight)
        
    *   [text](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatermarkLine/#text)
        
    *   [vertOffset](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatermarkLine/#vertoffset)

---

# Interface: WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Williams Alligator' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

Indexable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#indexable "Direct link to Indexable")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

▪ \[key: `string`\]: [`StudyOverrideValueType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyoverridevaluetype)

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### jaw.color[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#jawcolor "Direct link to jaw.color")

• **jaw.color**: `string`

Default value: `#2196F3`

* * *

### jaw.display[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#jawdisplay "Direct link to jaw.display")

• **jaw.display**: `number`

Default value: `15`

* * *

### jaw.linestyle[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#jawlinestyle "Direct link to jaw.linestyle")

• **jaw.linestyle**: `number`

Default value: `0`

* * *

### jaw.linewidth[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#jawlinewidth "Direct link to jaw.linewidth")

• **jaw.linewidth**: `number`

Default value: `1`

* * *

### jaw.plottype[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#jawplottype "Direct link to jaw.plottype")

• **jaw.plottype**: [`LineStudyPlotStyleName`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#linestudyplotstylename)

Default value: `line`

* * *

### jaw.trackprice[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#jawtrackprice "Direct link to jaw.trackprice")

• **jaw.trackprice**: `boolean`

Default value: `false`

* * *

### jaw.transparency[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#jawtransparency "Direct link to jaw.transparency")

• **jaw.transparency**: `number`

Default value: `0`

* * *

### lips.color[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#lipscolor "Direct link to lips.color")

• **lips.color**: `string`

Default value: `#66BB6A`

* * *

### lips.display[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#lipsdisplay "Direct link to lips.display")

• **lips.display**: `number`

Default value: `15`

* * *

### lips.linestyle[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#lipslinestyle "Direct link to lips.linestyle")

• **lips.linestyle**: `number`

.VortexIndicatorIndicatorOverrides

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

# Interface: WatchlistSettings | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v28**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.WatchlistSettings

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### default\_symbols[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#default_symbols "Direct link to default_symbols")

• **default\_symbols**: `string`\[\]

Sets the list of default symbols for watchlist. Any item in the list which is prefixed with `###` will be considered a section divider in the watchlist.

**`Default`**

\[\]

**Example:**

    default_symbols: ['###TOP SECTION', 'AAPL', 'IBM', '###SECOND SECTION', 'MSFT']

* * *

### readonly[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#readonly "Direct link to readonly")

• `Optional` **readonly**: `boolean`

Enables read-only mode for the watchlist.

**`Default`**

    false

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#properties)
    *   [default\_symbols](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#default_symbols)
        
    *   [readonly](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings/#readonly)

---

# Interface: VolumeProfileVisibleRangeIndicatorOverrides | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.VolumeProfileVisibleRangeIndicatorOverrides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.VolumeProfileVisibleRangeIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Volume Profile Visible Range' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

Indexable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.VolumeProfileVisibleRangeIndicatorOverrides/#indexable "Direct link to Indexable")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

▪ \[key: `string`\]: [`StudyOverrideValueType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyoverridevaluetype)

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.VolumeProfileVisibleRangeIndicatorOverrides/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

# Interface: SymbolSpecificTradingOptions | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Broker](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker)
.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### allowedDurations[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#alloweddurations "Direct link to allowedDurations")

• `Optional` **allowedDurations**: `string`\[\]

Array of strings with valid duration values. You can check that in Order Ticket.

* * *

### allowedOrderTypes[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#allowedordertypes "Direct link to allowedOrderTypes")

• `Optional` **allowedOrderTypes**: [`OrderType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Broker.OrderType)
\[\]

Supported order types for the instrument.

* * *

### supportAddBracketsToExistingOrder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportaddbracketstoexistingorder "Direct link to supportAddBracketsToExistingOrder")

• `Optional` **supportAddBracketsToExistingOrder**: `boolean`

Using this flag you can disable adding brackets to the existing order.

* * *

### supportBracketsInPips[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportbracketsinpips "Direct link to supportBracketsInPips")

• `Optional` **supportBracketsInPips**: `boolean`

Whether brackets could be set in ticks/pips.

**`Default`**

    true

* * *

### supportIndividualPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportindividualpositionbrackets "Direct link to supportIndividualPositionBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportIndividualPositionBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether trade brackets are supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### supportModifyBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportmodifybrackets "Direct link to supportModifyBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportModifyBrackets**: `boolean`

Using this flag you can disable existing order's brackets modification. If you set it to `false`, additional fields will be disabled in Order Ticket on the chart,

* * *

### supportModifyOrderBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportmodifyorderbrackets "Direct link to supportModifyOrderBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportModifyOrderBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether the integration supports the modification of existing order brackets.

* * *

### supportModifyPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportmodifypositionbrackets "Direct link to supportModifyPositionBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportModifyPositionBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether the integration supports the modification of existing position brackets.

* * *

### supportOrderBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportorderbrackets "Direct link to supportOrderBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportOrderBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether order brackets are supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### supportPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportpositionbrackets "Direct link to supportPositionBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportPositionBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether position brackets are supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### supportReversePosition[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportreverseposition "Direct link to supportReversePosition")

• `Optional` **supportReversePosition**: `boolean`

Whether position reversing is supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### supportRiskControlsAndInfo[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportriskcontrolsandinfo "Direct link to supportRiskControlsAndInfo")

• `Optional` **supportRiskControlsAndInfo**: `boolean`

Whether the risk controls and order info should be shown.

* * *

### warningMessage[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#warningmessage "Direct link to warningMessage")

• `Optional` **warningMessage**: `string`

A symbol-specific message that can be used to warn users.

    *   [allowedDurations](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#alloweddurations)

.TableFormatterInputs

Type parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `T` | extends [`TableFormatterInputValues`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#tableformatterinputvalues)<br> = [`TableFormatterInputValues`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Broker#tableformatterinputvalues) |

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### prevValues[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#prevvalues "Direct link to prevValues")

• `Optional` **prevValues**: `Partial`<`T` extends \[...args: A\[\]\] ? \[...A\[\]\] : `never`\>

Optional field. It is array of previous values so you can compare and format accordingly. It exists if current column has the `highlightDiff: true` key.

* * *

### values[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#values "Direct link to values")

• **values**: `T` extends \[...args: A\[\]\] ? \[...A\[\]\] : `never`

Array of values to be formatted. Values are obtained by extracting dependent properties from the data object.

*   [Type parameters](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#type-parameters)

# Interface: RankCorrelationIndexIndicatorOverrides | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RankCorrelationIndexIndicatorOverrides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.RankCorrelationIndexIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Rank Correlation Index' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

Indexable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RankCorrelationIndexIndicatorOverrides/#indexable "Direct link to Indexable")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

▪ \[key: `string`\]: [`StudyOverrideValueType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyoverridevaluetype)

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RankCorrelationIndexIndicatorOverrides/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### rci.color[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RankCorrelationIndexIndicatorOverrides/#rcicolor "Direct link to rci.color")

• **rci.color**: `string`

Default value: `#2196F3`

* * *

• **rci.display**: `number`

Default value: `15`

* * *

* * *

• **rci.trackprice**: `boolean`

Default value: `false`

* * *

### rci.transparency[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RankCorrelationIndexIndicatorOverrides/#rcitransparency "Direct link to rci.transparency")

• **rci.transparency**: `number`

Default value: `0`

* * *

### zero line.color[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RankCorrelationIndexIndicatorOverrides/#zero-linecolor "Direct link to zero line.color")

• **zero line.color**: `string`

Default value: `#787B86`

* * *

### zero line.linestyle[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RankCorrelationIndexIndicatorOverrides/#zero-linelinestyle "Direct link to zero line.linestyle")

• **zero line.linestyle**: `number`

• **zero line.visible**: `boolean`

Default value: `true`

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.StudyStyleInfoDefaults

Default study style preferences.

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStyleInfoDefaults/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### bands[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStyleInfoDefaults/#bands "Direct link to bands")

• `Optional` **bands**: readonly [`StudyBandStyle`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyBandStyle)
\[\]

Default study bands style preferences.

* * *

### filledAreasStyle[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStyleInfoDefaults/#filledareasstyle "Direct link to filledAreasStyle")

• `Optional` **filledAreasStyle**: `Record`<`string`, [`StudyFilledAreaStyle`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyfilledareastyle)
\>

Default study filled areas style preferences.

* * *

### graphics[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStyleInfoDefaults/#graphics "Direct link to graphics")

• `Optional` **graphics**: `Object`

Default study graphics style preferences.

#### Type declaration[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStyleInfoDefaults/#type-declaration "Direct link to Type declaration")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `hhists?` | `Record`<`string`, [`HHistPreferences`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.HHistPreferences)<br>\> | Histogram style preferences |
| `horizlines?` | `Record`<`string`, [`HorizLinePreferences`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.HorizLinePreferences)<br>\> | Horizontal line style preferences |
| `polygons?` | `Record`<`string`, [`PolygonPreferences`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PolygonPreferences)<br>\> | Polygon style preferences |
| `vertlines?` | `Record`<`string`, [`VertLinePreferences`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.VertLinePreferences)<br>\> | Vertical line style preferences |

* * *

### ohlcPlots[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStyleInfoDefaults/#ohlcplots "Direct link to ohlcPlots")

• `Optional` **ohlcPlots**: `Record`<`string`, [`StudyOhlcPlotPreferences`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyohlcplotpreferences)
\>

Default study OHLC plot style preferences.

* * *

### palettes[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStyleInfoDefaults/#palettes "Direct link to palettes")

• `Optional` **palettes**: `Record`<`string`, [`StudyPaletteStyle`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyPaletteStyle)
\>

Default study paletes style preferences.

* * *

### styles[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStyleInfoDefaults/#styles "Direct link to styles")

• `Optional` **styles**: `Record`<`string`, [`StudyPlotPreferences`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyplotpreferences)
\>

Default study styles.

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStyleInfoDefaults/#properties)
    *   [bands](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStyleInfoDefaults/#bands)

# Interface: SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'SuperTrend' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

Indexable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#indexable "Direct link to Indexable")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

▪ \[key: `string`\]: [`StudyOverrideValueType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyoverridevaluetype)

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### down arrow.color[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#down-arrowcolor "Direct link to down arrow.color")

• **down arrow.color**: `string`

Default value: `#FF0000`

* * *

### down arrow.display[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#down-arrowdisplay "Direct link to down arrow.display")

• **down arrow.display**: `number`

Default value: `15`

* * *

• **down arrow.linestyle**: `number`

Default value: `0`

* * *

• **down arrow.linewidth**: `number`

Default value: `3`

* * *

• **down arrow.location**: `string`

Default value: `AboveBar`

* * *

### down arrow.plottype[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#down-arrowplottype "Direct link to down arrow.plottype")

• **down arrow.plottype**: `string`

Default value: `shape_arrow_down`

* * *

• **down arrow.trackprice**: `boolean`

Default value: `false`

* * *

### down arrow.transparency[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#down-arrowtransparency "Direct link to down arrow.transparency")

• **down arrow.transparency**: `number`

Default value: `35`

* * *

### supertrend.color[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#supertrendcolor "Direct link to supertrend.color")

• **supertrend.color**: `string`

Default value: `#000080`

* * *

### supertrend.display[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#supertrenddisplay "Direct link to supertrend.display")

• **supertrend.display**: `number`

* * *

• **supertrend.linewidth**: `number`

Default value: `3`

* * *

### supertrend.plottype[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#supertrendplottype "Direct link to supertrend.plottype")

• **supertrend.plottype**: [`LineStudyPlotStyleName`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#linestudyplotstylename)

* * *

* * *

# Interface: PriceVolumeTrendIndicatorOverrides | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PriceVolumeTrendIndicatorOverrides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.PriceVolumeTrendIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Price Volume Trend' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

Indexable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PriceVolumeTrendIndicatorOverrides/#indexable "Direct link to Indexable")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

▪ \[key: `string`\]: [`StudyOverrideValueType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyoverridevaluetype)

# Interface: SymbolResolveExtension | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.SymbolResolveExtension

Additional information about the Symbol's currency or unit

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### currencyCode[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#currencycode "Direct link to currencyCode")

• `Optional` **currencyCode**: `string`

Indicates the currency for conversions if `currency_codes` configuration field is set, and `currency_code` is provided in the original symbol information ([LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
). Read more about [currency conversion](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Price-Scale#enable-currency-conversion)
.

* * *

### session[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#session "Direct link to session")

• `Optional` **session**: `string`

Trading session type, such as `"regular"` or `"extended"`, that the chart should currently display.

* * *

### unitId[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#unitid "Direct link to unitId")

• `Optional` **unitId**: `string`

Indicates the unit for conversion if `units` configuration field is set and `unit_id` is provided in the original symbol information ([LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
).

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#properties)
    *   [currencyCode](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#currencycode)
        
    *   [session](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#session)
        
    *   [unitId](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#unitid)

---

.QuoteDataResponse

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

    ↳ [`QuoteErrorData`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.QuoteErrorData)

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.QuoteDataResponse/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### n[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.QuoteDataResponse/#n "Direct link to n")

• **n**: `string`

Symbol name. This value must be **exactly the same** as in the request

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.TableFormatterInputs

Type parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `T` | extends [`TableFormatterInputValues`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#tableformatterinputvalues)<br> = [`TableFormatterInputValues`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#tableformatterinputvalues) |

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### prevValues[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#prevvalues "Direct link to prevValues")

• `Optional` **prevValues**: `Partial`<`T` extends \[...args: A\[\]\] ? \[...A\[\]\] : `never`\>

Optional field. It is array of previous values so you can compare and format accordingly. It exists if current column has the `highlightDiff: true` key.

* * *

### values[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#values "Direct link to values")

• **values**: `T` extends \[...args: A\[\]\] ? \[...A\[\]\] : `never`

Array of values to be formatted. Values are obtained by extracting dependent properties from the data object.

*   [Type parameters](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#type-parameters)

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFieldDescriptor/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.StudyFieldDescriptor

Description of a study field. This is used when `includedStudies: true` is defined in `exportData`.

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFieldDescriptor/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### plotTitle[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFieldDescriptor/#plottitle "Direct link to plotTitle")

• **plotTitle**: `string`

The title of the source plot.

* * *

### sourceId[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFieldDescriptor/#sourceid "Direct link to sourceId")

• **sourceId**: `string`

The ID of the source study.

* * *

### sourceTitle[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFieldDescriptor/#sourcetitle "Direct link to sourceTitle")

• **sourceTitle**: `string`

The title of the source study.

* * *

### sourceType[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFieldDescriptor/#sourcetype "Direct link to sourceType")

• **sourceType**: `"study"`

Source type is a `study`

* * *

### type[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFieldDescriptor/#type "Direct link to type")

• **type**: `"value"`

Type is a `value`

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFieldDescriptor/#properties)
    *   [plotTitle](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFieldDescriptor/#plottitle)
        
    *   [sourceId](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFieldDescriptor/#sourceid)

# Interface: SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Datafeed)
.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions

Options that define paginated [Symbol Search](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Symbol-Search)
 requests.

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### exchange[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#exchange "Direct link to exchange")

• **exchange**: `string`

The requested exchange. An empty value means no filter is specified.

* * *

### searchSource[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#searchsource "Direct link to searchSource")

• `Optional` **searchSource**: [`SearchInitiationPoint`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Datafeed.SearchInitiationPoint)

The source of the search ([SearchInitiationPoint](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Datafeed.SearchInitiationPoint)
).

* * *

### start[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#start "Direct link to start")

• `Optional` **start**: `number`

The number of results to skip, representing how many have already been returned for the same input.

* * *

### symbolType[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#symboltype "Direct link to symbolType")

• **symbolType**: `string`

Type of symbol. An empty value means no filter is specified.

* * *

### userInput[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#userinput "Direct link to userInput")

• **userInput**: `string`

Text entered by the user in the Symbol Search field.

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#properties)
    *   [exchange](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#exchange)
        
    *   [searchSource](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#searchsource)
        
    *   [start](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#start)
        
    *   [symbolType](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#symboltype)
        
    *   [userInput](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#userinput)

# Interface: WidgetBarParams | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.WidgetBarParams

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### datawindow[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#datawindow "Direct link to datawindow")

• `Optional` **datawindow**: `boolean`

Enables data window widget in the widget panel on the right.

**`Default`**

    false

* * *

### details[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#details "Direct link to details")

• `Optional` **details**: `boolean`

Enables details widget in the widget panel on the right.

**`Default`**

    false

* * *

### news[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#news "Direct link to news")

• `Optional` **news**: `boolean`

Enables news widget in the widget panel on the right.

**`Default`**

    false

* * *

### watchlist[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#watchlist "Direct link to watchlist")

• `Optional` **watchlist**: `boolean`

Enables watchlist widget in the widget panel on the right.

**`Default`**

    false

* * *

### watchlist\_settings[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WidgetBarParams/#watchlist_settings "Direct link to watchlist_settings")

• `Optional` **watchlist\_settings**: [`WatchlistSettings`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchlistSettings)

Watchlist settings

# Interface: StudyStylesInfo | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.StudyStylesInfo

Study styles description.

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### char[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#char "Direct link to char")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **char**: `string`

Char to display with the plot. Applicable only to chars plot types.

* * *

### forceOverlay[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#forceoverlay "Direct link to forceOverlay")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **forceOverlay**: `boolean`

optional flag to indicate the plot should be drawn on main page

* * *

### format[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#format "Direct link to format")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **format**: `Partial`<[`StudyPlotValuePrecisionFormat`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyPlotValuePrecisionFormat)
\>

Info about the Price Scale formatting

* * *

### histogramBase[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#histogrambase "Direct link to histogramBase")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **histogramBase**: `number`

Histogram base. A price value which will be considered as a start base point when rendering plot with histogram, columns or area style.

* * *

### isHidden[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#ishidden "Direct link to isHidden")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **isHidden**: `boolean`

If `true` then the styles tab will be hidden in the study dialog.

* * *

### joinPoints[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#joinpoints "Direct link to joinPoints")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **joinPoints**: `boolean`

If `true` then plot points will be joined with line, applicable only to `Circles` and `Cross` type plots. Default is false.

* * *

### maxHeight[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#maxheight "Direct link to maxHeight")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **maxHeight**: `number`

Maximum possible plot arrow size. Applicable only to arrow plot types.

* * *

### minHeight[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#minheight "Direct link to minHeight")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **minHeight**: `number`

Minimum possible plot arrow size. Applicable only to arrow plot types.

* * *

### showLast[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#showlast "Direct link to showLast")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **showLast**: `number`

If defined, defines the number of bars to plot on chart.

* * *

### size[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#size "Direct link to size")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **size**: `number` | [`PlotSymbolSize`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.PlotSymbolSize)

Size of characters on the chart. Possible values are: `auto`, `tiny`, `small`, `normal`, `large`,`huge`. Applicable to `chars` and `shapes` plot types.

* * *

### text[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#text "Direct link to text")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **text**: `string`

Text to display with the plot. Applicable to `chars` and `shapes` plot types.

* * *

### title[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#title "Direct link to title")

• `Readonly` **title**: `string`

Title used in the study dialog styles tab.

* * *

### zorder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#zorder "Direct link to zorder")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **zorder**: `number`

Used to control the zorder of the plot. Control if a plot is visually behind or in front of another.

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#properties)
    *   [char](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#char)
        
    *   [forceOverlay](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#forceoverlay)

    *   [histogramBase](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#histogrambase)
        
    *   [isHidden](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#ishidden)
        
    *   [joinPoints](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo/#joinpoints)
