# Interface: SymbolSpecificTradingOptions | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### allowedDurations[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#alloweddurations "Direct link to allowedDurations")

• `Optional` **allowedDurations**: `string`\[\]

Array of strings with valid duration values. You can check that in Order Ticket.

* * *

### allowedOrderTypes[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#allowedordertypes "Direct link to allowedOrderTypes")

• `Optional` **allowedOrderTypes**: [`OrderType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.OrderType)
\[\]

Supported order types for the instrument.

* * *

### supportAddBracketsToExistingOrder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportaddbracketstoexistingorder "Direct link to supportAddBracketsToExistingOrder")

• `Optional` **supportAddBracketsToExistingOrder**: `boolean`

Using this flag you can disable adding brackets to the existing order.

* * *

### supportBracketsInPips[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportbracketsinpips "Direct link to supportBracketsInPips")

• `Optional` **supportBracketsInPips**: `boolean`

Whether brackets could be set in ticks/pips.

**`Default`**

    true

* * *

### supportIndividualPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportindividualpositionbrackets "Direct link to supportIndividualPositionBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportIndividualPositionBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether trade brackets are supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### supportModifyBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportmodifybrackets "Direct link to supportModifyBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportModifyBrackets**: `boolean`

Using this flag you can disable existing order's brackets modification. If you set it to `false`, additional fields will be disabled in Order Ticket on the chart,

* * *

### supportModifyOrderBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportmodifyorderbrackets "Direct link to supportModifyOrderBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportModifyOrderBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether the integration supports the modification of existing order brackets.

* * *

### supportModifyPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportmodifypositionbrackets "Direct link to supportModifyPositionBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportModifyPositionBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether the integration supports the modification of existing position brackets.

* * *

### supportOrderBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportorderbrackets "Direct link to supportOrderBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportOrderBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether order brackets are supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### supportPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportpositionbrackets "Direct link to supportPositionBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportPositionBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether position brackets are supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### supportReversePosition[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportreverseposition "Direct link to supportReversePosition")

• `Optional` **supportReversePosition**: `boolean`

Whether position reversing is supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### supportRiskControlsAndInfo[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportriskcontrolsandinfo "Direct link to supportRiskControlsAndInfo")

• `Optional` **supportRiskControlsAndInfo**: `boolean`

Whether the risk controls and order info should be shown.

* * *

### warningMessage[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#warningmessage "Direct link to warningMessage")

• `Optional` **warningMessage**: `string`

A symbol-specific message that can be used to warn users.

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#properties)
    *   [allowedDurations](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#alloweddurations)
        
    *   [allowedOrderTypes](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#allowedordertypes)

# Interface: SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions

Options that define paginated [Symbol Search](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Symbol-Search)
 requests.

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### exchange[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#exchange "Direct link to exchange")

• **exchange**: `string`

The requested exchange. An empty value means no filter is specified.

* * *

### searchSource[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#searchsource "Direct link to searchSource")

• `Optional` **searchSource**: [`SearchInitiationPoint`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.SearchInitiationPoint)

The source of the search ([SearchInitiationPoint](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.SearchInitiationPoint)
).

* * *

### start[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#start "Direct link to start")

• `Optional` **start**: `number`

The number of results to skip, representing how many have already been returned for the same input.

* * *

### symbolType[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#symboltype "Direct link to symbolType")

• **symbolType**: `string`

Type of symbol. An empty value means no filter is specified.

* * *

### userInput[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#userinput "Direct link to userInput")

• **userInput**: `string`

Text entered by the user in the Symbol Search field.

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#properties)
    *   [exchange](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#exchange)
        
    *   [searchSource](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#searchsource)
        
    *   [start](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#start)
        
    *   [symbolType](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#symboltype)
        
    *   [userInput](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolSearchPaginatedOptions/#userinput)

# Interface: SymbolResolveExtension | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Datafeed)
.SymbolResolveExtension

Additional information about the Symbol's currency or unit

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### currencyCode[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#currencycode "Direct link to currencyCode")

• `Optional` **currencyCode**: `string`

Indicates the currency for conversions if `currency_codes` configuration field is set, and `currency_code` is provided in the original symbol information ([LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo)
). Read more about [currency conversion](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Price-Scale#enable-currency-conversion)
.

* * *

### session[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#session "Direct link to session")

• `Optional` **session**: `string`

Trading session type, such as `"regular"` or `"extended"`, that the chart should currently display.

* * *

### unitId[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#unitid "Direct link to unitId")

• `Optional` **unitId**: `string`

Indicates the unit for conversion if `units` configuration field is set and `unit_id` is provided in the original symbol information ([LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo)
).

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#properties)
    *   [currencyCode](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#currencycode)
        
    *   [session](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#session)
        
    *   [unitId](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#unitid)

---

### cutFractionalByPrecision[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#cutfractionalbyprecision "Direct link to cutFractionalByPrecision")

• `Optional` **cutFractionalByPrecision**: `boolean`

Cuts price by priceScalePrecision, without rounding

* * *

### ignoreLocaleNumberFormat[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#ignorelocalenumberformat "Direct link to ignoreLocaleNumberFormat")

• `Optional` **ignoreLocaleNumberFormat**: `boolean`

Ignore locale number format settings like decimal/thousands separators

• `Optional` **noExponentialForm**: `boolean`

• `Optional` **removeAllEndingZeros**: `boolean`

# Interface: WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides | Advanced Charts Documentation

This is documentation for **v28**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Williams Alligator' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

Indexable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsAlligatorIndicatorOverrides/#indexable "Direct link to Indexable")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

Default value: `#2196F3`

Add `tailSize` digits to fractional part of result string

### useRtlFormat[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#usertlformat "Direct link to useRtlFormat")

• `Optional` **useRtlFormat**: `boolean`

Use Right to left format

*   [Hierarchy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#hierarchy)
    
*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#properties)
    *   [cutFractionalByPrecision](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#cutfractionalbyprecision)
        
    *   [ignoreLocaleNumberFormat](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#ignorelocalenumberformat)
        
    *   [noExponentialForm](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#noexponentialform)
        
    *   [removeAllEndingZeros](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#removeallendingzeros)
        
    *   [signNegative](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#signnegative)
        
    *   [signPositive](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#signpositive)
        
    *   [tailSize](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Broker.PriceFormatterFormatOptions/#tailsize)

# Interface: RawStudyMetaInfoBase | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.RawStudyMetaInfoBase

Hierarchy[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#hierarchy "Direct link to Hierarchy")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   **`RawStudyMetaInfoBase`**
    
    ↳ [`RawStudyMetaInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfo)
    

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### \_metainfoVersion[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#_metainfoversion "Direct link to _metainfoVersion")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **\_metainfoVersion**: `number`

Metainfo version of the study. The current version is 53, and the default one is 0.

* * *

### bands[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#bands "Direct link to bands")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **bands**: readonly `Readonly`<[`StudyBandInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyBandInfo)
\>\[\]

Bands

* * *

### behind\_chart[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#behind_chart "Direct link to behind_chart")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **behind\_chart**: `boolean`

Define should be study on series level or not

* * *

### defaults[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#defaults "Direct link to defaults")

• `Readonly` **defaults**: `Readonly`<`Partial`<[`StudyDefaults`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyDefaults)
\>>

an object containing settings that are applied when user clicks 'Apply Defaults'. See dedicated article: [Custom Studies Defaults](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/custom_studies/metainfo/Custom-Studies-Defaults)

* * *

### description[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#description "Direct link to description")

• `Readonly` **description**: `string`

Description of the study. It will be displayed in the Indicators window and will be used as a name argument when calling the createStudy method

* * *

### filledAreas[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#filledareas "Direct link to filledAreas")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **filledAreas**: readonly `Readonly`<[`StudyFilledAreaInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyFilledAreaInfo)
\>\[\]

Filled area is a special object, which allows coloring an area between two plots or hlines. Please note, that it is impossible to fill the area between a band and a hline.

* * *

### financialPeriod[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#financialperiod "Direct link to financialPeriod")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **financialPeriod**: [`FinancialPeriod`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#financialperiod)

Financial Period

* * *

### format[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#format "Direct link to format")

• `Readonly` **format**: [`StudyPlotValueFormat`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyplotvalueformat)

A type of data that an indicator displays, such as `volume` or `price`. Values on the [Price Scale](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Price-Scale)
 depend on this data type. Additionally, you can adjust a precision of indicator values. To do this, specify the `precision` property in [StudyPlotValuePrecisionFormat](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyPlotValuePrecisionFormat)
.

For more information about `format`, refer to the [Metainfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/custom_studies/metainfo/)
 article.

* * *

### groupingKey[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#groupingkey "Direct link to groupingKey")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **groupingKey**: `string`

Key for grouping studies

* * *

### inputs[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#inputs "Direct link to inputs")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **inputs**: [`StudyInputInfoList`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyinputinfolist)

An array of objects that represent input parameters. For more information, refer to the [Inputs](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/custom_studies/metainfo/Custom-Studies-Inputs)
 article.

* * *

### isCustomIndicator[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#iscustomindicator "Direct link to isCustomIndicator")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **isCustomIndicator**: `boolean`

should be `true` in Custom Study

* * *

### is\_hidden\_study[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#is_hidden_study "Direct link to is_hidden_study")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **is\_hidden\_study**: `boolean`

Whether the study should appear in Indicators list.

* * *

### is\_price\_study[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#is_price_study "Direct link to is_price_study")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **is\_price\_study**: `boolean`

Whether the study should appear on the main series pane

* * *

### linkedToSeries[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#linkedtoseries "Direct link to linkedToSeries")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **linkedToSeries**: `boolean`

Whether the study price scale should be the same as the main series one.

* * *

### name[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#name "Direct link to name")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **name**: `string`

Name for the study

* * *

### ohlcPlots[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#ohlcplots "Direct link to ohlcPlots")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **ohlcPlots**: [`MappedObject`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.MappedObject)
<`Readonly`<[`StudyOhlcStylesInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyOhlcStylesInfo)
\>>

array with study plots info. See dedicated article: [Custom Studies OHLC Plots](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/custom_studies/Custom-Studies-OHLC-Plots)

* * *

### palettes[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#palettes "Direct link to palettes")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **palettes**: [`MappedObject`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.MappedObject)
<`Readonly`<[`StudyPalettesInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyPalettesInfo)
\>>

definitions of palettes that are used in plots and defaults. Palettes allows you use different styles (not only colors) for each line point.

This object contains palette names as keys, and palette info as values: `[palette.name]: { colors, valToIndex, addDefaultColor }`, where

*   `colors`\* - an object `{ [color_id]: { name: 'name' }}`, where name is a string that will appear on Style tab of study properties dialog.
*   `valToIndex` - an object, the mapping between the values that are returned by the script and palette colors.
*   `addDefaultColor` - boolean, if true the defaults are used for colorer type plot, when its value is null or undefined.

* * *

### plots[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#plots "Direct link to plots")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **plots**: readonly `Readonly`<[`StudyPlotInformation`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyplotinformation)
\>\[\]

array with study plots info. See dedicated article: [Custom Studies Plots](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/custom_studies/Custom-Studies-Plots)

* * *

### precision[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#precision "Direct link to precision")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **precision**: `string` | `number`

Use [format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfo#format)
 instead.

**`Deprecated`**

* * *

### priceScale[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#pricescale "Direct link to priceScale")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **priceScale**: [`StudyTargetPriceScale`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.StudyTargetPriceScale)

Price scale to use for the study

* * *

### shortDescription[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#shortdescription "Direct link to shortDescription")

• `Readonly` **shortDescription**: `string`

Short description of the study. Will be displayed on the chart

* * *

### styles[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.RawStudyMetaInfoBase/#styles "Direct link to styles")

• `Optional` `Readonly` **styles**: [`MappedObject`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.MappedObject)
<`Readonly`<[`StudyStylesInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyStylesInfo)
\>>

an object with plot id as keys and style info as values.

* * *

# Interface: SubscribeEventsMap | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.SubscribeEventsMap

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### activeChartChanged[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#activechartchanged "Direct link to activeChartChanged")

• **activeChartChanged**: (`chartIndex`: `number`) => `void`

#### Type declaration[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#type-declaration "Direct link to Type declaration")

▸ (`chartIndex`): `void`

Active chart has changed. **Note:** this event is only applicable to Trading Platform.

##### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#parameters "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `chartIndex` | `number` | index of the active chart |

##### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### add\_compare[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#add_compare "Direct link to add_compare")

• **add\_compare**: [`EmptyCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#emptycallback)

A compare instrument has been added

* * *

### chart\_load\_requested[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#chart_load_requested "Direct link to chart_load_requested")

• **chart\_load\_requested**: (`savedData`: `object`) => `void`

#### Type declaration[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#type-declaration-1 "Direct link to Type declaration")

▸ (`savedData`): `void`

New chart is about to be loaded

##### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#parameters-1 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `savedData` | `object` | chart data about to be loaded |

##### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#returns-1 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### chart\_loaded[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#chart_loaded "Direct link to chart_loaded")

• **chart\_loaded**: [`EmptyCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#emptycallback)

Chart has finished loading

* * *

### chart\_theme\_changed[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#chart_theme_changed "Direct link to chart_theme_changed")

• **chart\_theme\_changed**: (`themeName`: `string`, `isStandardTheme`: `boolean`, `onlyActiveChart`: `boolean`) => `void`

#### Type declaration[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#type-declaration-2 "Direct link to Type declaration")

▸ (`themeName`, `isStandardTheme`, `onlyActiveChart`): `void`

Fired when the chart theme is updated. This event can be triggered in several ways:

*   When a user applies a saved [chart template](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/saving_loading/#chart-templates)
    .
*   When the chart theme is reset to the default settings. (`isStandardTheme` parameter will be true)
*   When the [IChartingLibraryWidget.changeTheme](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#changetheme)
     method is called.
*   When the [IChartWidgetApi.loadChartTemplate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#loadcharttemplate)
     method is called.

##### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `themeName` | `string` | The name of the chart theme or template that was applied. |
| `isStandardTheme` | `boolean` | `true` if the default built-in theme was restored; `false`, if a custom chart template was applied. |
| `onlyActiveChart` | `boolean` | `false`, when applied to all charts |

##### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### compare\_add[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#compare_add "Direct link to compare_add")

• **compare\_add**: [`EmptyCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#emptycallback)

A compare dialog has been shown

* * *

### dragend[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#dragend "Direct link to dragend")

• **dragend**: [`EmptyCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#emptycallback)

Drag end

* * *

* * *

### drawing[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#drawing "Direct link to drawing")

) => `void`

| --- | --- | --- |

### drawing\_event[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#drawing_event "Direct link to drawing_event")

• **drawing\_event**: (`sourceId`: [`EntityId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#entityid)
, `drawingEventType`: [`DrawingEventType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#drawingeventtype)
) => `void`

#### Type declaration[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#type-declaration-5 "Direct link to Type declaration")

▸ (`sourceId`, `drawingEventType`): `void`

Drawing was hidden, shown, moved, removed, clicked, or created

##### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#parameters-5 "Direct link to Parameters")

### onAutoSaveNeeded[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#onautosaveneeded "Direct link to onAutoSaveNeeded")

• **onAutoSaveNeeded**: [`EmptyCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#emptycallback)

User has changed the chart. `Chart change` means any user action that can be undone. The callback function will not be called more than once every 5 seconds. See also `auto_save_delay` within the Widget constructor options ([ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions.auto\_save\_delay](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions#auto_save_delay)
).

* * *

# Interface: SymbolExt | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.SymbolExt

Hierarchy[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#hierarchy "Direct link to Hierarchy")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`LibrarySymbolInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
    
    ↳ **`SymbolExt`**
    

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### base\_name[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#base_name "Direct link to base_name")

• `Optional` **base\_name**: `string`\[\]

Array of base symbols Example: for `AAPL*MSFT` it is `['NASDAQ:AAPL', 'NASDAQ:MSFT']`

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[base\_name](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#base_name)

* * *

### build\_seconds\_from\_ticks[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#build_seconds_from_ticks "Direct link to build_seconds_from_ticks")

• `Optional` **build\_seconds\_from\_ticks**: `boolean`

The boolean value showing whether or not seconds bars for this symbol can be built from ticks. Only available in Trading Platform.

*   [LibrarySymbolInfo.has\_seconds](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_seconds)
     must also be `true`
*   [LibrarySymbolInfo.has\_ticks](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_ticks)
     must also be `true`
*   [LibrarySymbolInfo.seconds\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#seconds_multipliers)
     must be an empty array or only contain multipliers that the datafeed provides itself

The library builds resolutions from ticks only if there are no seconds resolutions from the datafeed or the provided resolutions are larger then the required one. For example, the datafeed provides `3S` resolution. In this case, the library can build only `1S` or `2S` resolutions from ticks. Resolutions such as `15S` will be build with seconds bars.

**`Default`**

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[build\_seconds\_from\_ticks](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#build_seconds_from_ticks)

* * *

### corrections[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#corrections "Direct link to corrections")

• `Optional` **corrections**: `string`

List of corrections for a symbol. The corrections are days when the trading session differs from the default one set in [LibrarySymbolInfo.session](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#session)
. The `corrections` value is a string in the following format: `SESSION:YYYYMMDD`. For more information, refer to [corrections](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#corrections)
.

The string below specifies corrections for two trading days:

*   November 13, 2018. This trading day is split into two sessions. The first session starts at 19:00 four days before (November 9, 2018) and ends at 23:50 four days before. The second session starts at 10:00 and ends at 18:45.
*   November 14, 2018. The session starts at 10:00 and ends at 14:00.

**`Example`**

    "1900F4-2350F4,1000-1845:20181113;1000-1400:20181114"

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[corrections](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#corrections)

* * *

### currency\_code[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#currency_code "Direct link to currency_code")

• `Optional` **currency\_code**: `string`

The currency in which the instrument is traded or some other currency if currency conversion is enabled. It is displayed in the Security Info dialog and on the price axes.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-3 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[currency\_code](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#currency_code)

* * *

### daily\_multipliers[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#daily_multipliers "Direct link to daily_multipliers")

• `Optional` **daily\_multipliers**: `string`\[\]

An array of daily resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "2"]`. This property is required to enable daily resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-days)
 article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `daily_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `daily_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**

    ["1"]

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-4 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[daily\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#daily_multipliers)

* * *

### data\_status[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#data_status "Direct link to data_status")

• `Optional` **data\_status**: `"streaming"` | `"endofday"` | `"delayed_streaming"`

The status code of a series with this symbol. For `delayed_streaming` and `endofday` type of data, the status is displayed as an icon and the _Data is delayed_ section in the [_Legend_](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Legend#display-delayed-data-information)
, next to the market status icon. Note that you should also enable the [`display_data_mode`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/customization/Featuresets#display_data_mode)
 featureset.

When declaring `delayed_streaming` you also have to specify its [LibrarySymbolInfo.delay](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#delay)
 in seconds.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-5 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[data\_status](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#data_status)

* * *

### delay[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#delay "Direct link to delay")

• `Optional` **delay**: `number`

Type of delay that is associated to the data or real delay for real time data.

*   `0` for realtime
*   `-1` for endofday
*   or delay in seconds (for delayed realtime)

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-6 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[delay](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#delay)

* * *

### description[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#description "Direct link to description")

• **description**: `string`

The description of the symbol. Will be displayed in the chart legend for this symbol.

### exchange\_logo[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#exchange_logo "Direct link to exchange_logo")

• `Optional` **exchange\_logo**: `string`

URL of image to be displayed as the logo for the exchange. The `show_exchange_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

The image should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively. Simple SVG images are recommended.

Examples:

*   `https://yourserver.com/exchangeLogo.svg`
*   `/images/myImage.png`

### format[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#format "Direct link to format")

• **format**: [`SeriesFormat`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#seriesformat)

Format of displaying labels on the price scale:

`price` - formats decimal or fractional numbers based on `minmov`, `pricescale`, `minmove2`, `fractional` and `variableMinTick` values. See [Price format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format)
 for more details. `volume` - formats decimal numbers in thousands, millions, billions or trillions

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-12 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#format)

* * *

### fractional[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#fractional "Direct link to fractional")

• `Optional` **fractional**: `boolean`

For common prices this can be skipped.

Fractional prices are displayed 2 different forms: 1) `xx'yy` (for example, `133'21`) 2) `xx'yy'zz` (for example, `133'21'5`).

*   `xx` is an integer part.
*   `minmov/pricescale` is a Fraction.
*   `minmove2` is used in form 2.
*   `fractional` is `true`.
*   `variableMinTick` is skipped.

Example:

If `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128` and `minmove2 = 4`:

*   `119'16'0` represents `119 + 16/32`
*   `119'16'2` represents `119 + 16.25/32`
*   `119'16'5` represents `119 + 16.5/32`
*   `119'16'7` represents `119 + 16.75/32`

More examples:

*   `ZBM2014 (T-Bond)` with `1/32`: `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 32`, `minmove2 = 0`
*   `ZCM2014 (Corn)` with `2/8`: `minmov = 2`, `pricescale = 8`, `minmove2 = 0`
*   `ZFM2014 (5 year t-note)` with `1/4 of 1/32`: `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128`, `minmove2 = 4`

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-13 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[fractional](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#fractional)

* * *

### has\_daily[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#has_daily "Direct link to has_daily")

• `Optional` **has\_daily**: `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains daily data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a daily resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

`has_daily` is set to `true` by default. However, you should also specify [daily\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#daily_multipliers)
 to enable daily resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-days)
 article for more information.

**`Default`**

    true

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-14 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[has\_daily](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_daily)

* * *

### has\_empty\_bars[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#has_empty_bars "Direct link to has_empty_bars")

• `Optional` **has\_empty\_bars**: `boolean`

The boolean value showing whether the library should generate empty bars in the session when there is no data from the data feed for this particular time.

I.e., if your session is `0900-1600` and your data has gaps between `11:00` and `12:00` and your `has_empty_bars` is `true`, then the Library will fill the gaps with bars for this time.

Flag `has_empty_bars` = `true` cannot be used if featureset `disable_resolution_rebuild` is enabled.

**`Default`**

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-15 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[has\_empty\_bars](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_empty_bars)

* * *

### has\_intraday[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#has_intraday "Direct link to has_intraday")

• `Optional` **has\_intraday**: `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains intraday (minutes) data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when an intraday resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

This property is required to enable intraday resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-minutes-intraday)
 article for more information.

**`Default`**

    false

* * *

• `Optional` **has\_seconds**: `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains seconds data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a seconds resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

You should set `has_seconds` to `true` to enable seconds resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-seconds)
 article for more information.

**`Default`**

    false

• `Optional` **has\_ticks**: `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains ticks data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a resolution in ticks is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

You should set `has_ticks` to `true` to enable ticks resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-ticks)
 article for more information.

* * *

### has\_weekly\_and\_monthly[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#has_weekly_and_monthly "Direct link to has_weekly_and_monthly")

• `Optional` **has\_weekly\_and\_monthly**: `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains weekly or monthly data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when the corresponding resolution is selected. To enable weekly or monthly resolutions, you should also specify the [weekly\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#weekly_multipliers)
 or [monthly\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#monthly_multipliers)
 properties. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-weeks--months)
 article for more information.

If `has_weekly_and_monthly` is set to `false`, the library attempts to build the resolutions using daily bars. Note that building bars requires a large number of requests to your datafeed. If the library fails to build bars, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

**`Default`**

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-19 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[has\_weekly\_and\_monthly](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_weekly_and_monthly)

* * *

• `Optional` **industry**: `string`

Industry for stocks to be displayed in the Security Info.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-20 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[industry](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#industry)

* * *

### intraday\_multipliers[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#intraday_multipliers "Direct link to intraday_multipliers")

• `Optional` **intraday\_multipliers**: `string`\[\]

An array of intraday (minutes) resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order, for example: `["1", "2"]`.

This property is required to enable intraday resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-minutes-intraday)
 article for more information. Note that each resolution in `intraday_multipliers` should be handled in the [IDatafeedChartApi.getBars](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi#getbars)
 implementation. Consider the [example](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#example)
.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `intraday_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `intraday_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

Note that the library **cannot** build daily, weekly, or monthly resolutions using intraday data.

**`Default`**

    [] — specifies that the datafeed can provide data for any requested resolution.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-21 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[intraday\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#intraday_multipliers)

* * *

Additional metadata to include with the symbol information. These parameters will not affect existing properties such as 'ticker' or 'name' and will not be processed by the library. However, they can be accessed later through the [IChartWidgetApi.symbolExt](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#symbolext)
 method, impacting existing properties such as 'ticker' or 'name'.

• **listed\_exchange**: `string`

short name of the exchange where this symbol is traded (real listed exchange). The name will be displayed in the chart legend for this symbol.

    "NYSE"

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-23 "Direct link to Inherited from")

### logo\_urls[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#logo_urls "Direct link to logo_urls")

• `Optional` **logo\_urls**: \[`string`, `string`\] | \[`string`\]

URL of image/s to be displayed as the logo/s for the symbol. The `show_symbol_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

*   If a single url is returned then that url will solely be used to display the symbol logo.
*   If two urls are provided then the images will be displayed as two partially overlapping circles with the first url appearing on top. This is typically used for FOREX where you would like to display two country flags are the symbol logo.

The image/s should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively.

Examples:

*   `https://yourserver.com/apple.svg`
*   `/images/myImage.png`
*   `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
*   `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`

Symbol Long description

Optional long(er) description for the symbol.

* * *

### minmov[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#minmov "Direct link to minmov")

• **minmov**: `number`

The number of units that make up one tick. For example, U.S. equities are quotes in decimals, and tick in decimals, and can go up +/- `.01`. Therefore, the tick increment is 1 and `minmov = 1`. But the e-mini S&P futures contract, though quoted in decimals, goes up in `.25` increments, so the tick increment is 25 and `minmov = 25`. Refer to [Price format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format)
 for more information.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-26 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[minmov](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#minmov)

* * *

### minmove2[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#minmove2 "Direct link to minmove2")

• `Optional` **minmove2**: `number`

This property is used to display prices in the [fraction of a fraction](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#fraction-of-a-fraction-format)
 format. For example, the ZBM2023 tick size is 1/4 of a 32nd. To display this security, set `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128`, `minmove2 = 4`.

For common prices, `minmove2` can be skipped or set to `0`.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-27 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[minmove2](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#minmove2)

* * *

### monthly\_multipliers[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#monthly_multipliers "Direct link to monthly_multipliers")

• `Optional` **monthly\_multipliers**: `string`\[\]

An array of monthly resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "3", "6", "12"]`. This property is required to enable monthly resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-weeks--months)
 article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `monthly_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `monthly_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**

    ['1']

.[monthly\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#monthly_multipliers)

* * *

### name[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#name "Direct link to name")

• **name**: `string`

It is a symbol name within an exchange, such as `AAPL` or `9988` (Hong Kong). Note that it should not contain the exchange name. This symbol name is visible to users and can be repeated.

By default, `name` is used to resolve symbols in the [Datafeed API](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/datafeed-api/)
. If you use [LibrarySymbolInfo.ticker](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#ticker)
, the library will use the ticker for Datafeed API requests.

### price\_source\_id[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#price_source_id "Direct link to price_source_id")

• `Optional` **price\_source\_id**: `string`

Optional ID of a price source for a symbol. Should match one of the price sources from the [price\_sources](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#price_sources)
 array.

Note that you should set the [`symbol_info_price_source`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/customization/Featuresets#symbol_info_price_source)
 featureset to `true` to display the symbol price source in the main series legend.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-32 "Direct link to Inherited from")

* * *

### price\_sources[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#price_sources "Direct link to price_sources")

• `Optional` **price\_sources**: [`SymbolInfoPriceSource`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolInfoPriceSource)
\[\]

Supported price sources for the symbol. Price sources appear in the series legend and indicate the origin of values represented by symbol bars. Example price sources: "Spot Price", "Ask", "Bid", etc. The price source information is valuable when viewing non-OHLC series types.

Note that you should set the [`symbol_info_price_source`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/customization/Featuresets#symbol_info_price_source)
 featureset to `true` to display the symbol price source in the main series legend.

**`Example`**

    [{ id: '1', name: 'Spot Price' }, { id: '321', name: 'Bid' }]

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-33 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[price\_sources](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#price_sources)

* * *

### pricescale[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#pricescale "Direct link to pricescale")

• **pricescale**: `number`

A number of decimal places or fractions that the price has.

The `pricescale` value depends on the [price format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format)
. If you want to display the price in the decimal format, `pricescale` should be `10^n`, where `n` is the number of decimal places. **Examples:** `1`, `10`, `10000000`.

If you want to display the price in the fractional format, `pricescale` should be `2^n`. This value represents the number of fractions. **Examples:** `8`, `16`, `256`.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-34 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[pricescale](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#pricescale)

* * *

### seconds\_multipliers[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#seconds_multipliers "Direct link to seconds_multipliers")

• `Optional` **seconds\_multipliers**: `string`\[\]

An array of seconds resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "2"]`. This property is required to enable seconds resolutions. Refer to the [Configure resolutions in datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions#resolution-in-seconds)
 article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `seconds_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `seconds_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions. Consider the example. You need to enable one-second and five-second resolutions but your datafeed contains only one-second data. In this case, you should set `seconds_multipliers` to `["1"]`. The library will build the five-second resolution from one-second data.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-35 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[seconds\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#seconds_multipliers)

* * *

### session[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#session "Direct link to session")

• **session**: `string`

Trading hours for the symbol. The time should be in the **exchange** time zone specified in the [LibrarySymbolInfo.timezone](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#timezone)
 property. Refer to the [Trading sessions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/Trading-Sessions)
 article for more information.

**`Example`**

    "1700-0200"

The session value to display in the UI. If not specified, then `session` is used.

### session\_holidays[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#session_holidays "Direct link to session_holidays")

• `Optional` **session\_holidays**: `string`

A string that contains a list of non-trading holidays for the symbol. Holiday dates should be in the `YYYYMMDD` format. These dates are not displayed on the chart.

You can specify a correction for a holiday using [LibrarySymbolInfo.corrections](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#corrections)
.

**`Example`**

    "20181105,20181107,20181112"

### supported\_resolutions[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#supported_resolutions "Direct link to supported_resolutions")

• `Optional` **supported\_resolutions**: [`ResolutionString`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring)
\[\]

An array of [resolutions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/time-and-sessions/configure-datafeed-resolutions)
 which should be enabled in the _Resolution_ drop-down menu for this symbol. Each item of the array is expected to be a string that has a specific [format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Resolution#resolution-format)
.

If one changes the symbol and the new symbol does not support the selected resolution, an error message will be shown on the chart.

**Resolution availability logic (pseudocode):**

    resolutionAvailable  =    resolution.isIntraday        ? symbol.has_intraday && symbol.supported_resolutions(resolution)        : symbol.supported_resolutions(resolution);

If `supported_resolutions` is `[]` (empty array), all resolutions are disabled in the _Resolution_ drop-down menu.

If `supported_resolutions` is `undefined`, all resolutions that the chart support ([DatafeedConfiguration.supported\_resolutions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DatafeedConfiguration#supported_resolutions)
) and custom resolutions are enabled.

Note that the list of available time frames depends on supported resolutions. Time frames that require resolutions that are unavailable for a particular symbol will be hidden. Refer to [Time frame toolbar](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/Time-Scale#time-frame-toolbar)
 for more information.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-42 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[supported\_resolutions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#supported_resolutions)

* * *

### ticker[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#ticker "Direct link to ticker")

• `Optional` **ticker**: `string`

It is an unique identifier for a particular symbol in your [symbology](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/connecting_data/Symbology)
. If you specify this property, its value will be used for all data requests for this symbol. `ticker` will be treated the same as [LibrarySymbolInfo.name](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#name)
 if not specified explicitly.

You should avoid using colons (":") in ticker values unless you are following the TradingView format: "NYSE:IBM". Using colons may cause unexpected behaviour and display bugs.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-43 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[ticker](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#ticker)

* * *

• **timezone**: [`Timezone`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#timezone)

The time zone of the **exchange** where the symbol is listed. The time zone value should be in the OlsonDB format. Refer to [Time zones](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/ui_elements/timezones)
 for a full list of supported time zones.

### variable\_tick\_size[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#variable_tick_size "Direct link to variable_tick_size")

• `Optional` **variable\_tick\_size**: `string`

Dynamic minimum price movement. It is used if the instrument's minimum price movement changes depending on the price range.

For example, '0.01 10 0.02 25 0.05', where the tick size is 0.01 for a price less than 10, the tick size is 0.02 for a price less than 25, the tick size is 0.05 for a price greater than or equal to 25.

* * *

### visible\_plots\_set[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#visible_plots_set "Direct link to visible_plots_set")

• `Optional` **visible\_plots\_set**: [`VisiblePlotsSet`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#visibleplotsset)

Represents what values are supported by the symbol. Possible values:

*   `ohlcv` — the symbol supports open, high, low, close prices and has volume.
*   `ohlc` — the symbol supports open, high, low, close, prices but doesn't have volume.
*   `c` — the symbol supports only close price. This makes the chart show the symbol data using only line-based styles.

**`Default`**

    'ohlcv'

* * *

• `Optional` **volume\_precision**: `number`

Integer showing typical volume value decimal places for a particular symbol. 0 means volume is always an integer. 1 means that there might be 1 numeric character after the comma.

**`Default`**

    '0'

An array of weekly resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "3"]`. This property is required to enable weekly resolutions.  article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `weekly_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `weekly_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**

# Interface: WilliamsFractalIndicatorOverrides | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsFractalIndicatorOverrides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v28**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.WilliamsFractalIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Williams Fractal' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

Indexable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsFractalIndicatorOverrides/#indexable "Direct link to Indexable")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

▪ \[key: `string`\]: [`StudyOverrideValueType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyoverridevaluetype)

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsFractalIndicatorOverrides/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### down fractals.color[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsFractalIndicatorOverrides/#down-fractalscolor "Direct link to down fractals.color")

• **down fractals.color**: `string`

Default value: `#F23645`

* * *

### down fractals.display[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsFractalIndicatorOverrides/#down-fractalsdisplay "Direct link to down fractals.display")

• **down fractals.display**: `number`

Default value: `15`

* * *

### down fractals.location[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsFractalIndicatorOverrides/#down-fractalslocation "Direct link to down fractals.location")

• **down fractals.location**: `string`

Default value: `BelowBar`

* * *

• **down fractals.plottype**: `string`

Default value: `shape_triangle_down`

* * *

### down fractals.transparency[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WilliamsFractalIndicatorOverrides/#down-fractalstransparency "Direct link to down fractals.transparency")

* * *

This is documentation for **v28**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.VolatilityOHLCIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'Volatility O-H-L-C' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

• **plot.color**: `string`

Default value: `#FF5252`

* * *

* * *

* * *

• **plot.linewidth**: `number`

Default value: `1`

* * *

### plot.plottype[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v28/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.VolatilityOHLCIndicatorOverrides/#plotplottype "Direct link to plot.plottype")
