[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyShapesPlotPreferences/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.StudyShapesPlotPreferences

Study shape plot style preferences.

Hierarchy[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyShapesPlotPreferences/#hierarchy "Direct link to Hierarchy")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`StudyPlotBasePreferences`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyPlotBasePreferences)
    
    ↳ **`StudyShapesPlotPreferences`**
    

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyShapesPlotPreferences/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### color[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyShapesPlotPreferences/#color "Direct link to color")

• **color**: `string`

Color.

* * *

### display[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyShapesPlotPreferences/#display "Direct link to display")

• **display**: [`StudyPlotDisplayMode`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyplotdisplaymode)

Display mode. See [StudyPlotDisplayMode](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyplotdisplaymode)
.

**`Example`**

    StudyPlotDisplayTarget.None // Do not display the plot.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyShapesPlotPreferences/#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[StudyPlotBasePreferences](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyPlotBasePreferences)

* * *

• **location**: [`MarkLocation`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/enums/Charting_Library.MarkLocation)

Location relative to the bar.

* * *

• **plottype**: [`PlotShapeId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/modules/Charting_Library#plotshapeid)

Plot type.

* * *

### textColor[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyShapesPlotPreferences/#textcolor "Direct link to textColor")

• **textColor**: `string`

Text color.

* * *

### transparency[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyShapesPlotPreferences/#transparency "Direct link to transparency")

• `Optional` **transparency**: `number`

Transparency. A number between 1 and 100.

**`Example`**

    80

[StudyPlotBasePreferences](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/latest/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.StudyPlotBasePreferences)

.WatchedValueSubscribeOptions

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchedValueSubscribeOptions/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

### callWithLast[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchedValueSubscribeOptions/#callwithlast "Direct link to callWithLast")

• `Optional` **callWithLast**: `boolean`

if it is set to true then the callback will be executed with the previous value (if available)

* * *

### once[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchedValueSubscribeOptions/#once "Direct link to once")

• `Optional` **once**: `boolean`

Subscribe for only one update, and remove subscription afterwards. (the callback will be executed only once)

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.WatchedValueSubscribeOptions/#properties)

# Wir stellen vor: Tick-Charts (Betaversion) — TradingView Blog

Main content

[CHARTS](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/de/category/charts/)

Wir stellen vor: Tick-Charts (Betaversion)
==========================================

Aug 7, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/08/introducing-tick-charts-beta-perview.jpg)

Heute möchten wir Ihnen ein oft angefragtes Feature vorstellen: Die Tick-Charts (Betaversion). Die Tick-Charts ermöglichen Ihnen, Balken basierend auf einer voreingestellten Anzahl von Transaktionen erstellen zu lassen, anstelle eines festgesetzten Zeitintervalls. Jeder Balken auf einem Tick-Chart stellt eine voreingestellte Anzahl von sukzessiven Ticks dar, ungeachtet der vergangenen Zeit. Dies bietet eine granulare Ansicht der Marktaktivitäten.

Aufgrund der verwendeten Konstruktionslogik bieten Tick-Charts eine klarere Ansicht über die aktivsten Zeiten auf einem Markt (oder wenn es auf dem Markt kaum Bewegungen gibt). Des Weiteren bieten sie auch eine ausführlichere Ansicht der Preisbewegungen, Trends und des Handelsvolumens, was Ihnen dabei helfen kann, schnellere Entscheidungen zu treffen. Wenn Sie Ihren Handelsaktivitäten zu höchst volatilen Zeiten nachgehen, dann können Ihnen die Tick-Balken eine optimierte Darstellung der Handelsverläufe auf einem Markt liefern (im Vergleich zu Charts auf einer Zeitbasis).

Wenn Sie über eines unserer [professionellen Abonnements verfügen](https://de.tradingview.com/pricing/?status=pro)
 – Expert, Elite oder Ultimate – dann können Sie jetzt die Tick-Charts ausprobieren. Aktuell stehen vier Intervalle zur Auswahl: 1T, 10T, 100T und 1000T. Wenn Sie einen Tick-Chart öffnen möchten, dann geben Sie entweder das gewünschte Intervall auf Ihrer Tastatur ein (während ein Chart geöffnet ist) oder wählen Sie das gewünschte Tick-Intervall im Dropdown-Menü für die Zeitintervalle aus.

Nehmen Sie bitte zur Kenntnis, dass aktuell nicht alle Börsen über Tick-Daten verfügen. Sie können sich eine Liste der unterstützten Börsen (sowie weitere Vorteile und Begrenzungen unserer Tick-Charts) in diesem [Artikel](https://de.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225-what-are-tick-based-intervals-for/)
 ansehen.

Das Feature befindet sich aktuell in der Betaversion, wir würden also Ihr Feedback und Verbesserungsvorschläge zu schätzen wissen — wir hören immer gern von Ihnen.

Vielen Dank, dass Sie sich für TradingView entschieden haben.

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView wurde für Sie entwickelt. Stellen Sie sicher, dass Sie unsere fantastischen Features optimal nutzen

[Supercharts öffnen](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

---

# Introduction des graphiques en ticks (beta) — TradingView Blog

Main content

[GRAPHIQUES](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/fr/category/charts/)

Introduction des graphiques en ticks (beta)
===========================================

Aug 7, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/08/introducing-tick-charts-beta-perview.jpg)

Aujourd’hui, nous présentons l’une des fonctionnalités les plus demandées: les graphiques en ticks (en version beta). Les graphiques en ticks vous permettent de construire des barres basées sur un nombre défini de transactions plutôt que sur des intervalles de temps fixes. Chaque barre d’un tel graphique représente un nombre déterminé de ticks successifs, indépendamment du temps écoulé, ce qui offre une vue granulaire de l’activité du marché.

Grâce à cette logique de construction, les graphiques en tic-tac peuvent donner une vision plus claire des moments où le marché est le plus actif ou à peine en mouvement. En outre, ils offrent une perspective plus détaillée de l’action des prix, des tendances et du volume des transactions, ce qui permet de prendre des décisions plus rapides. En période de forte volatilité, les tick bars offrent une meilleure visualisation des actions sur le marché que les graphiques basés sur le temps.

Si vous possédez l’un des [plans de niveau professionnel](https://fr.tradingview.com/pricing/?status=pro)
 – Expert, Elite ou Ultimate – vous pouvez essayer les ticks dès maintenant. Pour l’instant, vous avez le choix entre quatre intervalles: 1T, 10T, 100T et 1000T. Pour ouvrir un graphique en ticks, saisissez l’intervalle sur votre clavier lorsque le graphique est ouvert, ou sélectionnez l’intervalle en ticks souhaité dans le menu déroulant Intervalle de temps.

Veuillez noter que toutes les bourses ne disposent pas encore de données en ticks. Vous trouverez une liste actualisée de toutes les bourses prises en charge, ainsi que d’autres avantages et limitations de nos graphiques en ticks, dans [cet article sur les intervalles basés sur les ticks](https://fr.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225-what-are-tick-based-intervals-for/)
.

Cette fonctionnalité est actuellement en version beta, c’est pourquoi nous vous serions très reconnaissants de nous faire part de vos commentaires et de vos suggestions – nous aimons beaucoup recevoir des nouvelles de votre part.

Merci d’avoir choisi TradingView.

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView est conçu pour vous, assurez-vous donc de profiter au maximum de nos fonctionnalités exceptionnelles

[Lancer Supercharts](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

---

# Finalmente i grafici a tick (beta) — TradingView Blog

Main content

[GRAFICI](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/it/category/charts/)

Finalmente i grafici a tick (beta)
==================================

Aug 7, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/08/introducing-tick-charts-beta-perview.jpg)

Oggi introduciamo una delle funzioni storicamente più richieste: i grafici a tick (in versione beta). I grafici a tick consentono di costruire barre basate su un numero definito di transazioni piuttosto che su intervalli di tempo fissi. Ogni barra di un grafico di questo tipo rappresenta un numero prestabilito di tick successivi, indipendentemente dal tempo trascorso, il che offre una visione granulare dell’attività di mercato.

Grazie a questa logica di costruzione, i grafici a tick possono fornire una visione più chiara di quando il mercato è più attivo o poco mosso. Inoltre, forniscono una prospettiva più dettagliata dell’azione dei prezzi, delle tendenze e del volume degli scambi, facilitando così decisioni di trading più rapide. Quando si opera in periodi di forte volatilità, le barre a tick forniscono una migliore visualizzazione delle attività di trading sul mercato rispetto ai grafici basati sul tempo.

Se si dispone di uno dei [piani di livello professionale](https://it.tradingview.com/pricing/?status=pro)
 – Expert, Elite, or Ultimate – è possibile provarli già da oggi. Al momento si può scegliere tra quattro intervalli: 1T, 10T, 100T e 1000T. Per aprire un grafico a tick, è sufficiente digitare l’intervallo sulla tastiera mentre il grafico è aperto, oppure selezionare l’intervallo desiderato basato sui tick dal menu a tendina.

N.B.: al momento non tutte le borse dispongono di dati tick. Un elenco aggiornato di tutte le borse supportate, insieme ad altri vantaggi e limiti dei nostri grafici tick, è disponibile in [questo articolo](https://it.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225-what-are-tick-based-intervals-for/)
.

La funzione è attualmente in fase beta, pertanto vi saremmo particolarmente grati se ci inviaste commenti e suggerimenti.

Grazie per aver scelto TradingView.

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView è realizzato per te. Assicurati di sfruttare al massimo tutte le sue funzionalità

[Apri Grafico](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

---

# Przedstawiamy wykresy tickowe (wersja beta) — TradingView Blog

Main content

[WYKRESY](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/pl/category/charts/)

Przedstawiamy wykresy tickowe (wersja beta)
===========================================

Aug 7, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/08/introducing-tick-charts-beta-perview.jpg)

Dzisiaj wprowadzamy jedną z najbardziej oczekiwanych funkcji: wykresy tickowe (w wersji beta). Wykresy tickowe pozwalają budować słupki na podstawie określonej liczby transakcji, a nie stałych przedziałów czasowych. Każdy słupek na takim wykresie reprezentuje ustaloną liczbę kolejnych ticków, niezależnie od upływu czasu, co zapewnia szczegółowy widok aktywności rynkowej.

Dzięki tej logice konstrukcyjnej wykresy tickowe mogą dać wyraźniejszy obraz tego, kiedy rynek jest najbardziej aktywny lub ledwo się porusza. Ponadto zapewniają bardziej szczegółową perspektywę akcji cenowej, trendów i wolumenu obrotu, co ułatwia szybsze podejmowanie decyzji handlowych. Podczas handlu w okresach dużej zmienności, paski tickowe zapewniają lepszą wizualizację działań handlowych na rynku w porównaniu z wykresami opartymi na czasie.

IJeśli masz jeden z [planów profesjonalnych](https://pl.tradingview.com/pricing/?status=pro)
 – Expert, Elite lub Ultimate – możesz wypróbować ticks już teraz. W tej chwili są cztery interwały do ​​wyboru: 1T, 10T, 100T i 1000T. Aby otworzyć wykres tickowy, wpisz interwał na klawiaturze, gdy wykres jest otwarty, lub wybierz żądany interwał oparty na tickach z rozwijanej listy Interwał Czasowy.

Należy pamiętać, że nie wszystkie giełdy mają obecnie dane tickowe. Aktualną listę wszystkich obsługiwanych giełd, wraz z innymi zaletami i ograniczeniami naszych wykresów tickowych, można znaleźć w tym [artykule o interwałach tickowych](https://pl.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225-what-are-tick-based-intervals-for/)
.

Funkcja ta jest obecnie w fazie beta, dlatego będziemy bardzo wdzięczni za wszelkie opinie i sugestie — zawsze się na nie cieszymy.

Dziękujemy za wybranie TradingView.

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView jest stworzony dla Ciebie. Upewnij się, że w pełni wykorzystujesz nasze niesamowite funkcje

[Uruchom Superwykresy](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

---

# Memperkenalkan carta tick (beta) — TradingView Blog

Main content

[CARTA](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/my/category/charts/)

Memperkenalkan carta tick (beta)
================================

Aug 7, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/08/introducing-tick-charts-beta-perview.jpg)

Hari ini, kami memperkenalkan satu daripada ciri paling banyak dipinta: carta tick (dalam beta). Carta tick membenarkan anda untuk membina bar-bar berdasarkan kepada satu bilangan transaksi yang ditentukan dan bukannya selang masa tetap. Setiap bar pada carta sebegini mewakili satu set bilangan tick berterusan, tidak mengira masa yang telah berlalu, yang dapat memberikan pandangan granular untuk aktiviti pasaran.

Disebabkan logik pembinaan ini, carta tick dapat memberikan gambaran lebih jelas bila pasaran paling aktif atau paling kurang bergerak. Tambahan lagi, mereka memberikan perspektif lebih mendalam untuk tindakan harga, trend dan volum dagangan, yang membantu mempercepatkan keputusan dagangan. Apabila berdagang dalam masa ketidakstabilan sangat tinggi, bar-bar tick memberikan pemvisualan lebih baik untuk tindakan dagangan dalam pasaran berbanding carta berasaskan masa.

Jika anda mempunyai satu daripada [pelan bertaraf Profesional](https://my.tradingview.com/pricing/?status=pro)
 – Expert, Elite atau Ultimate – anda boleh terus mencuba tick. Pada masa ini, ada empat selang masa yang anda boleh pilih: 1T, 10T, 100T dan 1000T. Untuk membuka satu carta tick, sama ada taip selang masa pada papan kekunci anda jika anda telah membuka carta atau pilih selang masa berasaskan tick yang anda ingini dari menu lungsur turun Selang masa.

Sila ambil maklum bahawa tidak semua bursa mempunyai data tick pada masa ini. Anda boleh mendapatkan senarai terkini semua bursa yang menyokong, termasuk kelebihan dan kekurangan carta tick dalam [artikel selang masa berasaskan tick](https://my.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225-what-are-tick-based-intervals-for/)
 ini.

Ciri ini sekarang berada dalam fasa Beta, jadi kami sangat menghargai maklum balas dan cadangan anda — kami suka mendengar dari anda.

Terima kasih kerana memilih TradingView.

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView dibina untuk anda, pastikan anda memanfaatkan semua ciri-ciri hebat kami

# Представляем тиковые графики (бета) — TradingView Blog

Main content

[Графики](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/ru/category/charts/)

Представляем тиковые графики (бета)
===================================

7 авг, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/08/introducing-tick-charts-beta-perview.jpg)

Сегодня мы представляем одно из самых востребованных обновлений: тиковые графики (в бета-версии). Они позволяют строить бары на основе определённого количества транзакций, а не фиксированных временных интервалов. Каждый бар на таком графике представляет собой заданное количество последовательных тиков, независимо от прошедшего времени, что позволяет получить детальное представление о рыночной активности.

Благодаря такой логике построения, тиковые графики могут дать более четкое представление о том, когда рынок наиболее активен или почти не движется. Кроме того, они помогают лучше понимать движение цены, тренды и объём торгов, что позволяет быстрее принимать торговые решения. При торговле в периоды высокой волатильности тиковые графики обеспечивают более чёткую визуализацию движений на рынке по сравнению с временны́ми графиками.

Если у вас есть одна из наших [профессиональных подписок](https://ru.tradingview.com/pricing/?status=pro)
 — Expert, Elite или Ultimate — вы можете испытать тиковые графики уже сейчас. На данный момент можно выбирать из четырёх интервалов: 1T, 10T, 100T и 1000T. Чтобы переключиться на тиковый график, введите интервал на клавиатуре, пока открыт график, или выберите нужный тиковый интервал из выпадающего списка меню _Интервал_:

Обратите внимание, что на данный момент не все биржи поддерживают тиковые данные. Актуальный список бирж, а также другие преимущества и ограничения новых графиков можно найти в этой [статье о тиковых интервалах](https://ru.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225/)
.

В настоящее время тиковые графики находится на этапе бета-тестирования, поэтому мы будем благодарны за ваши отзывы и предложения.

Спасибо, что выбираете TradingView.

Look first Then leap
--------------------

Мы создали TradingView, чтобы вы могли пользоваться самыми крутыми функциями.

[Открыть Суперграфики](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

# Apresentando os gráficos de ticks (beta) — TradingView Blog

Main content

[GRáFICOS](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/pb/category/charts/)

Apresentando os gráficos de ticks (beta)
========================================

Aug 7, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/08/introducing-tick-charts-beta-perview.jpg)

Hoje, apresentamos um dos recursos mais solicitados da história: gráficos de ticks (em versão beta). Os gráficos de ticks permitem que você crie barras com base em um número definido de transações em vez de intervalos de tempo fixos. Cada barra em um gráfico desse tipo representa um número definido de ticks sucessivos, independentemente do tempo decorrido, o que oferece uma visão granular da atividade do mercado.

Devido a essa lógica de construção, os gráficos de ticks podem dar uma visão mais clara de quando o mercado está mais ativo ou pouco movimentado. Além disso, eles fornecem uma perspectiva mais detalhada do price action, das tendências e do volume de negociação, o que ajuda a facilitar decisões de negociação mais rápidas. Ao negociar durante períodos de alta volatilidade, as barras de ticks proporcionam uma visualização otimizada das ações de negociação no mercado em comparação com os gráficos baseados em Tempo.

Se você tiver um dos [planos de nível profissional](https://br.tradingview.com/pricing/?status=pro)
 – Expert, Elite ou Ultimate – poderá experimentar os ticks agora mesmo. No momento, há quatro intervalos para escolher: 1T, 10T, 100T e 1000T. Para abrir um gráfico de ticks, digite o intervalo no teclado enquanto o gráfico estiver aberto ou selecione o intervalo baseado em ticks desejado no menu suspenso Intervalo de tempo.

Observe que nem todas as bolsas têm dados de ticks no momento. Você pode encontrar uma lista atualizada de todas as bolsas suportadas, juntamente com outras características e limitações de nossos gráficos de ticks, neste [artigo sobre intervalos baseados em ticks](https://br.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225/)
.

O recurso está atualmente na versão Beta, portanto, agradecemos imensamente seus comentários e sugestões – adoramos ouvir suas opiniões.

Obrigado por escolher o TradingView.

Look first Then leap
--------------------

O TradingView é feito para você, então garanta que você está aproveitando ao máximo nossos recursos incríveis

[Abrir Supergráficos](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

# Memperkenalkan chart tick (beta) — TradingView Blog

Main content

[CHART](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/id/category/charts/)

Memperkenalkan chart tick (beta)
================================

Aug 7, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/08/introducing-tick-charts-beta-perview.jpg)

Hari ini, kami memperkenalkan salah satu fitur dengan riwayat yang paling banyak diminta: chart tick (dalam versi beta). Chart tick memungkinkan anda membangun bar berdasarkan sejumlah transaksi yang ditentukan, bukan interval waktu yang tetap. Setiap bar pada chart tersebut mewakili sejumlah tick berturut-turut, terlepas dari waktu yang telah berlalu, yang menawarkan tampilan terperinci dari aktivitas pasar.

Berkat logika konstruksi ini, chart tick dapat memberikan tampilan yang lebih jelas tentang kapan pasar paling aktif atau hampir tidak bergerak. Selain itu, chart ini memberikan perspektif yang lebih terperinci tentang price action, tren, dan volume trading, yang membantu memfasilitasi keputusan trading yang lebih cepat. Saat melakukan trading pada masa yang sangat fluktuatif, bar tick memberikan visualisasi yang lebih baik tentang aksi trading di pasar dibandingkan dengan chart berbasis waktu.

Jika anda memiliki salah satu [paket tingkat Profesional](https://id.tradingview.com/pricing/?status=pro)
 – Expert, Elite, atau Ultimate – anda dapat mencoba chart tick sekarang juga. Saat ini, terdapat empat interval yang dapat dipilih: 1T, 10T, 100T, dan 1000T. Untuk membuka chart tick, ketik interval pada keyboard anda saat chart terbuka, atau pilih interval berbasis tick yang diinginkan dari menu dropdown Interval waktu.

Harap perhatikan bahwa tidak semua bursa memiliki data tick untuk saat ini. Anda dapat menemukan daftar terkini untuk semua bursa yang didukung, beserta kelebihan dan keterbatasan lain dari chart tick kami, dalam [artikel interval berbasis tick](https://id.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225-what-are-tick-based-intervals-for/)
 ini.

Fitur ini masih dalam versi Beta, jadi kami akan sangat berterima kasih atas masukan dan saran anda — karena kami senang mendengar masukan dari anda.

Terima kasih telah memilih TradingView.

Look first Then leap
--------------------

# Giới thiệu biểu đồ tick (phiên bản beta) — TradingView Blog

Main content

[BIểU đồ](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/vi/category/charts/)

Giới thiệu biểu đồ tick (phiên bản beta)
========================================

Aug 7, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/08/introducing-tick-charts-beta-perview.jpg)

Hôm nay, chúng tôi giới thiệu một trong những tính năng được yêu cầu nhiều nhất trong lịch sử: biểu đồ tick (ở phiên bản beta). Biểu đồ tick cho phép bạn xây dựng các thanh dựa trên số lượng giao dịch xác định thay vì các khoảng thời gian cố định. Mỗi thanh trên biểu đồ như vậy biểu thị một số lượng tick liên tiếp, bất kể thời gian đã trôi qua, cung cấp góc nhìn chi tiết về hoạt động của thị trường.

Nhờ logic xây dựng này, biểu đồ tick có thể cung cấp góc nhìn rõ hơn về thời điểm thị trường hoạt động mạnh nhất hoặc hầu như không biến động. Ngoài ra, biểu đồ này cung cấp góc nhìn chi tiết hơn về hành động giá, xu hướng và khối lượng giao dịch, giúp đưa ra quyết định giao dịch nhanh hơn. Khi giao dịch trong thời gian biến động mạnh, thanh tick cung cấp hình ảnh trực quan hơn về các hành động giao dịch trên thị trường so với biểu đồ theo thời gian.

Nếu bạn có một trong các gói [Các gói chuyên nghiệp theo tier:](https://vn.tradingview.com/pricing/?status=pro#plans)
 Expert, Elite, hoặc Ultimate. Hãy dùng thử tick ngay bây giờ. Hiện tại, có bốn khoảng thời gian để lựa chọn: 1T, 10T, 100T và 1000T. Để mở biểu đồ tick, hãy nhập khoảng thời gian trên bàn phím khi bạn mở biểu đồ hoặc chọn khoảng thời gian chạy trên tick mong muốn từ danh sách thả xuống Khoảng thời gian.

Xin lưu ý rằng không phải tất cả các sàn giao dịch đều có dữ liệu tick tại thời điểm này. Bạn có thể tìm thấy danh sách cập nhật của tất cả các sàn giao dịch được hỗ trợ, cùng với các ưu điểm và hạn chế khác của biểu đồ tick của chúng tôi, trong bài viết về [khoảng thời gian chạy trên tick này](https://vn.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225-what-are-tick-based-intervals-for/)
.

Tính năng này hiện đang ở giai đoạn Beta, vì vậy chúng tôi sẽ rất biết ơn nếu nhận được phản hồi và đề xuất của bạn — chúng tôi rất muốn nghe ý kiến ​​của bạn.

Cảm ơn bạn đã chọn TradingView.

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView được xây dựng dành cho bạn, vì vậy nhớ đảm bảo bạn tận dụng hiệu quả nhất các tính năng tuyệt vời của chúng tôi

[Ra mắt Supercharts](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

# Introducing tick charts (beta) — TradingView Blog

Main content

[CHARTS](https://www.tradingview.com/blog/en/category/charts/)

Introducing tick charts (beta)
==============================

Aug 7, 2024

![](https://tvblog-static.tradingview.com/uploads/2024/08/introducing-tick-charts-beta-perview.jpg)

Today, we introduce one of the most historically requested features: tick charts (in beta). Tick charts allow you to build bars based on a defined number of transactions rather than fixed time intervals. Each bar on such a chart represents a set number of successive ticks, irrespective of the elapsed time, which offers a granular view of market activity.

Due to this construction logic, tick charts can give a clearer view of when the market is most active or barely moving. Additionally, they provide a more detailed perspective of price action, trends, and trading volume, which helps to facilitate faster trading decisions. When trading during highly volatile times, tick bars provide enhanced visualization of trading actions in the market compared to time-based charts.

If you have one of the [Professional-tier plans](https://www.tradingview.com/pricing/?status=pro)
 – Expert, Elite, or Ultimate – you can try ticks right now. At the moment, there are four intervals to choose from: 1T, 10T, 100T, and 1000T. To open a tick chart, either type the interval on your keyboard while you have the chart open, or select the desired tick-based interval from the Time interval dropdown.

Please note that not all exchanges have tick data at the moment. You can find an up to date list of all supported exchanges, along with other advantages and limitations of our tick charts, in this [tick-based intervals article](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225-what-are-tick-based-intervals-for/)
.

The feature is currently in Beta, so we would be extra grateful for your feedback and suggestions — we love hearing from you.

Thank you for choosing TradingView.

Look first Then leap
--------------------

TradingView is built for you, so make sure you're getting the most of our awesome features

[Launch Supercharts](https://www.tradingview.com/chart/?symbol=NASDAQ:AAPL)

# What are tick-based intervals — TradingView

 

[](https://www.tradingview.com/)

Search

EN 

[Get started](https://www.tradingview.com/pricing/?source=header_go_pro_button&feature=start_free_trial)

What are tick-based intervals
=============================

If you're an intraday trader and need to respond immediately to price fluctuations and capture short-term swings, with tick-based intervals you can access up to 40,000 bars and get a granular view of market changes.

Tick-based intervals can also be referred to as tick charts. These terms often used interchangeably.

**CONTENTS:**

*   [What are tick-based intervals](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225-what-are-tick-based-intervals/#What-are)
    
*   [Tick interval benefits](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225-what-are-tick-based-intervals/#Benefits)
    
*   [Tick-based interval limitations](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000709225-what-are-tick-based-intervals/#Limitations)
    

#### What are tick-based intervals

Unlike time-based charts, which periodically construct bars/candles in fixed blocks of time (e.g., each bar on a 30S chart represents 30 seconds), tick charts create bars based on the number of successive ticks, irrespective of the elapsed time. 

A 10-tick chart, for example, shows a new bar after every 10 consecutive ticks. A recorded tick contains price, time, and volume information, and each 10-tick bar shows the opening, high, low, and closing tick prices for each set of 10 ticks. The time elapsed in a 10-tick bar could be a few seconds, a minute, an hour, or even a day. In other words, the time it takes to record 10 ticks does not impact the formation of chart bars. A bar on a tick chart closes after it contains the required number of ticks, and a new bar begins when a new tick is available after the latest bar closes. The timestamp provided for each bar represents its starting time, i.e., the time of its first recorded tick.

> **! Note:** Tick-based bars, like other intraday bars, align with the beginning of the trading session. As such, a new bar on a tick chart will also open at the start of a session, even if the previous bar does not contain the required number of ticks.

#### Tick interval benefits

Tick charts provide some significant advantages.

A granular view of market activity. Since tick bars form based on a defined number of transactions, they can give a clearer view of when the market is most active or barely moving. New tick bars form more frequently during periods of high market activity. Conversely, new bars appear infrequently during periods of low market activity. 

A more detailed perspective of price action, trends, and trading volume can help you make smart trading decisions. This perspective is helpful for short-term strategies like scalping, where you need to make successive small profits from numerous quick trades.

Also, this interval can provide you with enhanced visualization of trading actions during highly volatile times in the market compared to time-based charts.

Users can choose from the following options: 1T, 10T, 100T, and 1,000T. So, one can load a chart whose bars show values for each tick (1T) or a chart that displays information for 10/100/1,000 ticks per bar. 

How to set up a tick-based chart

Tick-based intervals don't support the following chart types:

*   [Renko](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000502284-understanding-renko-charts/)
    
*   [Line break](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000502273-introduction-to-line-break-charts/)
    
*   [Kagi](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000502272-learn-to-use-kagi-charts/)
    
*   [Point and figure](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000502276-what-are-point-and-figure-charts/)
    

To use tick interval, select a supported chart type and choose a tick-based interval from the menu above the chart.

![](https://s3.amazonaws.com/cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/43470174338/original/9ZwxZs5Rc0KJaKuVXJ1mQ0mNC93in9HA6A.png?1709136616)

Alternatively, you can type 1T, 10T, 100T, or 1,000T on your keyboard and press "Enter."

![](https://s3.amazonaws.com/cdn.freshdesk.com/data/helpdesk/attachments/production/43470174674/original/Td9_yvA8DvAguSNGEXhmMkJm0ORBfZ-pzw.png?1709136688)

Tick data is now available for almost all exchanges and symbols.

However, it currently doesn't support:

*   Indices without volume
*   Options
*   Government bonds and yields with the "TVC:" prefix
*   End-of-day symbols

If a chart's symbol doesn't have tick data, the tick intervals are grayed out and unavailable in the intervals menu. 

> **! Note:** Continuous futures ("1!" and "2!" symbols, e.g., CME\_MINI:ES1!, CBOT\_MINI:YM1!) have additional limitations aside from the number of bars. The maximum tick data length for continuous futures can only include the current and previous contracts. No data is available for earlier contracts.

#### Tick-based interval limitations

*   Tick-based intervals are pre-set and cannot be customized
*   Bar Replay and deep backtesting don't work with tick-based intervals
*   Tick-based Renko, line break, Kagi, and point and figure charts aren't available yet
*   Server-side alerts aren't available for tick-based intervals
*   Tick-based intervals aren't present in our mobile apps yet
*   Public ideas don't allow using tick-based charts

#### Tick interval in a nutshell

Tick-based charts form bars based on the number of transactions rather than fixed time periods, providing you with a more granular view of the market. They're particularly useful for short-term trading strategies, as they show market activity density and offer enhanced visualization during volatile periods.

Also read:

*   [Indicators: simple steps to get started](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000543626-tradingview-indicators-simple-steps-to-get-started/)
    
*   [The technical analysis essentials](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000759577-the-technical-analysis-essentials-with-tradingview/)
    
*   [How to trade on TradingView](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000756695-how-to-trade-on-tradingview/)
    
*   [Paper Trading — main functionality](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000516466-paper-trading-main-functionality/)
    
*   [Chart types available on TradingView](https://www.tradingview.com/support/solutions/43000703407-chart-types-available-on-tradingview/)

# Interface: SymbolResolveExtension | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.SymbolResolveExtension

Additional information about the Symbol's currency or unit

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### currencyCode[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#currencycode "Direct link to currencyCode")

• `Optional` **currencyCode**: `string`

Indicates the currency for conversions if `currency_codes` configuration field is set, and `currency_code` is provided in the original symbol information ([LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
). Read more about [currency conversion](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/ui_elements/Price-Scale#currency-conversion)
.

* * *

### session[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#session "Direct link to session")

• `Optional` **session**: `string`

Trading session string

* * *

### unitId[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#unitid "Direct link to unitId")

• `Optional` **unitId**: `string`

Indicates the unit for conversion if `units` configuration field is set and `unit_id` is provided in the original symbol information ([LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
).

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#properties)
    *   [currencyCode](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#currencycode)
        
    *   [session](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#session)
        
    *   [unitId](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#unitid)

---

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### prevValues[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#prevvalues "Direct link to prevValues")

• `Optional` **prevValues**: `Partial`<`T` extends \[...args: A\[\]\] ? \[...A\[\]\] : `never`\>

Optional field. It is array of previous values so you can compare and format accordingly. It exists if current column has the `highlightDiff: true` key.

* * *

### values[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#values "Direct link to values")

• **values**: `T` extends \[...args: A\[\]\] ? \[...A\[\]\] : `never`

Array of values to be formatted. Values are obtained by extracting dependent properties from the data object.

*   [Type parameters](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#type-parameters)

### asterix[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TextWithCheckboxFieldCustomInfo/#asterix "Direct link to asterix")

• `Optional` **asterix**: `boolean`

Using `asterix` property you can manage input type. If `asterix` is set to `true` then a password input will be rendered.

* * *

### checkboxTitle[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TextWithCheckboxFieldCustomInfo/#checkboxtitle "Direct link to checkboxTitle")

• **checkboxTitle**: `string`

Title for the checkbox

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TextWithCheckboxFieldCustomInfo/#properties)
    *   [asterix](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TextWithCheckboxFieldCustomInfo/#asterix)
        
    *   [checkboxTitle](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TextWithCheckboxFieldCustomInfo/#checkboxtitle)

### chart\_theme\_changed[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#chart_theme_changed "Direct link to chart_theme_changed")

• **chart\_theme\_changed**: (`themeName`: `string`, `isStandardTheme`: `boolean`, `onlyActiveChart`: `boolean`) => `void`

Fired when the chart theme is updated. This event can be triggered in several ways:

*   When a user applies a saved [chart template](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/saving_loading/#chart-templates)
    .
*   When the chart theme is reset to the default settings. (`isStandardTheme` parameter will be true)
*   When the [IChartingLibraryWidget.changeTheme](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartingLibraryWidget#changetheme)
     method is called.
*   When the [IChartWidgetApi.loadChartTemplate](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#loadcharttemplate)
     method is called.

#### Type declaration[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#type-declaration-2 "Direct link to Type declaration")

▸ (`themeName`, `isStandardTheme`, `onlyActiveChart`): `void`

##### Parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#parameters-2 "Direct link to Parameters")

| Name | Type | Description |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `themeName` | `string` | The name of the chart theme or template that was applied. |
| `isStandardTheme` | `boolean` | `true` if the default built-in theme was restored; `false`, if a custom chart template was applied. |
| `onlyActiveChart` | `boolean` | `false`, when applied to all charts |

##### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#returns-2 "Direct link to Returns")

`void`

* * *

### onAutoSaveNeeded[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SubscribeEventsMap/#onautosaveneeded "Direct link to onAutoSaveNeeded")

• **onAutoSaveNeeded**: [`EmptyCallback`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#emptycallback)

User has changed the chart. `Chart change` means any user action that can be undone. The callback function will not be called more than once every 5 seconds. See also `auto_save_delay` within the Widget constructor options ([ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions.auto\_save\_delay](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.ChartingLibraryWidgetOptions#auto_save_delay)
).

* * *

# Interface: SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'SuperTrend' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

Indexable[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#indexable "Direct link to Indexable")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

▪ \[key: `string`\]: [`StudyOverrideValueType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#studyoverridevaluetype)

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SuperTrendIndicatorOverrides/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Interface: SymbolResolveExtension | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.SymbolResolveExtension

Additional information about the Symbol's currency or unit

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### currencyCode[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#currencycode "Direct link to currencyCode")

• `Optional` **currencyCode**: `string`

Indicates the currency for conversions if `currency_codes` configuration field is set, and `currency_code` is provided in the original symbol information ([LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
). Read more about [currency conversion](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/ui_elements/Price-Scale#enable-currency-conversion)
.

* * *

### session[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#session "Direct link to session")

• `Optional` **session**: `string`

Trading session type, such as `"regular"` or `"extended"`, that the chart should currently display.

* * *

### unitId[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#unitid "Direct link to unitId")

• `Optional` **unitId**: `string`

Indicates the unit for conversion if `units` configuration field is set and `unit_id` is provided in the original symbol information ([LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
).

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolResolveExtension/#properties)

**.

On this page

[Datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Datafeed)
.SymbolResolveExtension

Additional information about the Symbol's currency or unit

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### currencyCode[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#currencycode "Direct link to currencyCode")

• `Optional` **currencyCode**: `string`

Indicates the currency for conversions if `currency_codes` configuration field is set, and `currency_code` is provided in the original symbol information ([LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo)
). Read more about [currency conversion](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/ui_elements/Price-Scale#enable-currency-conversion)
.

* * *

• `Optional` **session**: `string`

Trading session type, such as `"regular"` or `"extended"`, that the chart should currently display.

* * *

### unitId[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.SymbolResolveExtension/#unitid "Direct link to unitId")

• `Optional` **unitId**: `string`

Indicates the unit for conversion if `units` configuration field is set and `unit_id` is provided in the original symbol information ([LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.LibrarySymbolInfo)
).

**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.SymbolExt

Hierarchy[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#hierarchy "Direct link to Hierarchy")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

*   [`LibrarySymbolInfo`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
    
    ↳ **`SymbolExt`**
    

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### base\_name[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#base_name "Direct link to base_name")

• `Optional` **base\_name**: `string`\[\]

Array of base symbols Example: for `AAPL*MSFT` it is `['NASDAQ:AAPL', 'NASDAQ:MSFT']`

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[base\_name](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#base_name)

* * *

### build\_seconds\_from\_ticks[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#build_seconds_from_ticks "Direct link to build_seconds_from_ticks")

• `Optional` **build\_seconds\_from\_ticks**: `boolean`

The boolean value showing whether or not seconds bars for this symbol can be built from ticks. Only available in Trading Platform.

*   [LibrarySymbolInfo.has\_seconds](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_seconds)
     must also be `true`
*   [LibrarySymbolInfo.has\_ticks](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_ticks)
     must also be `true`
*   [LibrarySymbolInfo.seconds\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#seconds_multipliers)
     must be an empty array or only contain multipliers that the datafeed provides itself

The library builds resolutions from ticks only if there are no seconds resolutions from the datafeed or the provided resolutions are larger then the required one. For example, the datafeed provides `3S` resolution. In this case, the library can build only `1S` or `2S` resolutions from ticks. Resolutions such as `15S` will be build with seconds bars.

**`Default`**

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-1 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[build\_seconds\_from\_ticks](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#build_seconds_from_ticks)

* * *

### corrections[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#corrections "Direct link to corrections")

• `Optional` **corrections**: `string`

List of corrections for a symbol. The corrections are days when the trading session differs from the default one set in [LibrarySymbolInfo.session](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#session)
. The `corrections` value is a string in the following format: `SESSION:YYYYMMDD`. For more information, refer to [corrections](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/connecting_data/Symbology#corrections)
.

The string below specifies corrections for two trading days:

*   November 13, 2018. This trading day is split into two sessions. The first session starts at 19:00 four days before (November 9, 2018) and ends at 23:50 four days before. The second session starts at 10:00 and ends at 18:45.
*   November 14, 2018. The session starts at 10:00 and ends at 14:00.

**`Example`**

    "1900F4-2350F4,1000-1845:20181113;1000-1400:20181114"

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-2 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[corrections](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#corrections)

* * *

### currency\_code[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#currency_code "Direct link to currency_code")

• `Optional` **currency\_code**: `string`

The currency in which the instrument is traded or some other currency if currency conversion is enabled. It is displayed in the Security Info dialog and on the price axes.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-3 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[currency\_code](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#currency_code)

* * *

### daily\_multipliers[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#daily_multipliers "Direct link to daily_multipliers")

• `Optional` **daily\_multipliers**: `string`\[\]

An array of daily resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "2"]`. This property is required to enable daily resolutions. Refer to the [Resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#resolution-in-days)
 article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `daily_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `daily_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**

    ["1"]

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-4 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[daily\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#daily_multipliers)

* * *

### data\_status[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#data_status "Direct link to data_status")

• `Optional` **data\_status**: `"streaming"` | `"endofday"` | `"delayed_streaming"`

The status code of a series with this symbol. For `delayed_streaming` and `endofday` type of data, the status is displayed as an icon and the _Data is delayed_ section in the [_Legend_](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/ui_elements/Legend#display-delayed-data-information)
, next to the market status icon. Note that you should also enable the [`display_data_mode`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/customization/Featuresets#display_data_mode)
 featureset.

When declaring `delayed_streaming` you also have to specify its [LibrarySymbolInfo.delay](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#delay)
 in seconds.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-5 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[data\_status](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#data_status)

* * *

### delay[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#delay "Direct link to delay")

• `Optional` **delay**: `number`

Type of delay that is associated to the data or real delay for real time data.

*   `0` for realtime
*   `-1` for endofday
*   or delay in seconds (for delayed realtime)

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-6 "Direct link to Inherited from")

.[delay](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#delay)

* * *

### description[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#description "Direct link to description")

• **description**: `string`

The description of the symbol. Will be displayed in the chart legend for this symbol.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-7 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[description](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#description)

* * *

### exchange[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#exchange "Direct link to exchange")

• **exchange**: `string`

Traded exchange (current (proxy) exchange). The name will be displayed in the chart legend for this symbol.

**`Example`**

    "NYSE"

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-8 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[exchange](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#exchange)

* * *

### exchange\_logo[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#exchange_logo "Direct link to exchange_logo")

• `Optional` **exchange\_logo**: `string`

URL of image to be displayed as the logo for the exchange. The `show_exchange_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

The image should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively. Simple SVG images are recommended.

Examples:

*   `https://yourserver.com/exchangeLogo.svg`
*   `/images/myImage.png`
*   `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
*   `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-9 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[exchange\_logo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#exchange_logo)

* * *

### expiration\_date[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#expiration_date "Direct link to expiration_date")

• `Optional` **expiration\_date**: `number`

Unix timestamp of the expiration date. One must set this value when `expired` = `true`. The library will request data for this symbol starting from that time point.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-10 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[expiration\_date](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#expiration_date)

* * *

### expired[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#expired "Direct link to expired")

• `Optional` **expired**: `boolean`

Boolean showing whether this symbol is expired futures contract or not.

**`Default`**

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-11 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[expired](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#expired)

* * *

### format[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#format "Direct link to format")

• **format**: [`SeriesFormat`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#seriesformat)

Format of displaying labels on the price scale:

`price` - formats decimal or fractional numbers based on `minmov`, `pricescale`, `minmove2`, `fractional` and `variableMinTick` values. See [Price format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format)
 for more details. `volume` - formats decimal numbers in thousands, millions, billions or trillions

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-12 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#format)

* * *

### fractional[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#fractional "Direct link to fractional")

• `Optional` **fractional**: `boolean`

For common prices this can be skipped.

Fractional prices are displayed 2 different forms: 1) `xx'yy` (for example, `133'21`) 2) `xx'yy'zz` (for example, `133'21'5`).

*   `xx` is an integer part.
*   `minmov/pricescale` is a Fraction.
*   `minmove2` is used in form 2.
*   `fractional` is `true`.
*   `variableMinTick` is skipped.

Example:

If `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128` and `minmove2 = 4`:

*   `119'16'0` represents `119 + 16/32`
*   `119'16'2` represents `119 + 16.25/32`
*   `119'16'5` represents `119 + 16.5/32`
*   `119'16'7` represents `119 + 16.75/32`

More examples:

*   `ZBM2014 (T-Bond)` with `1/32`: `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 32`, `minmove2 = 0`
*   `ZCM2014 (Corn)` with `2/8`: `minmov = 2`, `pricescale = 8`, `minmove2 = 0`
*   `ZFM2014 (5 year t-note)` with `1/4 of 1/32`: `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128`, `minmove2 = 4`

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-13 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[fractional](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#fractional)

* * *

### has\_daily[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#has_daily "Direct link to has_daily")

• `Optional` **has\_daily**: `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains daily data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a daily resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

`has_daily` is set to `true` by default. However, you should also specify [daily\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#daily_multipliers)
 to enable daily resolutions. Refer to the [Resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#resolution-in-days)
 article for more information.

**`Default`**

    true

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-14 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[has\_daily](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_daily)

* * *

### has\_empty\_bars[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#has_empty_bars "Direct link to has_empty_bars")

• `Optional` **has\_empty\_bars**: `boolean`

The boolean value showing whether the library should generate empty bars in the session when there is no data from the data feed for this particular time.

I.e., if your session is `0900-1600` and your data has gaps between `11:00` and `12:00` and your `has_empty_bars` is `true`, then the Library will fill the gaps with bars for this time.

Flag `has_empty_bars` = `true` cannot be used if featureset `disable_resolution_rebuild` is enabled.

**`Default`**

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-15 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[has\_empty\_bars](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_empty_bars)

* * *

### has\_intraday[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#has_intraday "Direct link to has_intraday")

• `Optional` **has\_intraday**: `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains intraday (minutes) data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when an intraday resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

This property is required to enable intraday resolutions. Refer to the [Resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#resolution-in-minutes-intraday)
 article for more information.

**`Default`**

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-16 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[has\_intraday](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_intraday)

* * *

### has\_seconds[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#has_seconds "Direct link to has_seconds")

• `Optional` **has\_seconds**: `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains seconds data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a seconds resolution is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

You should set `has_seconds` to `true` to enable seconds resolutions. Refer to the [Resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#resolution-in-seconds)
 article for more information.

**`Default`**

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-17 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[has\_seconds](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_seconds)

* * *

### has\_ticks[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#has_ticks "Direct link to has_ticks")

• `Optional` **has\_ticks**: `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains ticks data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when a resolution in ticks is selected. If `false`, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

You should set `has_ticks` to `true` to enable ticks resolutions. Refer to the [Resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#resolution-in-ticks)
 article for more information.

**`Default`**

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-18 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[has\_ticks](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_ticks)

* * *

### has\_weekly\_and\_monthly[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#has_weekly_and_monthly "Direct link to has_weekly_and_monthly")

• `Optional` **has\_weekly\_and\_monthly**: `boolean`

A flag indicating whether your datafeed contains weekly or monthly data for this symbol. If `true`, the library requests this data when the corresponding resolution is selected. To enable weekly or monthly resolutions, you should also specify the [weekly\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#weekly_multipliers)
 or [monthly\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#monthly_multipliers)
 properties. Refer to the [Resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#resolution-in-weeks--months)
 article for more information.

If `has_weekly_and_monthly` is set to `false`, the library attempts to build the resolutions using daily bars. Note that building bars requires a large number of requests to your datafeed. If the library fails to build bars, _No data here_ is displayed on the chart.

**`Default`**

    false

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-19 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[has\_weekly\_and\_monthly](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#has_weekly_and_monthly)

* * *

### industry[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#industry "Direct link to industry")

• `Optional` **industry**: `string`

Industry for stocks to be displayed in the Security Info.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-20 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[industry](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#industry)

* * *

### intraday\_multipliers[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#intraday_multipliers "Direct link to intraday_multipliers")

• `Optional` **intraday\_multipliers**: `string`\[\]

An array of intraday (minutes) resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order, for example: `["1", "2"]`.

This property is required to enable intraday resolutions. Refer to the [Resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#resolution-in-minutes-intraday)
 article for more information. Note that each resolution in `intraday_multipliers` should be handled in the [IDatafeedChartApi.getBars](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IDatafeedChartApi#getbars)
 implementation. Consider the [example](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#example)
.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `intraday_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `intraday_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

Note that the library **cannot** build daily, weekly, or monthly resolutions using intraday data.

**`Default`**

    [] — specifies that the datafeed can provide data for any requested resolution.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-21 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[intraday\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#intraday_multipliers)

* * *

### library\_custom\_fields[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#library_custom_fields "Direct link to library_custom_fields")

• `Optional` **library\_custom\_fields**: `Record`<`string`, `unknown`\>

Additional metadata to include with the symbol information. These parameters will not affect existing properties such as 'ticker' or 'name' and will not be processed by the library. However, they can be accessed later through the [IChartWidgetApi.symbolExt](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#symbolext)
 method, impacting existing properties such as 'ticker' or 'name'.

.[library\_custom\_fields](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#library_custom_fields)

### listed\_exchange[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#listed_exchange "Direct link to listed_exchange")

• **listed\_exchange**: `string`

short name of the exchange where this symbol is traded (real listed exchange). The name will be displayed in the chart legend for this symbol.

**`Example`**

    "NYSE"

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-23 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)

### logo\_urls[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#logo_urls "Direct link to logo_urls")

• `Optional` **logo\_urls**: \[`string`, `string`\] | \[`string`\]

URL of image/s to be displayed as the logo/s for the symbol. The `show_symbol_logos` featureset needs to be enabled for this to be visible in the UI.

*   If a single url is returned then that url will solely be used to display the symbol logo.
*   If two urls are provided then the images will be displayed as two partially overlapping circles with the first url appearing on top. This is typically used for FOREX where you would like to display two country flags are the symbol logo.

The image/s should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively.

Examples:

*   `https://yourserver.com/apple.svg`
*   `/images/myImage.png`
*   `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
*   `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-24 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[logo\_urls](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#logo_urls)

* * *

Symbol Long description

Optional long(er) description for the symbol.
