* * *

### minmov[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#minmov "Direct link to minmov")

• **minmov**: `number`

The number of units that make up one tick. For example, U.S. equities are quotes in decimals, and tick in decimals, and can go up +/- `.01`. Therefore, the tick increment is 1 and `minmov = 1`. But the e-mini S&P futures contract, though quoted in decimals, goes up in `.25` increments, so the tick increment is 25 and `minmov = 25`. Refer to [Price format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format)
 for more information.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-26 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[minmov](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#minmov)

* * *

### minmove2[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#minmove2 "Direct link to minmove2")

• `Optional` **minmove2**: `number`

This property is used to display prices in the [fraction of a fraction](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/connecting_data/Symbology#fraction-of-a-fraction-format)
 format. For example, the ZBM2023 tick size is 1/4 of a 32nd. To display this security, set `minmov = 1`, `pricescale = 128`, `minmove2 = 4`.

For common prices, `minmove2` can be skipped or set to `0`.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-27 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[minmove2](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#minmove2)

* * *

### monthly\_multipliers[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#monthly_multipliers "Direct link to monthly_multipliers")

• `Optional` **monthly\_multipliers**: `string`\[\]

An array of monthly resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "3", "6", "12"]`. This property is required to enable monthly resolutions. Refer to the [Resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#resolution-in-weeks--months)
 article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `monthly_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `monthly_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**

    ['1']

* * *

### name[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#name "Direct link to name")

• **name**: `string`

It is a symbol name within an exchange, such as `AAPL` or `9988` (Hong Kong). Note that it should not contain the exchange name. This symbol name is visible to users and can be repeated.

By default, `name` is used to resolve symbols in the [Datafeed API](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/connecting_data/datafeed-api/)
. If you use [LibrarySymbolInfo.ticker](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#ticker)
, the library will use the ticker for Datafeed API requests.

Optional ID of a price source for a symbol. Should match one of the price sources from the [price\_sources](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#price_sources)
 array.

Note that you should set the [`symbol_info_price_source`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/customization/Featuresets#symbol_info_price_source)
 featureset to `true` to display the symbol price source in the main series legend.

### price\_sources[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#price_sources "Direct link to price_sources")

• `Optional` **price\_sources**: [`SymbolInfoPriceSource`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolInfoPriceSource)
\[\]

Supported price sources for the symbol. Price sources appear in the series legend and indicate the origin of values represented by symbol bars. Example price sources: "Spot Price", "Ask", "Bid", etc. The price source information is valuable when viewing non-OHLC series types.

Note that you should set the [`symbol_info_price_source`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/customization/Featuresets#symbol_info_price_source)
 featureset to `true` to display the symbol price source in the main series legend.

**`Example`**

    [{ id: '1', name: 'Spot Price' }, { id: '321', name: 'Bid' }]

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-33 "Direct link to Inherited from")

.[price\_sources](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#price_sources)

* * *

### pricescale[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#pricescale "Direct link to pricescale")

• **pricescale**: `number`

A number of decimal places or fractions that the price has.

The `pricescale` value depends on the [price format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/connecting_data/Symbology#price-format)
. If you want to display the price in the decimal format, `pricescale` should be `10^n`, where `n` is the number of decimal places. **Examples:** `1`, `10`, `10000000`.

If you want to display the price in the fractional format, `pricescale` should be `2^n`. This value represents the number of fractions. **Examples:** `8`, `16`, `256`.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-34 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[pricescale](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#pricescale)

* * *

### seconds\_multipliers[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#seconds_multipliers "Direct link to seconds_multipliers")

• `Optional` **seconds\_multipliers**: `string`\[\]

An array of seconds resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "2"]`. This property is required to enable seconds resolutions. Refer to the [Resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#resolution-in-seconds)
 article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `seconds_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `seconds_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions. Consider the example. You need to enable one-second and five-second resolutions but your datafeed contains only one-second data. In this case, you should set `seconds_multipliers` to `["1"]`. The library will build the five-second resolution from one-second data.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-35 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[seconds\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#seconds_multipliers)

* * *

.

.[subsessions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#subsessions)

* * *

### supported\_resolutions[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#supported_resolutions "Direct link to supported_resolutions")

• `Optional` **supported\_resolutions**: [`ResolutionString`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring)
\[\]

An array of [resolutions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution)
 which should be enabled in the _Resolution_ drop-down menu for this symbol. Each item of the array is expected to be a string that has a specific [format](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#resolution-format)
.

If one changes the symbol and the new symbol does not support the selected resolution, an error message will be shown on the chart.

**Resolution availability logic (pseudocode):**

    resolutionAvailable  =    resolution.isIntraday        ? symbol.has_intraday && symbol.supported_resolutions(resolution)        : symbol.supported_resolutions(resolution);

If `supported_resolutions` is `[]` (empty array), all resolutions are disabled in the _Resolution_ drop-down menu.

If `supported_resolutions` is `undefined`, all resolutions that the chart support ([DatafeedConfiguration.supported\_resolutions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.DatafeedConfiguration#supported_resolutions)
) and custom resolutions are enabled.

Note that the list of available time frames depends on supported resolutions. Time frames that require resolutions that are unavailable for a particular symbol will be hidden. Refer to [Time frame toolbar](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/ui_elements/Time-Scale#time-frame-toolbar)
 for more information.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-42 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[supported\_resolutions](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#supported_resolutions)

* * *

### ticker[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#ticker "Direct link to ticker")

• `Optional` **ticker**: `string`

It is an unique identifier for a particular symbol in your [symbology](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/connecting_data/Symbology)
. If you specify this property, its value will be used for all data requests for this symbol. `ticker` will be treated the same as [LibrarySymbolInfo.name](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#name)
 if not specified explicitly.

You should avoid using colons (":") in ticker values unless you are following the TradingView format: "NYSE:IBM". Using colons may cause unexpected behaviour and display bugs.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-43 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[ticker](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#ticker)

* * *

### timezone[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#timezone "Direct link to timezone")

• **timezone**: [`Timezone`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#timezone)

The time zone of the **exchange** where the symbol is listed. The time zone value should be in the OlsonDB format. Refer to [Time zones](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/ui_elements/timezones)
 for a full list of supported time zones.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-44 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[timezone](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#timezone)

* * *

### type[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#type "Direct link to type")

• **type**: `string`

Type of the instrument. Possible values: [SymbolType](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#symboltype)

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-45 "Direct link to Inherited from")

.[type](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#type)

* * *

### unit\_conversion\_types[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#unit_conversion_types "Direct link to unit_conversion_types")

• `Optional` **unit\_conversion\_types**: `string`\[\]

Allowed unit conversion group names.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-46 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[unit\_conversion\_types](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#unit_conversion_types)

* * *

### unit\_id[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#unit_id "Direct link to unit_id")

• `Optional` **unit\_id**: `string`

A unique identifier of a unit in which the instrument is traded or some other identifier if unit conversion is enabled. It is displayed on the price axes.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-47 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[unit\_id](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#unit_id)

* * *

### variable\_tick\_size[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#variable_tick_size "Direct link to variable_tick_size")

• `Optional` **variable\_tick\_size**: `string`

Dynamic minimum price movement. It is used if the instrument's minimum price movement changes depending on the price range.

For example, '0.01 10 0.02 25 0.05', where the tick size is 0.01 for a price less than 10, the tick size is 0.02 for a price less than 25, the tick size is 0.05 for a price greater than or equal to 25.

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-48 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[variable\_tick\_size](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#variable_tick_size)

* * *

### visible\_plots\_set[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#visible_plots_set "Direct link to visible_plots_set")

• `Optional` **visible\_plots\_set**: [`VisiblePlotsSet`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#visibleplotsset)

Represents what values are supported by the symbol. Possible values:

*   `ohlcv` — the symbol supports open, high, low, close prices and has volume.
*   `ohlc` — the symbol supports open, high, low, close, prices but doesn't have volume.
*   `c` — the symbol supports only close price. This makes the chart show the symbol data using only line-based styles.

**`Default`**

    'ohlcv'

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-49 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[visible\_plots\_set](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#visible_plots_set)

* * *

### volume\_precision[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#volume_precision "Direct link to volume_precision")

• `Optional` **volume\_precision**: `number`

Integer showing typical volume value decimal places for a particular symbol. 0 means volume is always an integer. 1 means that there might be 1 numeric character after the comma.

**`Default`**

    '0'

#### Inherited from[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#inherited-from-50 "Direct link to Inherited from")

[LibrarySymbolInfo](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo)
.[volume\_precision](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#volume_precision)

* * *

### weekly\_multipliers[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.SymbolExt/#weekly_multipliers "Direct link to weekly_multipliers")

• `Optional` **weekly\_multipliers**: `string`\[\]

An array of weekly resolutions that your datafeed supports. Items in the array should be listed in ascending order and **should not** include letters, for example: `["1", "3"]`. This property is required to enable weekly resolutions. Refer to the [Resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/core_concepts/Resolution#resolution-in-weeks--months)
 article for more information.

The library also uses resolutions listed in `weekly_multipliers` to display higher resolution that your datafeed does not explicitly support. If `weekly_multipliers` is not specified, the library cannot build additional resolutions.

**`Default`**

    ['1']

.[weekly\_multipliers](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#weekly_multipliers)

# Interface: SymbolSpecificTradingOptions | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Broker](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Broker)
.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### allowedDurations[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#alloweddurations "Direct link to allowedDurations")

• `Optional` **allowedDurations**: `string`\[\]

Array of strings with valid duration values. You can check that in Order Ticket.

* * *

### allowedOrderTypes[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#allowedordertypes "Direct link to allowedOrderTypes")

• `Optional` **allowedOrderTypes**: [`OrderType`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/enums/Broker.OrderType)
\[\]

Supported order types for the instrument.

* * *

### supportAddBracketsToExistingOrder[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportaddbracketstoexistingorder "Direct link to supportAddBracketsToExistingOrder")

• `Optional` **supportAddBracketsToExistingOrder**: `boolean`

Using this flag you can disable adding brackets to the existing order.

* * *

### supportBracketsInPips[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportbracketsinpips "Direct link to supportBracketsInPips")

• `Optional` **supportBracketsInPips**: `boolean`

Whether brackets could be set in ticks/pips.

**`Default`**

    true

* * *

### supportIndividualPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportindividualpositionbrackets "Direct link to supportIndividualPositionBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportIndividualPositionBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether trade brackets are supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### supportModifyBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportmodifybrackets "Direct link to supportModifyBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportModifyBrackets**: `boolean`

Using this flag you can disable existing order's brackets modification. If you set it to `false`, additional fields will be disabled in Order Ticket on the chart,

* * *

### supportModifyOrderBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportmodifyorderbrackets "Direct link to supportModifyOrderBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportModifyOrderBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether the integration supports the modification of existing order brackets.

* * *

### supportModifyPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportmodifypositionbrackets "Direct link to supportModifyPositionBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportModifyPositionBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether the integration supports the modification of existing position brackets.

* * *

### supportOrderBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportorderbrackets "Direct link to supportOrderBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportOrderBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether order brackets are supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### supportPositionBrackets[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportpositionbrackets "Direct link to supportPositionBrackets")

• `Optional` **supportPositionBrackets**: `boolean`

Whether position brackets are supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### supportReversePosition[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#supportreverseposition "Direct link to supportReversePosition")

• `Optional` **supportReversePosition**: `boolean`

Whether position reversing is supported for the symbol. Defaults to the value in the config.

* * *

### warningMessage[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.SymbolSpecificTradingOptions/#warningmessage "Direct link to warningMessage")

• `Optional` **warningMessage**: `string`

A symbol-specific message that can be used to warn users.

.TRIXIndicatorOverrides

Overrides for the 'TRIX' indicator.

Use these properties to customize indicator via [IChartWidgetApi.createStudy](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IChartWidgetApi#createstudy)
 and [IStudyApi.applyOverrides](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.IStudyApi#applyoverrides)
.

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

**.

[Broker](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Broker)
.TableFormatterInputs

Type parameters[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#type-parameters "Direct link to Type parameters")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `T` | extends [`TableFormatterInputValues`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Broker#tableformatterinputvalues)<br> = [`TableFormatterInputValues`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Broker#tableformatterinputvalues) |

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

--------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### prevValues[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#prevvalues "Direct link to prevValues")

• `Optional` **prevValues**: `Partial`<`T` extends \[...args: A\[\]\] ? \[...A\[\]\] : `never`\>

Optional field. It is array of previous values so you can compare and format accordingly. It exists if current column has the `highlightDiff: true` key.

* * *

### values[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#values "Direct link to values")

• **values**: `T` extends \[...args: A\[\]\] ? \[...A\[\]\] : `never`

Array of values to be formatted. Values are obtained by extracting dependent properties from the data object.

*   [Type parameters](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Broker.TableFormatterInputs/#type-parameters)

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

| Name | Type |
| --- | --- |
| `T` | extends [`TableFormatterInputValues`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#tableformatterinputvalues)<br> = [`TableFormatterInputValues`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#tableformatterinputvalues) |

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### prevValues[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#prevvalues "Direct link to prevValues")

• `Optional` **prevValues**: `Partial`<`T` extends \[...args: A\[\]\] ? \[...A\[\]\] : `never`\>

Optional field. It is array of previous values so you can compare and format accordingly. It exists if current column has the `highlightDiff: true` key.

* * *

### values[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#values "Direct link to values")

• **values**: `T` extends \[...args: A\[\]\] ? \[...A\[\]\] : `never`

Array of values to be formatted. Values are obtained by extracting dependent properties from the data object.

*   [Type parameters](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#type-parameters)
    
*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#properties)
    *   [prevValues](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#prevvalues)
        
    *   [values](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TableFormatterInputs/#values)

# Interface: TimeFieldDescriptor | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFieldDescriptor/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.TimeFieldDescriptor

Used in the schema defined in exportData API to describe the time field. This is used when `includeTime: true` is defined in `exportData` to add time to exported data.

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFieldDescriptor/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### type[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFieldDescriptor/#type "Direct link to type")

• **type**: `"time"`

time field descriptor

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFieldDescriptor/#properties)
    *   [type](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFieldDescriptor/#type)

---

# Interface: TimeFrameItem | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.TimeFrameItem

Definition of visible timeframes that can be selected at the bottom of the chart

**`Example`**

    { text: "3y", resolution: "1W", description: "3 Years", title: "3yr" }

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### description[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#description "Direct link to description")

• `Optional` **description**: `string`

Optional text displayed in the pop-up menu. If not set, `title` wil be used instead.

* * *

### resolution[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#resolution "Direct link to resolution")

• **resolution**: [`ResolutionString`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring)

Resolution to be set

* * *

### text[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#text "Direct link to text")

• **text**: `string`

Defines the range to be set for the given resolution. It has the `<integer><y|m|d>` ( \\d+(y|m|d) as Regex ) format.

* * *

### title[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#title "Direct link to title")

• `Optional` **title**: `string`

Optional text representing the time frame. If not set a generated string will be set based on `text` property.

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#properties)
    *   [description](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#description)
        
    *   [resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#resolution)
        
    *   [text](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#text)
        
    *   [title](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#title)

---

# Interface: TimescaleMark | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.TimescaleMark

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### color[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#color "Direct link to color")

• **color**: `string`

Color for the timescale mark

* * *

### id[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#id "Direct link to id")

• **id**: `string` | `number`

ID of the timescale mark

* * *

### imageUrl[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#imageurl "Direct link to imageUrl")

• `Optional` **imageUrl**: `string`

Optional URL for an image to be displayed within the timescale mark.

The image should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively.

Examples:

*   `https://s3-symbol-logo.tradingview.com/crypto/XTVCBTC.svg`
*   `/images/myImage.png`
*   `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
*   `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`

* * *

### label[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#label "Direct link to label")

• **label**: `string`

Label for the timescale mark

* * *

### labelFontColor[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#labelfontcolor "Direct link to labelFontColor")

• `Optional` **labelFontColor**: `string`

Color for the timescale mark text label. If undefined then the value provided for `color` will be used.

* * *

### shape[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#shape "Direct link to shape")

• `Optional` **shape**: [`TimeScaleMarkShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#timescalemarkshape)

Shape of the timescale mark

* * *

### showLabelWhenImageLoaded[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#showlabelwhenimageloaded "Direct link to showLabelWhenImageLoaded")

• `Optional` **showLabelWhenImageLoaded**: `boolean`

Continue to show text label even when an image has been loaded for the timescale mark.

Defaults to `false` if undefined.

* * *

### time[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#time "Direct link to time")

• **time**: `number`

Time for the mark. Amount of **milliseconds** since Unix epoch start in **UTC** timezone.

* * *

### tooltip[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#tooltip "Direct link to tooltip")

• **tooltip**: `string`\[\]

Tooltip content

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#properties)
    *   [color](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#color)
        
    *   [id](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#id)

    *   [label](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#label)
        
    *   [labelFontColor](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#labelfontcolor)
        
    *   [shape](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimescaleMark/#shape)

# Interface: TimezoneInfo | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v26**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.TimezoneInfo

Supported timezone identifier are LibrarySymbolInfo.timezones and [LibrarySymbolInfo.exchange](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#exchange)
. `exchange` is a special "timezone" that means the timezone of a currently active symbol. Thus the actual timezone will be calculated every time a symbol changes.

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### alias[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#alias "Direct link to alias")

• `Optional` **alias**: [`TimezoneId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#timezoneid)
 | \`Etc/GMT-${number}\` | \`Etc/GMT-${number}:${number}\` | \`Etc/GMT+${number}\` | \`Etc/GMT+${number}:${number}\`

Id of supported default timezone to use for the actual timezone calculations, see [TimezoneId](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#timezoneid)

* * *

### id[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#id "Direct link to id")

• **id**: [`TimezoneId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#timezoneid)
 | [`CustomTimezoneId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#customtimezoneid)

Timezone identifier [TimezoneId](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/modules/Charting_Library#timezoneid)

* * *

### offset[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#offset "Direct link to offset")

• `Optional` **offset**: `number`

Optional number to indicate an offset

* * *

### title[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#title "Direct link to title")

• **title**: `string`

Name of the timezone

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#properties)
    *   [alias](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#alias)
        
    *   [id](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#id)
        
    *   [offset](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#offset)
        
    *   [title](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v26/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#title)

---

**.

# Interface: TimeFrameItem | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.TimeFrameItem

Definition of visible time frames that can be selected at the bottom of the chart

**`Example`**

    { text: "3y", resolution: "1W", description: "3 Years", title: "3yr" }

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### description[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#description "Direct link to description")

• `Optional` **description**: `string`

Optional text displayed in the pop-up menu. If not set, `title` wil be used instead.

* * *

### resolution[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#resolution "Direct link to resolution")

• **resolution**: [`ResolutionString`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#resolutionstring)

Resolution to be set

* * *

### text[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#text "Direct link to text")

• **text**: `string`

Defines the range to be set for the given resolution. It has the `<integer><y|m|d>` ( \\d+(y|m|d) as Regex ) format.

* * *

### title[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#title "Direct link to title")

• `Optional` **title**: `string`

Optional text representing the time frame. If not set a generated string will be set based on `text` property.

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#properties)
    *   [description](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#description)
        
    *   [resolution](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#resolution)
        
    *   [text](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#text)
        
    *   [title](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFrameItem/#title)

---

# Interface: TimeFramePeriodBack | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFramePeriodBack/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.TimeFramePeriodBack

Defines a time frame at a specific date

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFramePeriodBack/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

-----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### type[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFramePeriodBack/#type "Direct link to type")

• **type**: [`PeriodBack`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/enums/Charting_Library.TimeFrameType#periodback)

Time frame period is `period-back`

* * *

### value[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFramePeriodBack/#value "Direct link to value")

• **value**: `string`

Time frame string. For example `'1D'` or `'6M'`.

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFramePeriodBack/#properties)
    *   [type](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFramePeriodBack/#type)
        
    *   [value](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimeFramePeriodBack/#value)

---

# Interface: TimescaleMark | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.TimescaleMark/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Datafeed](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Datafeed)
.TimescaleMark

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.TimescaleMark/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

---------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### color[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.TimescaleMark/#color "Direct link to color")

• **color**: `string`

Color for the timescale mark

* * *

### id[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.TimescaleMark/#id "Direct link to id")

• **id**: `string` | `number`

ID of the timescale mark

* * *

### imageUrl[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.TimescaleMark/#imageurl "Direct link to imageUrl")

• `Optional` **imageUrl**: `string`

Optional URL for an image to be displayed within the timescale mark.

The image should ideally be square in dimension. You can use any image type which the browser supports natively.

Examples:

*   `https://s3-symbol-logo.tradingview.com/crypto/XTVCBTC.svg`
*   `/images/myImage.png`
*   `data:image/svg+xml;base64,PHN2ZyB4bWxucz0iaHR0cDovL3...`
*   `data:image/jpeg;base64,/9j/4AAQSkZJRgABAQAASABIAAD/4...`

* * *

### label[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.TimescaleMark/#label "Direct link to label")

• **label**: `string`

Label for the timescale mark

* * *

### labelFontColor[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.TimescaleMark/#labelfontcolor "Direct link to labelFontColor")

• `Optional` **labelFontColor**: `string`

Color for the timescale mark text label. If undefined then the value provided for `color` will be used.

* * *

### shape[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.TimescaleMark/#shape "Direct link to shape")

• `Optional` **shape**: [`TimeScaleMarkShape`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Datafeed#timescalemarkshape)

Shape of the timescale mark

* * *

### showLabelWhenImageLoaded[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.TimescaleMark/#showlabelwhenimageloaded "Direct link to showLabelWhenImageLoaded")

• `Optional` **showLabelWhenImageLoaded**: `boolean`

Continue to show text label even when an image has been loaded for the timescale mark.

Defaults to `false` if undefined.

* * *

### time[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Datafeed.TimescaleMark/#time "Direct link to time")

• **time**: `number`

Time for the mark. Unix timestamp in seconds.

* * *

# Interface: TimezoneInfo | Advanced Charts Documentation

[Skip to main content](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#__docusaurus_skipToContent_fallback)

This is documentation for **v29**, which is no longer actively maintained.

For up-to-date documentation, see the **[latest version](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/)
**.

On this page

[Charting Library](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library)
.TimezoneInfo

Supported timezone identifier are LibrarySymbolInfo.timezones and [LibrarySymbolInfo.exchange](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.LibrarySymbolInfo#exchange)
. `exchange` is a special "timezone" that means the timezone of a currently active symbol. Thus the actual timezone will be calculated every time a symbol changes.

Properties[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#properties "Direct link to Properties")

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### alias[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#alias "Direct link to alias")

• `Optional` **alias**: [`TimezoneId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#timezoneid)
 | \`Etc/GMT-${number}\` | \`Etc/GMT-${number}:${number}\` | \`Etc/GMT+${number}\` | \`Etc/GMT+${number}:${number}\`

Id of supported default timezone to use for the actual timezone calculations, see [TimezoneId](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#timezoneid)

* * *

### id[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#id "Direct link to id")

• **id**: [`TimezoneId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#timezoneid)
 | [`CustomTimezoneId`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#customtimezoneid)

Timezone identifier [TimezoneId](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Charting_Library#timezoneid)

* * *

### offset[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#offset "Direct link to offset")

• `Optional` **offset**: `number`

Optional number to indicate an offset

Accessors[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#accessors "Direct link to Accessors")

-------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

### title[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#title "Direct link to title")

• `get` **title**(): `string`

Name of the timezone

#### Returns[​](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#returns "Direct link to Returns")

`string`

*   [Properties](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#properties)
    *   [alias](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#alias)
        
    *   [id](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#id)
        
    *   [offset](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#offset)
        
*   [Accessors](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#accessors)
    *   [title](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/interfaces/Charting_Library.TimezoneInfo/#title)

• `Optional` **customFields**: [`TradingDialogCustomField`](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/api/modules/Broker#tradingdialogcustomfield)
\[\]

Custom fields that are displayed in the Order Ticket. Refer to the [Add custom fields](https://www.tradingview.com/charting-library-docs/v29/trading_terminal/order-ticket#add-custom-fields)
 section for more information.

**Example**

    customFields: [    {        inputType: 'TextWithCheckBox',        id: '2410',        title: 'Digital Signature',        placeHolder: 'Enter your personal digital signature',        value: {            text: '',            checked: false,        },        customInfo: {            asterix: true,            checkboxTitle: 'Save',        },    }]
